```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

*Demultiplexing of Miseq and Novoseq*
These are linux bash scripts:

```{r demultiplex echo = TRUE,eval = FALSE}
#primers.fasta and matrix.txt is here:
#/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/DIET_PRIMERS_MATRIX/primers_18S.fasta
#/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/DIET_PRIMERS_MATRIX/matrix_18S.txt

##############################################################################
###____________________________ Demultiplexing MiSeq1 _____________________###
##############################################################################
#!/bin/bash
#PBS -N skewer_demultiplex_mis_1
#PBS -l select=2:ncpus=32:mem=64gb:scratch_local=50gb
#PBS -l walltime=01:30:00
#PBS -m n

# [1] SKEWER - DEMULTIPLEXING
# Define the fasta file that contains primers
ADAPT_PATH=$(echo /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/DIET_PRIMERS_MATRIX/primers_18S.fasta)
# Define the matrix file that contains primer match matrix
MATRIX_PATH=$(echo /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/DIET_PRIMERS_MATRIX/matrix_18S.txt)

# Go to raw data directory
cd /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/set1/raw_fastq

# Define the samples (filter by the sequence run label) CHANGE THIS FOR MISEQ
SAMPLES=$(ls -1 | grep "_R[12].fastq.gz" | sed -r 's/_R[12]\.fastq\.gz//' | sort | uniq)

# Create directory for demultiplexed samples
mkdir -p ../demultiplexed.18S.1

# Demultiplexing the data
for i in $SAMPLES; do
    /storage/praha1/home/solakh/softwares/skewer/skewer-0.2.2-linux-x86_64 \
    -x $ADAPT_PATH -M $MATRIX_PATH -b -m head -k 35 -d 0 -t 8 \
    "$i"_R1.fastq.gz "$i"_R2.fastq.gz -o ../demultiplexed.18S.1/"$i"
done >> ./log

# Go to the demultiplexed folder
cd /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/set1/demultiplexed.18S.1

# Unzip the demultiplexed .gz files
gzip *.fastq

# Clean scratch after the end
trap 'clean_scratch' TERM EXIT
```

*Trimming of Miseq and Novoseq*
These are linux bash scripts:
```{r trimming}
##############################################################################
###____________________________ Trimming Miseq1 _____________________###
##############################################################################
#!/bin/bash
#PBS -N skewer_miseq1_18S_trim
#PBS -l select=4:ncpus=20:mem=32gb:scratch_local=500gb
#PBS -l walltime=05:00:00
#PBS -m n

cd /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/set1/demultiplexed.18S.1
  
mkdir ../dem_trim_18S1

#list of unique demutiplexed files

SAMPLES_16S=$(ls -1 *fastq.gz | grep -- "-assigned-"|sed -r 's/-pair[12].fastq.gz//'|sort| uniq)

#run trimming with skewer, the inline barcodes are denoted as Ns so anything can be there
#K is size of the primer length, means minumum overlap parameter k=20 for primer(adapter) with 20bps
for i in $SAMPLES_16S; do /storage/praha1/home/solakh/softwares/skewer/skewer-0.2.2-linux-x86_64 -x NNNNGATYTGTCTGGTTVATTCCG -y CATCACAGACCTGTTATYGC  -m head -k 35 -d 0 -t 8 "$i"-pair1.fastq.gz "$i"-pair2.fastq.gz -o ../dem_trim_18S1/"$i"; done >> ../dem_trim_18S1/log.trimmed.head

cd ../dem_trim_18S1
gzip *fastq


#clean scratch after the end
trap 'clean_scratch' TERM EXIT

##############################################################################
###____________________________ Trimming Miseq2 _____________________###

##############################################################################
###____________________________ Trimming Novoseq1 _____________________###
##############################################################################
#!/bin/bash
#PBS -N skewer_novo1_18S_trim
#PBS -l select=4:ncpus=20:mem=32gb:scratch_local=50gb
#PBS -l walltime=02:00:00
#PBS -m n


#[2]SKEWER TRIMMING
cd /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/

#make folder for demuplitpexed+trimmed data (those will be used in DADA)
mkdir dem_trim_18S1

#go to demutliplexed data from previous step which are input for trimming
cd demultiplexed.18S.1


#list of unique demutiplexed files (they have "assigned" in the name - i.e. that is how skewer label the demultiplexed files generated in previ
SAMPLES_16S=$(ls -1 *fastq.gz | grep "_MKDL240008182-1A_22C2KLLT4_L2-assigned"|sed -r 's/-pair[12].fastq.gz//'|sort| uniq)

#run trimming with skewer, the inline barcodes are denoted as Ns so anything can be there
#K is size of the primer length, means minumum overlap parameter k=20 for primer(adapter) with 20bps
for i in $SAMPLES_16S; do /storage/praha1/home/solakh/softwares/skewer/skewer-0.2.2-linux-x86_64 -x NNNNGATYTGTCTGGTTVATTCCG -y CATCACAGACCTGTTATYGC  -m head -k 35 -d 0 -t 8 "$i"-pair1.fastq.gz "$i"-pair2.fastq.gz -o ../dem_trim_18S1/"$i"; done >> ../dem_trim_18S1/log.trimmed.head

cd ../dem_trim_18S1
gzip *fastq


#clean scratch after the end
trap 'clean_scratch' TERM EXIT
```

*Prepare OTU table with dada2*
We will use filterAndTrim() function instead of fastqPairedFilter function. Bcuz the new function allows
to have different ASV sequence lengths instead of previous function. 
```{r dada2}

#Change the directories for each run 

library(dada2)
library(ggplot2)
setwd("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene/18S_novo1_dem_trim")

LIST<-list.files()
LIST
F_reads<-LIST[grep("pair1.fastq.gz",LIST)]
R_reads<-LIST[grep("pair2.fastq.gz",LIST)]

sample.names<-gsub("-trimmed-pair1.fastq.gz","",F_reads)
sample.names<-gsub("-assigned-","",sample.names)
tail(sample.names)

tail(sample.names)
#Names for filtered files
filtFs <- paste0(sample.names, "_READ1_filt.fastq.gz")
filtRs <- paste0(sample.names, "_READ2_filt.fastq.gz")
tail(filtRs)

#Q filtering
for(x in 1:length(F_reads)) {
  print(sample.names[x])
  fastqPairedFilter(c(F_reads[x], R_reads[x]), c(filtFs[x], filtRs[x]),
                    maxN=0, maxEE=2, minQ=2,truncQ=2,
                    compress=TRUE, verbose=TRUE,
                    minLen = c(85,85),truncLen = c(85,85))
}



# Create OTU seqtab
fns <- list.files()
fastqs <- fns[grepl(".fastq.gz$", fns)]
fastqs <- sort(fastqs)

fnFs <- fastqs[grepl("_READ1_filt.fastq.gz", fastqs)]
fnRs <- fastqs[grepl("_READ2_filt.fastq.gz", fastqs)]

sample.names <- gsub("_READ1_filt.fastq.gz","",fnFs)

#dereplicate sequences (get unique sequences from each file)
derepFs <- derepFastq(fnFs,n = 1e+05, verbose=T)
derepRs <- derepFastq(fnRs,n = 1e+05, verbose=T)

save(derepFs,file="/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene/seqtab_18S_novo1/derepFs_PNG_18S_novo1.R")
save(derepRs,file="/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene/seqtab_18S_novo1/derepRs_PNG_18S_novo1.R")

names(derepFs) <- sample.names
names(derepRs) <- sample.names

#denoising
dadaFs <- dada(derepFs,err=NULL, multithread = T,selfConsist = TRUE,MAX_CONSIST=25)
dadaRs <- dada(derepRs,err=NULL, multithread = T,selfConsist = TRUE,MAX_CONSIST=25)

save(dadaFs,file="/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene/seqtab_18S_novo1/dadaFs_18S_novo1.R")
save(dadaRs,file="/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene/seqtab_18S_novo1/dadaRs_18S_novo1.R")

#merging
mergers <- mergePairs(dadaFs, derepFs, dadaRs, derepRs, verbose=TRUE, minOverlap = 10,maxMismatch=1,justConcatenate=F)

#final table
seqtab <- makeSequenceTable(mergers)

#save results
save(seqtab,file="/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene/seqtab_18S_novo1/otutab_PNG_18S_novo1.R")


```

*CRABS*
```{r CRABS1}
#[1]Create initial reference database: 
#/storage/praha1/home/solakh/scripts/qsub/CRABS_get_ref.qsub

#[2] Get your ASV sequences
REFS<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/REF_PNG_18S_mis1.fasta")
names(REFS)<-paste0("ASV",1:length(REFS))
ShortRead::writeFasta(REFS,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/REF_PNG_18S_mis1_RNM.fasta")
REFS<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis2/REF_PNG_18S_mis2.fasta")
names(REFS)<-paste0("ASV",1:length(REFS))
ShortRead::writeFasta(REFS,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis2/REF_PNG_18S_mis2_RNM.fasta")
REFS<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo1/REF_PNG_18S_novo1.fasta")
names(REFS)<-paste0("ASV",1:length(REFS))
ShortRead::writeFasta(REFS,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo1/REF_PNG_18S_novo1_RNM.fasta")
REFS<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo2/REF_PNG_18S_novo2.fasta")
names(REFS)<-paste0("ASV",1:length(REFS))
ShortRead::writeFasta(REFS,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo2/REF_PNG_18S_novo2_RNM.fasta")
REFS<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/REF_PNG_18S_novo3.fasta")
names(REFS)<-paste0("ASV",1:length(REFS))
ShortRead::writeFasta(REFS,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/REF_PNG_18S_novo3_RNM.fasta")

#[3] Blast your ASV sequences to CRABS reference database and filter the low matching reference seqs:

# JK: This is probably the most important step. Many sequences in the final crabs database show poor alignment with the 18S ASVs. This means that they will play a negligible role in the taxonomic assignment. More importantly, these sequences are often not homologous to the 18S region sequences. I therefore created a blast database from ASVs and blasted all reference sequences against this database. Sequences from the reference databases that had a low similarity level (85% - the threshold can probably be even higher) were excluded. The result of this part is: 1) Hits.flanks200_200_BlastFIlt.fasta (i.e. sequences with flanking regions) and REF_qiime.fasta_BlastFIlt.fasta (i.e. sequences from the final database, this file should be used for taxonomic classifiacation of ASVs).

#### DO THIS FOR EACH OTU TABLE ####
############### ------------------------- Miseq 1 -------------------- ###############

#_______________________#UNIX PART#___________________________________________#

#qsub -I -l select=1:ncpus=12:mem=32gb -l walltime=04:00:00
module load ncbi-magicblast/

mkdir -p /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/CRABS  

#create reference database using your query ASV sequences

#_______________________#R PART#___________________________________________#
library(ShortRead)
#filtering reference fastas based on out
REF_blast<-read.delim("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/CRABS/outfile",header = F)
length(unique(REF_blast$V1))
#69169
head(REF_blast)

REF_blast.filt<-REF_blast[duplicated(REF_blast$V1)==F,]
# hist(REF_blast.filt$V3)
REF_blast.filt<-REF_blast.filt[REF_blast.filt$V3>85,]
dim(REF_blast.filt)
#[1]  62536    11

#remove items from original reference (with flanks) 
REF_flanks<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/CRABS_data/Hits.flanks200_200.fasta")
length(REF_flanks)
#80460
FILT<-names(REF_flanks)%in%REF_blast.filt$V1
REF_flanks.filt<-REF_flanks[FILT]
length(REF_flanks.filt)
#60298

#hist(width(REF_flanks.filt))
ShortRead::writeFasta(REF_flanks.filt,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/CRABS/Hits.flanks200_200_BlastFIlt.fasta")


#remove items from original reference (without flanks) 
REF<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/CRABS_data/REF_qiime.fasta.fasta")
length(REF)
#81784
FILT<-names(REF)%in%names(REF_flanks.filt)
REF.filt<-REF[FILT]
length(REF.filt)
#60298
ShortRead::writeFasta(REF.filt,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/CRABS/REF_qiime.fasta_BlastFIlt.fasta")


#############################
#Taxonomy table for Qiime####
#############################

REF.filt<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/CRABS/REF_qiime.fasta_BlastFIlt.fasta")
TAX.qiime<-read.delim("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/CRABS_data/REF_qiime.fasta.txt", header=F)

TAX.qiime.filt<-TAX.qiime[TAX.qiime$V1%in%names(REF.filt),]

sum(names(REF.filt)!=TAX.qiime.filt$V1)

write.table(TAX.qiime.filt,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/CRABS/REF_qiime.fasta_BlastFIlt.txt",
            row.names = F,col.names = F, quote = F, sep="\t")


#########################################
#Reference fasta for dada2###############
#########################################
length(REF.filt)
# [1] 60298
length(TAX.qiime.filt$V2)
# [1] 60298


REF.filt.dada<-REF.filt
TAX.dada.filt<-TAX.qiime.filt$V2
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("k__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("p__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("c__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("o__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("f__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("g__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("s__","",TAX.dada.filt)

names(REF.filt.dada)<-TAX.dada.filt

REF.filt.dada <- REF.filt.dada[!is.na(names(REF.filt.dada))]

writeFasta(REF.filt.dada,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/PNG_18S_Miseq/seqtab_18S_Mis1/CRABS/REF_dadaB_BlastFIlt.fasta")

blastn -db /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/ASVS_18S_nt -query /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/CRABS_data/REF_qiime.fasta.fasta -max_target_seqs 10 -num_threads 8 -out /storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/outfile -outfmt "6 qseqid sseqid pident length mismatch gapope qstart qend sstart send evalue bitscore"



#_______________________#R PART#___________________________________________#
library(ShortRead)
#filtering reference fastas based on out
REF_blast<-read.delim("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/outfile",header = F)
length(unique(REF_blast$V1))
#66037
head(REF_blast)

REF_blast.filt<-REF_blast[duplicated(REF_blast$V1)==F,]
# hist(REF_blast.filt$V3)
REF_blast.filt<-REF_blast.filt[REF_blast.filt$V3>85,]
dim(REF_blast.filt)
#[1]  58413    11

#remove items from original reference (with flanks) 
REF_flanks<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/CRABS_data/Hits.flanks200_200.fasta")
length(REF_flanks)
#80460
FILT<-names(REF_flanks)%in%REF_blast.filt$V1
REF_flanks.filt<-REF_flanks[FILT]
length(REF_flanks.filt)
#56275

#hist(width(REF_flanks.filt))
ShortRead::writeFasta(REF_flanks.filt,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/Hits.flanks200_200_BlastFIlt.fasta")


#remove items from original reference (without flanks) 
REF<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/CRABS_data/REF_qiime.fasta.fasta")
length(REF)
#81784
FILT<-names(REF)%in%names(REF_flanks.filt)
REF.filt<-REF[FILT]
length(REF.filt)
#56275
ShortRead::writeFasta(REF.filt,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/REF_qiime.fasta_BlastFIlt.fasta")


#############################
#Taxonomy table for Qiime####
#############################

REF.filt<-readDNAStringSet("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/REF_qiime.fasta_BlastFIlt.fasta")
TAX.qiime<-read.delim("/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/CRABS_data/REF_qiime.fasta.txt", header=F)

TAX.qiime.filt<-TAX.qiime[TAX.qiime$V1%in%names(REF.filt),]

sum(names(REF.filt)!=TAX.qiime.filt$V1)

write.table(TAX.qiime.filt,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/REF_qiime.fasta_BlastFIlt.txt",
            row.names = F,col.names = F, quote = F, sep="\t")


#########################################
#Reference fasta for dada2###############
#########################################
length(REF.filt)
# [1] 56275
length(TAX.qiime.filt$V2)
# [1] 56275


REF.filt.dada<-REF.filt
TAX.dada.filt<-TAX.qiime.filt$V2
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("k__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("p__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("c__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("o__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("f__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("g__","",TAX.dada.filt)
TAX.dada.filt<-gsub("s__","",TAX.dada.filt)

names(REF.filt.dada)<-TAX.dada.filt

REF.filt.dada <- REF.filt.dada[!is.na(names(REF.filt.dada))]

writeFasta(REF.filt.dada,file = "/storage/praha1/home/solakh/data/papuang/novogene2/seqtab_18S_novo3/CRABS/REF_dadaB_BlastFIlt.fasta")
```

*Create Phyloseqs*
```{r phyloseqs1}
rm(list = ls()) #clear the environment
library(ShortRead)
library(phyloseq)

#########################################################################################################
############### ------------------------- Miseq 1 -------------------- ###############
#########################################################################################################

load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/seqtab_18S_Mis1/otutab_PNG_18S_mis1.R") 
seqtab1=seqtab
dim(seqtab1) #1057 18328
sum(seqtab1) #28051966
seqtab1<-otu_table(seqtab1,taxa_are_rows = F) # Build OTU Table

load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/seqtab_18S_Mis1/TAX_PNG_18S_mis1.R")
taxa=tax_table(taxa)

HAPLO1=readDNAStringSet("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/seqtab_18S_Mis1/REF_PNG_18S_mis1.fasta")

PNG_18S_mis1=merge_phyloseq(seqtab1, taxa, HAPLO1)

save(PNG_18S_mis1, file = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis1.R")

#### merge metadata 1
rm(list = ls()) 
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis1.R")
SNAMES=sample_names(PNG_18S_mis1) #TGCAGCTA_GAGCCTTA_F_NT9R_NT-trimmed-pair1.fastq.gz
head(SNAMES)
#"AAGAGGCA_AAGGAGTANT5NT" "AAGAGGCA_AAGGAGTANT8NT" "AAGAGGCA_AAGGAGTANT9NT" "AAGAGGCA_AAGGCTATNT5NT"

MET<-read.delim("D:/PapuaNG_RAW/Diet_PNG_MW_set1/DIET_PNG_MW_set1_HMS.txt",
                header = T,
                stringsAsFactors = F)

#get reverse complement of Adaptor_F
#MET$Adaptor_F[1] AGCTAGAA
#                 AGCTAGAA

MET$Adaptor_F=spgs::reverseComplement(MET$Adaptor_F)
MET$Adaptor_F=toupper(MET$Adaptor_F)

SN<-paste(MET$Adaptor_R,"_" ,MET$Adaptor_F,MET$primerF,MET$primer_R, sep="") #combine F and R Primers and inline barcodes, these are sample ID

head(sort(SN)) 
head(sort(sample_names(PNG_18S_mis1)))

length(SN) #768
length(SNAMES) #1057

which(SN%in%sample_names(PNG_18S_mis1)==F)# All TRUE


SSUM<-sample_sums(PNG_18S_mis1) #Returns the total number of sequences observed from each sample
SNAMES<-sample_names(PNG_18S_mis1) #Get sample names

NOT_IN_META<-data.frame(SSUM,SNAMES) #Combined data frame of sample names and total number of reads
NOT_IN_META<-NOT_IN_META[!sample_names(PNG_18S_mis1)%in%SN,] #JUST ONE
#GCTACGCT_CTAAGCCT_F_NT9R_NT-trimmed-pair1.fastq.gz

MET<-sample_data(MET)
sample_names(MET)<-SN

sample_names(MET)%in%sample_names(PNG_18S_mis1)

PNG_18S_mis1= merge_phyloseq(PNG_18S_mis1,MET) 

NOT_IN_PHYLOSEQ<-MET[!sample_names(MET)%in%sample_names(PNG_18S_mis1)] #ALL OK

save(PNG_18S_mis1, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis1_MET.R")
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 18328 taxa and 768 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 768 samples by 32 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 18328 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 18328 reference sequences ]
```

*Merge Phyloseqs and host metadata*
```{r phyloseqs2}
rm(list = ls()) #clear the environment

#Miseqs
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis1_MET.R")
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis2.R")

#novoseqs
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo1.R")
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo2.R")
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo3.R")


#merge phyloseqs
PNG_18S_Miseq12=merge_phyloseq(PNG_18S_mis1,PNG_18S_mis2)
PNG_18S_Novoseq123=merge_phyloseq(PNG_18S_novo1,PNG_18S_novo2,PNG_18S_novo3)

PNG_18S_Miseq12
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 26992 taxa and 1206 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1206 samples by 32 sample variables ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 26992 reference sequences ]

PNG_18S_Novoseq123
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 28288 taxa and 759 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 759 samples by 43 sample variables ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 28288 reference sequences ]

################## HOST METADATA #############################
MET11=as.data.frame(sample_data(PNG_18S_Miseq12))
class(MET11) #phyloseq
```

*Compare duplicates*
```{r dups}
rm(list = ls()) #clear the environment
#compare and merge duplicates
#merge two phyloseqs
#change sample names to real sample names
#remove singletons
#filter taxonomy

####################### MISEQ ###############################
library(vegan)
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT.R")

#remove samples with less than 500 reads
PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT<-prune_samples(sample_sums(PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT)>500,PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT)

# duplicates
PNG_18S_novo_merged_A<-prune_samples(regexpr("_A",sample_data(PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT)$ID)>0,PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT) #596 
PNG_18S_novo_merged_B<-prune_samples(regexpr("_B",sample_data(PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT)$ID)>0,PNG_18S_Miseq12_HT) #599 

#intersect
INTER<-intersect(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D,sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D)
PNG_18S_novo_merged_A<-prune_samples(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D%in%INTER,PNG_18S_novo_merged_A) #592 
PNG_18S_novo_merged_B<-prune_samples(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D%in%INTER,PNG_18S_novo_merged_B) #593 

# Any duplicate IDs?
sum(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D))  # 3
sum(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D))  # 4

which(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D))
#[1] 419 420 421 422
which(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D))
#[1] 419 420 421

#remove 422 for now 
PNG_18S_novo_merged_B <- prune_samples(sample_names(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B) != sample_names(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)[422], PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)


#normalise
PNG_18S_novo_merged_A<-transform_sample_counts(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A, function(x) x/sum(x))
PNG_18S_novo_merged_B<-transform_sample_counts(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B, function(x) x/sum(x))

#protest
DA<-as.matrix(vegdist(otu_table(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A))^0.5)
DB<-as.matrix(vegdist(otu_table(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B))^0.5)

colnames(DA)<-rownames(DA)<-sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D
colnames(DB)<-rownames(DB)<-sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D

DB<-DB[rownames(DA),colnames(DA)]
# colnames(DB)==colnames(DA)
# rownames(DB)==rownames(DA)

DA.pc<-ape::pcoa(DA)
DB.pc<-ape::pcoa(DB)

PROTEST<-protest(DA.pc$vectors,DB.pc$vectors)
PROTEST

# Call:
# protest(X = DA.pc$vectors, Y = DB.pc$vectors) 
# 
# Procrustes Sum of Squares (m12 squared):        0.4443 
# Correlation in a symmetric Procrustes rotation: 0.7455 
# Significance:  0.001 
# 
# Permutation: free
# Number of permutations: 999

plot(PROTEST)



####################### NOVOSEQ ###############################
library(vegan)
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT.R")

#remove samples with less than 500 reads
PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT<-prune_samples(sample_sums(PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT)>500,PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT)

# duplicates
PNG_18S_novo_merged_A<-prune_samples(regexpr("_A",sample_data(PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT)$ID)>0,PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT) #371   
PNG_18S_novo_merged_B<-prune_samples(regexpr("_B",sample_data(PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT)$ID)>0,PNG_18S_Novoseq123_HT) #365   

#intersect
INTER<-intersect(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D,sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D)
PNG_18S_novo_merged_A<-prune_samples(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D%in%INTER,PNG_18S_novo_merged_A) #357  
PNG_18S_novo_merged_B<-prune_samples(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D%in%INTER,PNG_18S_novo_merged_B) #593 


#normalise
PNG_18S_novo_merged_A<-transform_sample_counts(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A, function(x) x/sum(x))
PNG_18S_novo_merged_B<-transform_sample_counts(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B, function(x) x/sum(x))

#protest
DA<-as.matrix(vegdist(otu_table(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A))^0.5)
DB<-as.matrix(vegdist(otu_table(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B))^0.5)

colnames(DA)<-rownames(DA)<-sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_A)$ID_D
colnames(DB)<-rownames(DB)<-sample_data(PNG_18S_novo_merged_B)$ID_D

DB<-DB[rownames(DA),colnames(DA)]
# colnames(DB)==colnames(DA)
# rownames(DB)==rownames(DA)

DA.pc<-ape::pcoa(DA)
DB.pc<-ape::pcoa(DB)

PROTEST<-protest(DA.pc$vectors,DB.pc$vectors)
PROTEST

# Call:
# protest(X = DA.pc$vectors, Y = DB.pc$vectors) 
# 
# Procrustes Sum of Squares (m12 squared):        0.4258 
# Correlation in a symmetric Procrustes rotation: 0.7578 
# Significance:  0.001 
# 
# Permutation: free
# Number of permutations: 999

plot(PROTEST)
```

*Merge Duplicates *
```{r mergedup, warning=F, message=F}
rm(list = ls())

#https://github.com/benjjneb/dada2/issues/745
# Custom functions by Jakub Kreisinger

## exclude nonduplicated samples
dupl.concensus<-function(PHYLOSEQ,NAMES){
  
  # exclude nonduplicated samples
  IDS<-as.character(data.frame(sample_data(PHYLOSEQ))[,NAMES])
  IDS.dupl<-IDS[duplicated(IDS)]
  
  PHYLOSEQ<-prune_samples(IDS%in%IDS.dupl, PHYLOSEQ)
  if(length(IDS.dupl)*2<length(IDS)) {NONUPLICATED<-prune_samples(!IDS%in%IDS.dupl, PHYLOSEQ)
                                       print(paste("Following names are nonduplicated",sample_names(NONUPLICATED)))}
  
  CATS<-as.character(data.frame(sample_data(PHYLOSEQ))[,NAMES])
  CATS2<-levels(factor(CATS))
  OTU_TAB<-otu_table(PHYLOSEQ)
  rownames(OTU_TAB)<-CATS
  
  # i<-5
  for (i in 1:length(CATS2))
  {
    # print(CATS2[i])
    FILTER.act<-colSums(OTU_TAB[rownames(OTU_TAB)==CATS2[i],]>0)>1 #this filter deletes all
    OTU_TAB[rownames(OTU_TAB)==CATS2[i],]
    OTU_TAB[rownames(OTU_TAB)==CATS2[i],]<-t(apply(OTU_TAB[rownames(OTU_TAB)==CATS2[i],],1,function(x) x*FILTER.act))
  }
  
  rownames(OTU_TAB)<-sample_names(PHYLOSEQ)
  otu_table(PHYLOSEQ)<-OTU_TAB
  PHYLOSEQ.clean<-prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYLOSEQ)>0,PHYLOSEQ) #here I get the error, taxa_sums=0
  
  PHYLOSEQ.clean
}



merge.duplicates<-function(PHYLOSEQ,NAMES){
  CATS<-as.character(data.frame(sample_data(PHYLOSEQ))[,NAMES])
  sample_data(PHYLOSEQ)$duplic.id<-CATS
  SAMDAT<-sample_data(PHYLOSEQ)
  SAMDAT.sub<-SAMDAT[duplicated(CATS)==F,]
  FASTA<-refseq(PHYLOSEQ)
  rownames(SAMDAT.sub)<-SAMDAT.sub$duplic.id
  PHYLOSEQ.merge<-merge_samples(PHYLOSEQ,"duplic.id")
  sample_data(PHYLOSEQ.merge)<-SAMDAT.sub
  PHYLOSEQ.merge<-merge_phyloseq(PHYLOSEQ.merge,FASTA)
  PHYLOSEQ.merge
}

#before run this part make sure that there is no sample with only one duplicate, exclude the ones with no duplicate.


#Miseqs
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis1_MET.R") #18328 taxa and 768 samples
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis2.R") #13768 taxa and 438 samples

#novoseqs
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo1.R") #18907 taxa and 335 samples
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo2.R") #17306 taxa and 335 samples
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo3.R") #10578 taxa and 89 samples


length(which(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_mis1)$sample_ID ==T))) #767
length(which(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_mis2)$sample_ID ==T))) #437
length(which(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo1)$sample_ID ==T))) #334
length(which(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo2)$sample_ID ==T))) #334
length(which(duplicated(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo3)$sample_ID ==T))) #88


##############################  _____ Miseq1 _____####################################

SUMMARY<-summary(as.factor(sample_data(PNG_18S_mis1)$sample_ID), maxsum = 10000)
rev(sort(SUMMARY)) 

SUMMARY.n1<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==1] # NO ANY
SUMMARY.n2<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==2] # Take only the samples with duplicates
SUMMARY.n3<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==3] # NO ANY
SUMMARY.n4<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==4] # NO ANY


PNG_18S_mis1.dupl<-prune_samples(sample_data(PNG_18S_mis1)$sample_ID%in%SUMMARY.n2==T, PNG_18S_mis1) # 768 samples
NAMES=sample_data(PNG_18S_mis1.dupl)$sample_ID #get sample ID of first dataset

CONSIST<-dupl.concensus(PHYLOSEQ=PNG_18S_mis1.dupl,NAMES="sample_ID") # take the technical duplicates for each sample and remove any ASVs that are not present in both of them.
CONSIST.merged<-merge.duplicates(PHYLOSEQ=CONSIST,NAMES="sample_ID") # Merge duplicates and save it.

PNG_18S_mis1.dupl # 18328 taxa and 768 samples
sum(otu_table(PNG_18S_mis1.dupl)) # 28.051.744

CONSIST.merged # 5253 taxa and 384 samples
sum(otu_table(CONSIST.merged)) # 26.401.695

PNG_18S_mis1.dupl=CONSIST.merged
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 5253 taxa and 384 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 384 samples by 33 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 5253 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 5253 reference sequences ]
save(PNG_18S_mis1.dupl,file = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis1.dupl.R") 


##############################  _____ Miseq2 _____####################################

SUMMARY<-summary(as.factor(sample_data(PNG_18S_mis2)$sample_ID), maxsum = 10000)
rev(sort(SUMMARY)) 

SUMMARY.n1<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==1] # NO ANY
SUMMARY.n2<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==2] # Take only the samples with duplicates
SUMMARY.n3<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==3] # NO ANY

NAMES=sample_data(PNG_18S_mis2.dupl)$sample_ID #get sample ID of first dataset

CONSIST<-dupl.concensus(PHYLOSEQ=PNG_18S_mis2.dupl,NAMES="sample_ID") # take the technical duplicates for each sample and remove any ASVs that are not present in both of them.

##############################  _____ Novoseq123 _____####################################

PNG_18S_novo123=merge_phyloseq(PNG_18S_novo1,PNG_18S_novo2,PNG_18S_novo3)
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 28288 taxa and 759 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 759 samples by 43 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 28288 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 28288 reference sequences ]

#remove ID column: FR1200.005_CC_EtOH_C, FR1200.005_CC_EtOH_D, FR1200.039_CC_EtOH_C, FR1200.040_CC_EtOH_C, PNGlost9_CC_EtOH_C

REMOVE=c("FR1200.005_CC_EtOH_C", "FR1200.005_CC_EtOH_D", "FR1200.039_CC_EtOH_C", "FR1200.040_CC_EtOH_C", "PNGlost9_CC_EtOH_C")

SUMMARY.n1<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==1] # "NK_PCR_11" "NK_PCR_12" "NK_PCR_8"  "NK_PCR_9" 
SUMMARY.n2<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==2] # 375
SUMMARY.n3<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==3] # REMOVED
SUMMARY.n4<-names(SUMMARY)[SUMMARY==4] # REMOVED


PNG_18S_novo123.dupl<-prune_samples(sample_data(PNG_18S_novo123)$sample_ID%in%SUMMARY.n2==T, PNG_18S_novo123) # 768 samples
NAMES=sample_data(PNG_18S_novo123.dupl)$sample_ID #get sample ID of first dataset

CONSIST<-dupl.concensus(PHYLOSEQ=PNG_18S_novo123.dupl,NAMES="sample_ID") # take the technical duplicates for each sample and remove any ASVs that are not present in both of them.
CONSIST.merged<-merge.duplicates(PHYLOSEQ=CONSIST,NAMES="sample_ID") # Merge duplicates and save it.

PNG_18S_novo123.dupl # 28288 taxa and 750 samples
sum(otu_table(PNG_18S_novo123.dupl)) # 56.614.563

CONSIST.merged # 13708 taxa and 375 samples
sum(otu_table(CONSIST.merged)) # 51.926.823

PNG_18S_novo123.dupl=CONSIST.merged
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 13708 taxa and 375 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 375 samples by 44 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 13708 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 13708 reference sequences ]
save(PNG_18S_novo123.dupl,file = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo123.dupl.R") 


###############
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis1.dupl.R")
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis2.dupl.R")
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_novo123.dupl.R")

ps=merge_phyloseq(PNG_18S_mis1.dupl, PNG_18S_mis1.dupl, PNG_18S_novo123.dupl)
#########individual raw abundance
ott=as.data.frame(otu_table(ps))
ott=t(ott)
taxx=as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
ind_tax_abun <- merge(taxx, ott, by="row.names", all.x = TRUE)
write.csv(ind_tax_abun, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/tables/summary tables/ind_raw_abund_notfiltered.csv")
```

```{r hostmeta, warning=F, message=F}
rm(list = ls())

load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_mis12_novo123.dupl.HT.R")


#____________________________________________________________##
library(BiocGenerics)
summary(width(refseq(PNG_18S_mis12_novo123.dupl.HT)))
   # Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
   # 51.0   100.0   101.0   104.5   102.0   230.0 



hist(width(refseq(PNG_18S_mis12_novo123.dupl.HT)))


PHYSEQ=PNG_18S_mis12_novo123.dupl.HT



library(ShortRead)
library(vegan)
PHYSEQ.t<-transform_sample_counts(PHYSEQ,function(x) x/sum(x))
BC<-vegdist(otu_table(PHYSEQ.t)^0.5)
ord<-ordinate(PHYSEQ.t,distance = BC,method = "PCoA")
plot_ordination(PHYSEQ.t,ord, color = "seqrun")
plot_ordination(PHYSEQ.t,ord, color = "Metalokalita")
p=plot_ordination(PHYSEQ.t,ord, color = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others")

library(plotly)
interactive_plot <- ggplotly(p)
interactive_plot



PHYSEQ.mis<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$seqrun=="mis",PHYSEQ)
PHYSEQ.novo<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$seqrun=="novo",PHYSEQ)

PHYSEQ.mis<-prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.mis)>0,PHYSEQ.mis)
PHYSEQ.novo<-prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.novo)>0,PHYSEQ.novo)
ALL_TAXA<-c(taxa_names(PHYSEQ.mis),taxa_names(PHYSEQ.novo)) #21338
DUPL<-ALL_TAXA[duplicated(ALL_TAXA)]
length(DUPL) #2891
summary(sample_sums(PHYSEQ.mis))
   # Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
   #  736   55891   79466   88167  107964  327533 
summary(sample_sums(PHYSEQ.novo))
   # Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
   # 1624   77472  126395  141873  180483  640032 
PHYSEQ.mis.common<-prune_taxa(taxa_names(PHYSEQ.mis)%in%DUPL,PHYSEQ.mis)
PHYSEQ.mis.unq<-prune_taxa(!taxa_names(PHYSEQ.mis)%in%DUPL,PHYSEQ.mis)
PHYSEQ.novo.unq<-prune_taxa(!taxa_names(PHYSEQ.novo)%in%DUPL,PHYSEQ.novo)

PHYSEQ.mis.common<-prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.mis.common)>0,PHYSEQ.mis.common) #2891  taxa
PHYSEQ.mis.unq<-prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.mis.unq)>0,PHYSEQ.mis.unq) #4739   taxa
PHYSEQ.novo.unq<-prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.novo.unq)>0,PHYSEQ.novo.unq) #10817   taxa

sum(otu_table(PHYSEQ.mis.unq))/sum(otu_table(PHYSEQ.mis)) #uniq taxa - 10% of miseq run
sum(otu_table(PHYSEQ.novo.unq))/sum(otu_table(PHYSEQ.novo)) #uniq taxa - 31% of novo run

DOMINANT.common<-rev(sort(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.mis.common)))[1:10]
DOMINANT.mis<-rev(sort(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.mis.unq)))[1:10]
DOMINANT.novo<-rev(sort(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.novo.unq)))[1:10]

# subset of 10 dominant ASVs make <50% for each phyloseq
sum(DOMINANT.common)/sum(otu_table(PHYSEQ.mis.common)) #32%
sum(DOMINANT.mis)/sum(otu_table(PHYSEQ.mis.unq)) #38%
sum(DOMINANT.novo)/sum(otu_table(PHYSEQ.novo.unq)) #41%


FASTA.common<-DNAStringSet(names(DOMINANT.common))
FASTA.mis<-DNAStringSet(names(DOMINANT.mis))
FASTA.novo<-DNAStringSet(names(DOMINANT.novo))

names(FASTA.common)<-paste0("COM_",1:length(FASTA.common))
names(FASTA.mis)<-paste0("mis_",1:length(FASTA.mis))
names(FASTA.novo)<-paste0("novo_",1:length(FASTA.novo))

library(DECIPHER)

ALGN.common<-AlignSeqs(RNAStringSet(FASTA.common))
ALGN.mis<-AlignSeqs(RNAStringSet(FASTA.mis))
ALGN.novo<-AlignSeqs(RNAStringSet(FASTA.novo))

ALGN.all<-AlignSeqs(RNAStringSet(c(FASTA.common,FASTA.mis,FASTA.novo)))

writeFasta(DNAStringSet(ALGN.common),file = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/fastas/ALGN.common.fasta")
writeFasta(DNAStringSet(ALGN.mis),file = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/fastas/ALGN.mis.fasta")
writeFasta(DNAStringSet(ALGN.novo),file = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/fastas/ALGN.novo.fasta")
writeFasta(DNAStringSet(ALGN.all),file = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/fastas/ALGN.all.fasta")

TREE<-TreeLine(DNAStringSet(ALGN.all))
plot(TREE)
```

*Filter taxonomy *
```{r filtertax, warning=F, message=F}
rm(list = ls())
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent.dupl.R")
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 16655 taxa and 827 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 827 samples by 143 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 16655 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 16655 reference sequences ]


# Start with the input phyloseq object
PHYSEQ <- PNG_18S_rodent.dupl #16655 taxa and 827 samples

# Remove ASVs with missing phylum
PHYSEQ <- subset_taxa(PHYSEQ, !is.na(Phylum) & Phylum != "") #11427 taxa and 827 samples

# ------------------------------
# Phylum-level filtering
# ------------------------------
phyla_to_remove <- c(
  "Acanthocephala", "Apicomplexa", "Bacillariophyta", "Blastocladiomycota",
  "Cercozoa", "Ciliophora", "Chytridiomycota", "Endomyxa", "Evosea",
  "Mucoromycota", "Nematoda", "Oomycota", "Parabasalia", "Preaxostyla", 
  "Tubulinea", "Zoopagomycota", "Olpidiomycota", "Sanchytriomycota",
  "Platyhelminthes", "Cnidaria", "Ctenophora", "Discosea", "Echinodermata", 
  "Gastrotricha", "Nematomorpha", "Nemertea", "Porifera", "Prasinodermophyta",
  "Rhodophyta", "Rotifera", "Tardigrada", "Xenacoelomorpha","Euglenozoa"
)

PHYSEQ <- subset_taxa(PHYSEQ, !(Phylum %in% phyla_to_remove)) #6825  taxa and 827 samples

# ------------------------------
# Refine Chordata filtering
# Remove:
# - Class: NA
# - Class: Ascidiacea
# - Order: NA
# - Family: Hominidae
# Keep only Amphibia or Lepidosauria if in Chordata
# ------------------------------

PHYSEQ <- subset_taxa(
  PHYSEQ,
  !(Phylum == "Chordata" & (
      is.na(Class) |
      Class == "Ascidiacea" |
      is.na(Order) |
      Family == "Hominidae" |
      !(Class %in% c("Amphibia", "Lepidosauria"))
  ))
) #6233  taxa and 827 samples
# ------------------------------
# Class-level filtering
# ------------------------------
exclude_class <- c(
  "Atractiellomycetes", "Cystobasidiomycetes", "Exobasidiomycetes", "Microbotryomycetes", 
  "Moniliellomycetes", "Pucciniomycetes", "Tritirachiomycetes", "Ustilaginomycetes", 
  "Wallemiomycetes", "Saccharomycetes", "Eurotiomycetes", "Trematoda", 
  "Agaricostilbomycetes", "Chlorophyceae", "Dipodascomycetes", "Dothideomycetes",
  "Hexanauplia", "Klebsormidiophyceae", "Malasseziomycetes", "Pichiomycetes", 
  "Schizosaccharomycetes", "Sordariomycetes", "Spiculogloeomycetes", 
  "Taphrinomycetes", "Thecostraca", "Trebouxiophyceae", "Tremellomycetes", 
  "Ulvophyceae"
)

PHYSEQ <- prune_taxa(!(tax_table(PHYSEQ)[,"Class"] %in% exclude_class), PHYSEQ)
#4866 taxa and 827 samples
# ------------------------------
# Order-level filtering
# ------------------------------
exclude_order <- c("Ixodida", "Mesostigmata", "Pseudoscorpiones", "Sarcoptiformes", 
                   "Trombidiformes", "Enchytraeida", "Hirudinida", "Monhysterida", 
                   "Plectida", "Rhabditida")

PHYSEQ <- prune_taxa(!(tax_table(PHYSEQ)[,"Order"] %in% exclude_order), PHYSEQ)
#4727  taxa and 827 samples
# ------------------------------
# Family-level filtering
# ------------------------------
exclude_family <- c("Aeolosomatidae")

PHYSEQ <- prune_taxa(!(tax_table(PHYSEQ)[,"Family"] %in% exclude_family), PHYSEQ)
#4726  taxa and 827 samples

# ------------------------------
# Final object
# ------------------------------
PNG_18S_rodent_filtered <- PHYSEQ
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 4726 taxa and 827 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 827 samples by 143 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 4729 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 4729 reference sequences ]


sum(otu_table(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered))/sum(otu_table(PNG_18S_rodent.dupl))
#67% of data is excluded


tax_table_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered))

# Add the "Diet" column
library(dplyr)
tax_table_df$Diet <- case_when(
  tax_table_df$Phylum %in% c("Arthropoda", "Chordata", "Mollusca", "Annelida") ~ "Animal",
  tax_table_df$Phylum %in% c("Ascomycota", "Basidiomycota") ~ "Fungi",
  tax_table_df$Phylum %in% c("Streptophyta", "Chlorophyta") ~ "Plant",
  TRUE ~ "NA"
)
# Reorder columns to move Diet to the first position
# Reorder columns to make Diet the second column
tax_table_df <- tax_table_df %>%
  select(1, Diet, everything())


# Convert the modified data frame back to a matrix
tax_table_new <- as.matrix(tax_table_df)

# Overwrite the taxonomy table in the phyloseq object
tax_table(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered) <- tax_table_new


save(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered.R")




### This part added here after final analyses:
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# 1. Pre-process the initial phyloseq
# Remove samples with less than 500 reads
PNG_initial_trimmed <- prune_samples(sample_sums(PNG_18S_rodent.dupl) >= 500, PNG_18S_rodent.dupl)

# 2. Identify the taxa present in 'initial' but NOT in 'final'
all_taxa_initial <- taxa_names(PNG_initial_trimmed)
all_taxa_final <- taxa_names(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)
excluded_taxa_ids <- setdiff(all_taxa_initial, all_taxa_final)

# 3. Calculate Raw Abundance for all taxa in the initial object
# taxa_sums() calculates the total number of reads for each ASV across all samples
abundance_data <- data.frame(Raw_Abundance = taxa_sums(PNG_initial_trimmed)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ASV_ID")

# 4. Create the taxonomy table and join with abundance
tax_table_all <- as.data.frame(tax_table(PNG_initial_trimmed)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ASV_ID")

excluded_taxa_table <- tax_table_all %>%
  filter(ASV_ID %in% excluded_taxa_ids) %>%
  left_join(abundance_data, by = "ASV_ID") %>%
  select(ASV_ID, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Raw_Abundance)

# 5. Optional: Sort by abundance so the most common filtered taxa are at the top
excluded_taxa_table <- excluded_taxa_table %>%
  arrange(desc(Raw_Abundance))

# 6. Save the results
write.csv(excluded_taxa_table, "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/tables/supplementary_excluded_taxa_with_abundance.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Quick check of the top 10 most abundant excluded taxa
head(excluded_taxa_table, 10)

```

*Prepare sampling table*
```{r samtable, warning=F, message=F}
rm(list = ls())
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)

load("D:/Rwd/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PHYSEQ=PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# 1. Access PHYSEQ sample data and explicitly coerce the class
metadata_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(PHYSEQ))
# --- FIX --- Explicitly overwrite the class to ensure dplyr treats it as a standard data.frame
class(metadata_df) <- "data.frame"
# -----------

# 2. Process and create the count table
count_table_exact_altitudes <- metadata_df %>%
    
    # Select the relevant columns (Alt and Metalokalita)
    dplyr::select(Alt, Metalokalita) %>%
    
    # Group by exact Altitude and Mountain Range, then count the samples
    dplyr::group_by(Alt, Metalokalita) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
    
    # Pivot the data to create separate columns for the mountains
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
        names_from = Metalokalita,
        values_from = Count,
        values_fill = 0
    ) %>%
    
    # Calculate the Total column
    dplyr::mutate(Total = Finisterres + Mt_Wilhelm) %>%
    
    # Rename and order the columns and rows
    dplyr::rename(Altitudes = Alt) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(Altitudes) %>%
    
    # Ensure final column order is correct
    dplyr::select(Altitudes, Finisterres, Mt_Wilhelm, Total)

# Print the resulting table
print(count_table_exact_altitudes)

# Optional: Save the table to a CSV file
write_csv(count_table_exact_altitudes, file="D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/tables_new/sampling/sampling.csv")

```
*Prepare barplots*
```{r barplots, warning=F, message=F}
rm(list = ls())
# Load required packages
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggnewscale)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered.R")

PHYSEQ=PNG_18S_rodent_filtered
unique(sort(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$ecological_Ensembles))
# [1] "large.na_herbivore-rodent"         "na.scansorial_omnivore-rodent"     "na.terrestrial_carnivore-rodent"  
# [4] "na.terrestrial_omnivore-rodent"    "small.scansorial_herbivore-rodent"

sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Ecological_ensembles <- gsub("-", "_", gsub("\\.", "_", sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Ecological_ensembles))

unique(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Ecological_ensembles)
# [1] "na_terrestrial_omnivore_rodent"    "small_scansorial_herbivore_rodent" "na_terrestrial_carnivore_rodent"  
# [4] "na_scansorial_omnivore_rodent"     "large_na_herbivore_rodent"  

# Split the column into parts
split_data <- strsplit(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Ecological_ensembles, "_")

# Assign parts to new columns
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$size <- sapply(split_data, `[`, 1)       # 1st part
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$lifestyle <- sapply(split_data, `[`, 2)  # 2nd part
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$diet <- sapply(split_data, `[`, 3)       # 3rd part


####import new lifestyle information for missing samples
unique(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$lifestyle)
# [1] "terrestrial" "scansorial"  "na" 

species_lifestyle <- c(
  "Anisomys imitatorFR" = "scansorial",
  "Anisomys imitatorMW" = "scansorial",
  "Hyomys goliath" = "terrestrial",
  "Mallomys cf_aroaensis" = "terrestrial",
  "Mallomys hercules" = "terrestrial",
  "Mallomys istapantap" = "terrestrial",
  "Mallomys rothschildi" = "scansorial",
  "Uromys anak" = "scansorial"
)

# Get the sample data
sam_data <- sample_data(PHYSEQ)

# Update lifestyle for matching species
sam_data$lifestyle <- ifelse(
  sam_data$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others %in% names(species_lifestyle),
  species_lifestyle[sam_data$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others],
  sam_data$lifestyle  # Keep original value if no match
)

# Update the phyloseq object
sample_data(PHYSEQ) <- sam_data

#update the new_ensembles column
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$new_ensembles <- with(sample_data(PHYSEQ),
  paste(size, lifestyle, diet, sep = "_"))

unique(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$new_ensembles)
# [1] "na_terrestrial_omnivore"     "small_scansorial_herbivore"  "na_terrestrial_carnivore"   
# [4] "na_scansorial_omnivore"      "large_terrestrial_herbivore" "large_scansorial_herbivore" 
# Collapse to Order level and keep top 25 most abundant orders


sample_data(PHYSEQ)$new_ensembles <- ifelse(
  # Condition: Only modify if ensemble is "na_terrestrial_omnivore"
  sample_data(PHYSEQ)$new_ensembles == "na_terrestrial_omnivore",
  # If TRUE: Append tribe (lowercase)
  paste(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$new_ensembles, tolower(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Tribe), sep = "_"),
  # If FALSE: Keep original ensemble
  sample_data(PHYSEQ)$new_ensembles
)

# Verify
unique(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$new_ensembles)
# [1] "na_terrestrial_omnivore_rattini"    "small_scansorial_herbivore"        
# [3] "na_terrestrial_omnivore_hydromyini" "na_terrestrial_carnivore"          
# [5] "na_scansorial_omnivore"             "large_terrestrial_herbivore"       
# [7] "large_scansorial_herbivore"          "large_na_herbivore" 

PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles=PHYSEQ
save(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles.R")

############################################## BARPLOTS ##########################
##################################################################################
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggnewscale)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)
rm(list = ls())
load("D:/Rwd/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PHYSEQ=PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# =========================
# Merge Mountain Ranges
# =========================
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Metalokalita <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Metalokalita %in% c("Baitabag", "Nagada", "Wanang"),
  "Mt_Wilhelm",
  sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Metalokalita
)

# Convert to factor and drop unused levels
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Metalokalita <- factor(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Metalokalita)

# Verify the result
unique(sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Metalokalita)

# Rename WG200 -> MW150 in the Loc column
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Loc[sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Loc == "WG200"] <- "MW150"
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Loc[sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Loc == "BAI"] <- "MW45"

#Remove norvecigus
PHYSEQ=prune_samples(!sample_data(PHYSEQ)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others=="Rattus norvegicus", PHYSEQ)
PHYSEQ <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ) > 0, PHYSEQ)

PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean=PHYSEQ
save(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")

# ===============================
# ====================================================================
# SECTION 1: AGGREGATION, MERGING, and GLOBAL SETUP
# ====================================================================
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)
library(ggnewscale) 


manage_unassigned<-function(PHYLOSEQ,UNASS_STRING=NA,ADD,AFTER=TRUE){
   TAXall<-data.frame(tax_table(PHYLOSEQ),stringsAsFactors = F)
   if(!is.na(UNASS_STRING)){TAXall[UNASS_STRING]<-NA}
   # TAXall<-as.character(TAXall[i,])
   for(i in 1:dim(TAXall)[1]){
       TAX<-as.character(TAXall[i,])
       if(AFTER==TRUE) {for(j in 2: length(TAX)){
                            if(is.na(TAX[j])){TAX[j]<-ifelse(regexpr(ADD,TAX[j-1])>0,
                                           TAX[j-1],paste(TAX[j-1],ADD,sep=""))}}}
      if(AFTER==FALSE) {for(j in 2: length(TAX)){
                            if(is.na(TAX[j])){TAX[j]<-ifelse(regexpr(ADD,TAX[j-1])>0,
                                     TAX[j-1],ADD,paste(TAX[j-1],sep=""))}}}
      TAXall[i,]<-TAX
    }
   TAXA<-colnames(TAXall)
   SPECIES<-rownames(TAXall)
   TAXall<-tax_table(TAXall)
   taxa_names(TAXall)<-SPECIES
   colnames(TAXall)<-TAXA
   tax_table(PHYLOSEQ)<-TAXall

   #output
   PHYLOSEQ
}




# ====================================================================
# SECTION 1: AGGREGATION and GLOBAL SETUP
# ====================================================================
PHYSEQ=manage_unassigned(PHYLOSEQ=PHYSEQ, UNASS_STRING = "NA", ADD = "", AFTER=TRUE)
which(tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"]=="") #1942 1943 1944 3910 4217

tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"][1942]="Class:Agaricomycetes1"
tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"][1943]="Class:Agaricomycetes2"
tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"][1944]="Class:Agaricomycetes3"
tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"][3910]="Class:Gastropoda1"
tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"][4217]="Class:Symphyla1"


# 1. AGGREGATION: Order (from original PHYSEQ, no pre-merging)
PHYSEQ_order <- tax_glom(PHYSEQ, taxrank = 'Order')


# 2. Define the base distinct color palette (26 colors)
# We do NOT expand this to 130 colors anymore.
base_colors <- c("#E31A1C", "green4", "#6A3D9A", "#FF7F00", "black", "dodgerblue2",
                 "skyblue2", "#FB9A99", "palegreen2", "#CAB2D6", "#FDBF6F", "gold1", "khaki2",
                 "maroon", "orchid1", "deeppink1", "blue1", "steelblue4", "darkturquoise",
                 "green1", "yellow4", "yellow3", "darkorange4", "brown", "purple", "darkred", "pink")

# 3. Define ensembles
ensembles <- list(
    TER_OMN_RAT = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_order, new_ensembles == "na_terrestrial_omnivore_rattini"),
    TER_OMN_HYD = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_order, new_ensembles == "na_terrestrial_omnivore_hydromyini"),
    SMA_SCA_HER = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_order, new_ensembles == "small_scansorial_herbivore"),
    TER_CAR     = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_order, new_ensembles == "na_terrestrial_carnivore"),
    SCA_OMN     = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_order, new_ensembles == "na_scansorial_omnivore"),
    LAR_TER_HER = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_order, new_ensembles == "large_terrestrial_herbivore"),
    LAR_SCA_HER = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_order, new_ensembles == "large_scansorial_herbivore")
)

# Store plots
ensemble_plots <- list()

# =========================
# Plotting function - Keep top 25 orders per ensemble
# =========================
plot_ensemble <- function(ps_obj, ensemble_name) {
    # Prune zero taxa/samples
    ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_obj) > 0, ps_obj)
    ps_obj <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps_obj) > 0, ps_obj)
    
    cat("  Initial taxa count:", ntaxa(ps_obj), "\n")
    
    # Get taxonomy table and OTU matrix
    tax_tab <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_obj))
    otu_mat <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
    if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) {
        otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)  # Ensure taxa are rows
    }
    
    # Calculate abundance per taxon (Order)
    tax_tab$taxa_id <- rownames(tax_tab)
    tax_tab$total_abundance <- rowSums(otu_mat)
    
    # Count unique orders
    unique_orders <- unique(tax_tab$Order)
    cat("  Unique orders present:", length(unique_orders), "\n")
    
    # Summarize abundance by Order
    order_abundance <- tax_tab %>%
        group_by(Order) %>%
        summarize(
            order_total = sum(total_abundance),
            n_taxa = n()
        ) %>%
        arrange(desc(order_total))
    
    # Check for orders with multiple taxa (safety check)
    multi_taxa_orders <- order_abundance %>% filter(n_taxa > 1)
    if (nrow(multi_taxa_orders) > 0) {
        cat("  WARNING:", nrow(multi_taxa_orders), "orders have multiple taxa - re-aggregating\n")
        ps_obj <- tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = 'Order')
        
        # Recalculate after re-aggregation
        tax_tab <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_obj))
        otu_mat <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
        if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) {
            otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
        }
        tax_tab$taxa_id <- rownames(tax_tab)
        tax_tab$total_abundance <- rowSums(otu_mat)
        order_abundance <- tax_tab %>%
            group_by(Order) %>%
            summarize(order_total = sum(total_abundance)) %>%
            arrange(desc(order_total))
    }
    
    n_orders_present <- nrow(order_abundance)
    cat("  Final unique orders:", n_orders_present, "\n")
    
    # Determine top 25 orders
    n_top <- min(25, n_orders_present)
    top_orders <- order_abundance$Order[1:n_top]
    orders_to_merge <- setdiff(order_abundance$Order, top_orders)
    
    cat("  Keeping top", n_top, "orders\n")
    
    # If we have more than 25 orders, merge the rest into "Others"
    if (length(orders_to_merge) > 0) {
        cat("  Merging", length(orders_to_merge), "orders into 'Others'\n")
        
        taxa_to_merge <- tax_tab$taxa_id[tax_tab$Order %in% orders_to_merge]
        taxa_to_keep <- tax_tab$taxa_id[tax_tab$Order %in% top_orders]
        
        others_abundance <- colSums(otu_mat[taxa_to_merge, , drop = FALSE])
        otu_mat_filtered <- otu_mat[taxa_to_keep, , drop = FALSE]
        otu_mat_final <- rbind(otu_mat_filtered, Others = others_abundance)
        
        tax_tab_filtered <- as.matrix(tax_table(ps_obj)[taxa_to_keep, , drop = FALSE])
        
        others_tax <- tax_tab_filtered[1, , drop = FALSE]
        rownames(others_tax) <- "Others"
        others_tax[, "Order"] <- "Others"
        for (col in colnames(others_tax)) {
            if (col != "Order") {
                others_tax[, col] <- "Others"
            }
        }
        
        tax_tab_final <- rbind(tax_tab_filtered, others_tax)
        
        otu_table_new <- otu_table(otu_mat_final, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
        tax_table_new <- tax_table(tax_tab_final)
        
        ps_obj <- phyloseq(otu_table_new, tax_table_new, sample_data(ps_obj))
        
        cat("  Final taxa count after merging:", ntaxa(ps_obj), "\n")
    } else {
        cat("  No orders to merge (total orders <= 25)\n")
    }
    
    # Normalize
    ps_obj <- transform_sample_counts(ps_obj, function(x) x / sum(x))

    # Extract metadata and order samples
    species_vec     <- as.character(sample_data(ps_obj)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)
    species_vec[is.na(species_vec)] <- "Unknown"
    mountain_vec  <- as.character(sample_data(ps_obj)$Metalokalita)
    mountain_vec[is.na(mountain_vec)] <- "Unknown"
    altitude_vec  <- as.numeric(sample_data(ps_obj)$Alt)

    sample_df <- data.frame(
        sample_ID = sample_names(ps_obj), species = species_vec, 
        mountain = mountain_vec, altitude = altitude_vec, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    sample_df <- sample_df %>% arrange(species, mountain, altitude)
    ordered_sample_names <- sample_df$sample_ID
    sample_data(ps_obj)$sample_ID <- factor(sample_names(ps_obj), levels = ordered_sample_names)

    species_vec  <- sample_df$species
    mountain_vec <- sample_df$mountain
    altitude_vec <- sample_df$altitude

    # =========================
    # Annotations
    # =========================
    n_species <- length(unique(species_vec))
    species_levels <- unique(species_vec)
    species_colors <- setNames(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(8, "Set1"))(n_species), species_levels)
    
    custom_palette <- c("#6dd3ce", "#c8e9a0", "#f7a278", "#a13d63", "#351e29")
    mtn_levels <- unique(mountain_vec)
    n_mtn <- length(mtn_levels)
    mountain_colors <- setNames(rep(custom_palette, length.out = n_mtn), mtn_levels)

    species_df <- sample_df %>%
        group_by(species) %>%
        summarize(xmin = min(as.numeric(factor(sample_ID, levels = ordered_sample_names))) - 0.5,
                  xmax = max(as.numeric(factor(sample_ID, levels = ordered_sample_names))) + 0.5) %>%
        ungroup()
    mountain_rle <- rle(mountain_vec)
    start_idx <- cumsum(c(1, head(mountain_rle$lengths, -1)))
    end_idx    <- cumsum(mountain_rle$lengths)
    mountain_df <- data.frame(
        xmin = start_idx - 0.5, xmax = end_idx + 0.5, mountain = mountain_rle$values
    )
    
    annotation_height <- 0.05
    annotation_gap    <- 0.02
    species_ymin   <- -annotation_height - annotation_gap
    species_ymax   <- -annotation_gap
    mountain_ymin  <- -2*annotation_height - 2*annotation_gap
    mountain_ymax  <- -annotation_height - 2*annotation_gap
    altitude_y     <- -3*annotation_height - 3*annotation_gap + annotation_height/2

    title_map <- c(TER_OMN_RAT = "Terrestrial Omnivore Rattini", TER_OMN_HYD = "Terrestrial Omnivore Hydromyini", 
                   SMA_SCA_HER = "Small Scansorial Herbivores", TER_CAR = "Terrestrial Carnivores",
                   SCA_OMN = "Scansorial Omnivores", LAR_TER_HER = "Large Terrestrial Herbivores",
                   LAR_SCA_HER = "Large Scansorial Herbivores")
    plot_title <- ifelse(ensemble_name %in% names(title_map), title_map[[ensemble_name]], ensemble_name)

    # =========================
    # DYNAMIC COLOR MAPPING (Fixed Section)
    # =========================
    
    # 1. Get the orders present in this specific ensemble (Top 25 + Others)
    tax_table_df_ensemble <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_obj))
    orders_in_ensemble <- unique(tax_table_df_ensemble$Order)
    
    # 2. Separate "Others" from the regular taxonomic orders
    regular_orders <- orders_in_ensemble[orders_in_ensemble != "Others"]
    
    # 3. Create a local palette map using base_colors for regular orders
    # We take the first N colors from base_colors, where N is the number of regular orders
    local_colors <- base_colors[1:length(regular_orders)]
    ensemble_color_map <- setNames(local_colors, regular_orders)
    
    # 4. Add gray for "Others" if it exists
    if ("Others" %in% orders_in_ensemble) {
        ensemble_color_map["Others"] <- "gray70"
    }

    # 5. Create Diet Labels (Local logic only)
    order_diet_map_ensemble <- tax_table_df_ensemble %>%
        select(Order, Diet) %>%
        distinct() %>%
        group_by(Order) %>%
        summarize(Diet = first(Diet))
    
    # Sort orders for the legend: 
    # Option A: By Abundance (matches the bar order) -> using top_orders from earlier
    # Option B: By Diet (matches original script intent)
    # Let's use Diet to keep legend organized, but ensure Others is last
    order_levels_ensemble <- regular_orders
    
    # Create labels
    order_labels_with_diet_ensemble <- sapply(order_levels_ensemble, function(ord) {
        diet_val <- order_diet_map_ensemble$Diet[order_diet_map_ensemble$Order == ord]
        if (length(diet_val) > 0 && !is.na(diet_val)) {
            paste0(diet_val, ": ", ord)
        } else {
            ord
        }
    })
    
    # Add Others to levels and labels if needed
    if ("Others" %in% orders_in_ensemble) {
        order_levels_ensemble <- c(order_levels_ensemble, "Others")
        order_labels_with_diet_ensemble <- c(order_labels_with_diet_ensemble, Others = "Others")
    }
    
    names(order_labels_with_diet_ensemble) <- order_levels_ensemble

    # =========================
    # PLOT
    # =========================
    p <- plot_bar(ps_obj, x = "sample_ID", fill = "Order") +
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
        scale_fill_manual(
            values = ensemble_color_map,
            breaks = order_levels_ensemble,
            labels = order_labels_with_diet_ensemble,
            name = "Taxonomic Order",
            guide = guide_legend(order = 1)
        ) +
        theme_classic() +
        theme(
            text = element_text(size = 18),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            legend.position = "right"
        ) +
        # Species merged color bar
        ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
        geom_rect(
            data = species_df,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = species_ymin, ymax = species_ymax, fill = species),
            inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = species_colors, name = "Species", guide = guide_legend(order = 2)) +
        # Mountain merged color bar
        ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
        geom_rect(
            data = mountain_df,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = mountain_ymin, ymax = mountain_ymax, fill = mountain),
            inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = mountain_colors, name = "Mountain range", guide = guide_legend(order = 3)) +
        # Altitude continuous color bar
        ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
        geom_tile(
            data = data.frame(sample_ID = ordered_sample_names, altitude = altitude_vec, y = altitude_y),
            aes(x = sample_ID, y = y, fill = altitude),
            height = annotation_height, width = 0.9, inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_gradientn(
            colours = viridis(100),
            name = "Altitude",
            guide = guide_colorbar(order = 4)
        ) +
        scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.05)), limits = c(-0.27, NA)) +
        ggtitle(plot_title)

    assign(paste0(ensemble_name, "_plot_Order"), p, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    ensemble_plots[[ensemble_name]] <<- p
    
    cat("  --> Final display: ", length(orders_in_ensemble), " groups plotted.\n\n")
}

# =========================
# Generate plots
# =========================
for (ensemble_name in names(ensembles)) {
    cat("========================================\n")
    cat("Creating plot for:", ensemble_name, "\n")
    plot_ensemble(ensembles[[ensemble_name]], ensemble_name)
}

# Example to view the final plot
# TER_OMN_RAT_plot_Order
# Creating plot for: TER_OMN_RAT_plot_Order
# Creating plot for: TER_OMN_HYD_plot_Order
# Creating plot for: SMA_SCA_HER_plot_Order
# Creating plot for: TER_CAR_plot_Order
# Creating plot for: SCA_OMN_plot_Order
# Creating plot for: LAR_TER_HER_plot_Order
# Creating plot for: LAR_SCA_HER_plot_Order


#Class level plots
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)
library(ggnewscale) 

# ====================================================================
# GLOBAL SETUP: Aggregation and Fixed Color Map
# ====================================================================

# 1. AGGREGATION: Class
PHYSEQ_class <- tax_glom(PHYSEQ, taxrank = 'Class')

# 2. Define a complete, expanded color palette (30+ colors)
# We use a mix of existing colors and colors generated by colorRampPalette for uniqueness.
base_colors <- c("#E31A1C", "green4","gray70" , "#6A3D9A", "#FF7F00", "black", "dodgerblue2",
                 "skyblue2", "#FB9A99", "palegreen2", "#CAB2D6", "#FDBF6F", "gold1", "khaki2",
                 "maroon", "orchid1", "deeppink1", "blue1", "steelblue4", "darkturquoise",
                 "green1", "yellow4", "yellow3", "darkorange4", "brown", "purple", "darkred", "pink")
# Generate additional distinct colors (using Set1 and Dark2 palettes)
n_needed <- length(unique(tax_table(PHYSEQ_class)[, "Class"]))
if (n_needed > length(base_colors)) {
    extra_colors <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(8, "Set1"))(n_needed - length(base_colors))
    class_colors_all <- c(base_colors, extra_colors)
} else {
    class_colors_all <- base_colors[1:n_needed]
}

# 3. Create GLOBAL Class-Diet Mapping and Color Map
tax_table_df_global <- as.data.frame(tax_table(PHYSEQ_class))

# Global Class-Diet map for consistent ordering
class_diet_map_global <- tax_table_df_global %>%
    select(Class, Diet) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    group_by(Class) %>%
    summarize(Diet = first(Diet)) %>%
    arrange(Diet, Class)

# Global ordered factor levels for the legend
class_levels_global <- class_diet_map_global$Class
class_labels_with_diet_global <- paste0(class_diet_map_global$Diet, ": ", class_diet_map_global$Class)
names(class_labels_with_diet_global) <- class_diet_map_global$Class

# Global color map: maps every unique Class to a fixed color
class_color_map_global <- setNames(class_colors_all[1:n_needed], class_levels_global)


# 4. Define ensembles (using PHYSEQ_class)
ensembles <- list(
    TER_OMN_RAT = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_class, new_ensembles == "na_terrestrial_omnivore_rattini"),
    TER_OMN_HYD = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_class, new_ensembles == "na_terrestrial_omnivore_hydromyini"),
    SMA_SCA_HER = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_class, new_ensembles == "small_scansorial_herbivore"),
    TER_CAR     = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_class, new_ensembles == "na_terrestrial_carnivore"),
    SCA_OMN     = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_class, new_ensembles == "na_scansorial_omnivore"),
    LAR_TER_HER = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_class, new_ensembles == "large_terrestrial_herbivore"),
    LAR_SCA_HER = subset_samples(PHYSEQ_class, new_ensembles == "large_scansorial_herbivore")
)

# Store plots
ensemble_plots <- list()

# =========================
# Plotting function (Modified to use GLOBAL color and level maps)
# =========================
plot_ensemble <- function(ps_obj, ensemble_name) {
    # Prune zero taxa/samples and normalize
    ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_obj) > 0, ps_obj)
    ps_obj <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps_obj) > 0, ps_obj)
    ps_obj <- transform_sample_counts(ps_obj, function(x) x / sum(x))

    # Extract metadata and order samples (Same as original logic)
    species_vec     <- as.character(sample_data(ps_obj)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)
    species_vec[is.na(species_vec)] <- "Unknown"
    mountain_vec  <- as.character(sample_data(ps_obj)$Metalokalita)
    mountain_vec[is.na(mountain_vec)] <- "Unknown"
    altitude_vec  <- as.numeric(sample_data(ps_obj)$Alt)

    sample_df <- data.frame(
        sample_ID = sample_names(ps_obj), species = species_vec, 
        mountain = mountain_vec, altitude = altitude_vec, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    sample_df <- sample_df %>% arrange(species, mountain, altitude)
    ordered_sample_names <- sample_df$sample_ID
    sample_data(ps_obj)$sample_ID <- factor(sample_names(ps_obj), levels = ordered_sample_names)

    species_vec  <- sample_df$species
    mountain_vec <- sample_df$mountain
    altitude_vec <- sample_df$altitude

    # =========================
    # Annotations (No change needed)
    # =========================
    # Species colors
    n_species <- length(unique(species_vec))
    species_levels <- unique(species_vec)
    species_colors <- setNames(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(8, "Set1"))(n_species), species_levels)
    
    # Mountain range colors
    custom_palette <- c("#6dd3ce", "#c8e9a0", "#f7a278", "#a13d63", "#351e29")
    mtn_levels <- unique(mountain_vec)
    n_mtn <- length(mtn_levels)
    mountain_colors <- setNames(rep(custom_palette, length.out = n_mtn), mtn_levels)

    # Annotation rectangles and positioning... (omitted for brevity, assume original logic is here)
    species_df <- sample_df %>%
        group_by(species) %>%
        summarize(xmin = min(as.numeric(factor(sample_ID, levels = ordered_sample_names))) - 0.5,
                  xmax = max(as.numeric(factor(sample_ID, levels = ordered_sample_names))) + 0.5) %>%
        ungroup()
    mountain_rle <- rle(mountain_vec)
    start_idx <- cumsum(c(1, head(mountain_rle$lengths, -1)))
    end_idx    <- cumsum(mountain_rle$lengths)
    mountain_df <- data.frame(
        xmin = start_idx - 0.5, xmax = end_idx + 0.5, mountain = mountain_rle$values
    )
    
    annotation_height <- 0.05
    annotation_gap    <- 0.02
    species_ymin   <- -annotation_height - annotation_gap
    species_ymax   <- -annotation_gap
    mountain_ymin  <- -2*annotation_height - 2*annotation_gap
    mountain_ymax  <- -annotation_height - 2*annotation_gap
    altitude_y     <- -3*annotation_height - 3*annotation_gap + annotation_height/2

    title_map <- c(TER_OMN_RAT = "Terrestrial Omnivore Rattini", TER_OMN_HYD = "Terrestrial Omnivore Hydromyini", 
                   SMA_SCA_HER = "Small Scansorial Herbivores", TER_CAR = "Terrestrial Carnivores",
                   SCA_OMN = "Scansorial Omnivores", LAR_TER_HER = "Large Terrestrial Herbivores",
                   LAR_SCA_HER = "Large Scansorial Herbivores")
    plot_title <- ifelse(ensemble_name %in% names(title_map), title_map[[ensemble_name]], ensemble_name)


    # =========================
    # PLOT: Uses GLOBAL scales
    # =========================
    p <- plot_bar(ps_obj, x = "sample_ID", fill = "Class") + 
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
        scale_fill_manual(
            # --- USE GLOBAL MAPS HERE ---
            values = class_color_map_global, 
            breaks = class_levels_global,
            labels = class_labels_with_diet_global,
            name = "Taxonomic Class",
            guide = guide_legend(order = 1)
        ) +
        theme_classic() +
        theme(
            text = element_text(size = 18),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            legend.position = "right"
        ) +
        # Species merged color bar
        ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
        geom_rect(
            data = species_df,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = species_ymin, ymax = species_ymax, fill = species),
            inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = species_colors, name = "Species", guide = guide_legend(order = 2)) +
        # Mountain merged color bar (contiguous blocks)
        ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
        geom_rect(
            data = mountain_df,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = mountain_ymin, ymax = mountain_ymax, fill = mountain),
            inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = mountain_colors, name = "Mountain range", guide = guide_legend(order = 3)) +
        # Altitude continuous color bar
        ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
        geom_tile(
            data = data.frame(sample_ID = ordered_sample_names, altitude = altitude_vec, y = altitude_y),
            aes(x = sample_ID, y = y, fill = altitude),
            height = annotation_height, width = 0.9, inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_gradientn(
            colours = viridis(100),
            name = "Altitude",
            guide = guide_colorbar(order = 4)
        ) +
        scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.05)), limits = c(-0.27, NA)) +
        ggtitle(plot_title)

    assign(paste0(ensemble_name, "_plot_Class"), p, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    ensemble_plots[[ensemble_name]] <<- p
}

# =========================
# Generate plots
# =========================
for (ensemble_name in names(ensembles)) {
    cat("Creating plot for:", ensemble_name, "\n")
    plot_ensemble(ensembles[[ensemble_name]], ensemble_name)
}
# TER_OMN_RAT_plot_Class # Example to view the final plot



### Merged Plots
# ====================================================================
# SECTION 0: LOAD LIBRARIES
# ====================================================================
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2) # Already loaded, but good practice

# ====================================================================
# SECTION 1: DEFINITIONS (Must match your original script's output)
# ====================================================================

ensemble_names_list <- c(
    "TER_OMN_RAT", "TER_OMN_HYD", "SMA_SCA_HER", "TER_CAR",
    "SCA_OMN", "LAR_TER_HER", "LAR_SCA_HER"
)

title_map <- c(
    TER_OMN_RAT = "Terrestrial Omnivore Rattini",
    TER_OMN_HYD = "Terrestrial Omnivore Hydromyini",
    SMA_SCA_HER = "Small Scansorial Herbivores",
    TER_CAR     = "Terrestrial Carnivores",
    SCA_OMN     = "Scansorial Omnivores",
    LAR_TER_HER = "Large Terrestrial Herbivores",
    LAR_SCA_HER = "Large Scansorial Herbivores"
)

# Store the final merged figures
final_side_by_side_plots <- list()

# ====================================================================
# SECTION 2: MERGING LOOP
# ====================================================================

for (ensemble in ensemble_names_list) {
    cat(sprintf("Merging plots for ensemble: %s...\n", ensemble))

    # 1. Retrieve the original, unmodified plots
    p_order <- get(paste0(ensemble, "_plot_Order"))
    p_class <- get(paste0(ensemble, "_plot_Class"))

    # 2. Add sub-titles to plots to denote the taxonomic rank
    # NOTE: Your original plots already have titles (from the plot_ensemble function) 
    # which is the ensemble name. We need to override that or ensure it is blank.
    
    # Temporarily remove the ensemble title from individual plots
    p_order_temp <- p_order + ggtitle("A) Order Level") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
    p_class_temp <- p_class + ggtitle("B) Class Level") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))


    # 3. Use patchwork to combine the plots horizontally (|)
    merged_figure <- p_order_temp | p_class_temp
    
    # 4. Add the main ensemble title
    final_figure <- merged_figure +
        plot_annotation(
            title = title_map[[ensemble]],
            theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 20))
        )
    
    # 5. Store and Display
    final_side_by_side_plots[[ensemble]] <- final_figure
    
    if (ensemble == ensemble_names_list[1]) {
        cat("\nDisplaying the first merged plot (TER_OMN_RAT) side-by-side:\n")
        print(final_figure)
    }
}
# To view any specific merged plot, run:
# final_side_by_side_plots[["SCA_OMN"]]
```

```{r tree, warning=F, message=F}
head(HYDR_TREE_UPT$tip.label) # "Melomys.low", "Paramelomys.platyopsBAI", etc.

# Function to prune tree to one tip per species
prune_to_one_per_species <- function(tree) {
  # Create a data frame of tip labels
  tip_data <- data.frame(
    tip_label = tree$tip.label,
    species = tree$tip.label,  # In your case, whole label is species name
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # For each species, keep the first occurrence
  tips_to_keep <- tip_data %>%
    group_by(species) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    pull(tip_label)
  
  # Prune the tree
  pruned_tree <- keep.tip(tree, tips_to_keep)
  return(pruned_tree)
}

# Apply to both trees
RAT_TREE_CLEANED <- prune_to_one_per_species(RAT_TREE_UPT)
HYDR_TREE_CLEANED <- prune_to_one_per_species(HYDR_TREE_UPT)



# Optionally, save the cleaned trees with species names as tips
write.tree(RAT_TREE_CLEANED, "D:/Rwd/papuang/ddRAD_trees/cleaned_RAT_tree_species_names.treefile")
write.tree(HYDR_TREE_CLEANED, "D:/Rwd/papuang/ddRAD_trees/cleaned_HYD_tree_species_names.treefile")

# phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(RAT, function(x) x / sum(x))

# phyloseq_species <- merge_samples(phyloseq_diet, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun=mean)
# phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_species, function(x) x / sum(x))


# Collapse to Diet level
phyloseq_diet <- tax_glom(RAT, taxrank = "Diet")

# Merge samples by species
phyloseq_species <- merge_samples(phyloseq_diet, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun=mean)

# Calculate relative abundance per species
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_species, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Melt to long format
df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel)

# Clean and prepare data with sample sizes
df_summary <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
  summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(label = Sample) %>%
  left_join(sample_sizes, by = "label")  # Add sample size information

# Create a separate data frame for sample size labels
size_labels <- df_summary %>% 
  distinct(label, SampleSize)

# Plot base tree
p <- ggtree(RAT_TREE_UPT) +
  geom_tiplab(align = TRUE, linetype = "dotted", linesize = 0.3, offset = 0.001) +
  theme_tree2() + 
  xlim(0, 0.2)

# Add diet relative abundance bar plot
# Plot base tree
p <- ggtree(RAT_TREE_UPT, size= 0.8) +
  geom_tiplab(
    align = TRUE,
    linetype = "dotted",
    linesize = 0.5,
    fontface = 3,
    offset = 0.001,
    size = 14 / .pt  # Tip label font size
  ) +
  theme_tree2() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),  # X-axis tick labels
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14), # (if you add x-axis title later)
    text = element_text(size = 14)          # Global text setting fallback
  ) +
  xlim(0, 0.06) + # Note: Your xlim is 0.5, but breaks are only up to 0.05.
                # You might want to adjust xlim or breaks depending on your data range.
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 0.06, by = 0.015))
```
