```{r tree, warning=F, message=F}
# Add diet relative abundance bar plot
p2 <- p +
  geom_fruit(
    data = df_summary,
    geom = geom_bar,
    mapping = aes(y = label, x = rel_abundance, fill = Diet),
    stat = "identity",
    orientation = "y",
    offset = 0.35,  # Adjust as needed
    pwidth = 0.8,   # Adjust width of barplot
    axis.params = list(
      axis = "x",
      text.size = 5,
      text.angle = 90,
      breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1),
      hjust = 1,
    )
  ) +
  # Add sample size labels to the right of the barplots
  geom_fruit(
    data = size_labels,
    geom = geom_text,
    mapping = aes(y = label, label = paste0("n=", SampleSize)),
    offset = 0.01,  # Position to the right of barplots
    pwidth = 0.2,   # Width of text area
    size = 5,       # Text size
    hjust = 0       # Left-align text
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Diet",
    values = c(
      "Animal" = "#f8766d",  # Green
      "Plant" = "#00ba38",    # Orange (originally Fungi)
      "Fungi" = "#619cff"    # Purple (originally Plant)
    )
  )+
  # Add theme settings for legend AFTER geom_fruit and scale_fill_manual
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16), # Adjust size as needed for the title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14)   # Adjust size as needed for the elements
  )


p2

#save 1600*800

#save it transparent
p3=p2+theme(panel.background =element_rect(fill = "transparent",color = NA), plot.background =element_rect(fill = "transparent",color = NA), legend.background =element_rect(fill = "transparent",color = NA) )

ggsave("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/plots/trees/rat_transparent.png", plot = p3, bg = "transparent", width = 16, height = 9, dpi = 300)

# ============================================================================ #
# ====================== RAT INTERINDIVIDUAL VARIANCE TREE AND REL ABUND ===== #
# ============================================================================ #
library(ape)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggtree)
library(dplyr)
library(ggtreeExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(phytools)
library(tidytree)

rm(list = ls())

# ===== LOAD DATA =====
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")

# ===== SUBSET TO RATTINI =====
RAT <- prune_samples(!sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)$Tribe == "Hydromyini", 
                     PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)
RAT <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(RAT) > 0, RAT)

# ===== CALCULATE INTERINDIVIDUAL VARIATION =====
# Calculate distances at Order level
PNG_18S_Order <- tax_glom(RAT, taxrank = "Order")
wunifrac_dist <- phyloseq::distance(PNG_18S_Order, method = "wunifrac")

# Get species information
species_vector <- sample_data(PNG_18S_Order)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others

# Calculate betadisper
betadisper_model <- betadisper(wunifrac_dist, species_vector)

# Extract individual distances
individual_distances <- data.frame(
  Sample_ID = names(betadisper_model$distances),
  Species = betadisper_model$group,
  Distance_to_Centroid = betadisper_model$distances,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate average variation per species
species_variation <- individual_distances %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_distance = mean(Distance_to_Centroid),
    median_distance = median(Distance_to_Centroid),
    sd_distance = sd(Distance_to_Centroid),
    n_samples = n()
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ===== LOAD AND PREPARE TREE =====
RAT_TREE_UPT <- ape::read.nexus("D:/Rwd/papuang/ddRAD_trees/new_trees_19.11.2024/figtree_rat.nex")
RAT_TREE_UPT$tip.label <- gsub("_", " ", RAT_TREE_UPT$tip.label)
RAT_TREE_UPT$root.edge <- 0.5

# ===== PREPARE DATA FOR CONTMAP =====
# Create named vector for contMap
variation_vector <- setNames(species_variation$mean_distance, species_variation$Species)

# Keep only species in both tree and data
matching_species <- intersect(RAT_TREE_UPT$tip.label, names(variation_vector))
variation_vector_filtered <- variation_vector[matching_species]
RAT_TREE_pruned <- keep.tip(RAT_TREE_UPT, matching_species)

# ===== CREATE CONTMAP TREE =====
# Generate contMap object
contmap_obj <- contMap(RAT_TREE_pruned, 
                       variation_vector_filtered,
                       plot = FALSE,
                       res = 200)

# Set custom colors (low=blue, high=red)
contmap_obj <- setMap(contmap_obj, colors = c("blue", "cyan", "yellow", "orange", "red"))

# ===== CALCULATE PAGEL'S LAMBDA =====
lambda_result <- phylosig(RAT_TREE_pruned, 
                          variation_vector_filtered,
                          method = "lambda",
                          test = TRUE)

cat("\n===== Pagel's Lambda =====\n")
cat("Lambda:", round(lambda_result$lambda, 3), "\n")
cat("P-value:", round(lambda_result$P, 4), "\n\n")

# ===== EXTRACT TRAIT VALUES AND COLORS FROM CONTMAP =====
# Function to get color for a given trait value
get_color_for_value <- function(value, min_val, max_val, colors) {
  # Normalize value to 0-1 range
  norm_val <- (value - min_val) / (max_val - min_val)
  # Get color index (1 to length of colors)
  color_idx <- round(norm_val * (length(colors) - 1)) + 1
  color_idx <- max(1, min(color_idx, length(colors)))
  return(colors[color_idx])
}

# Get min and max values
min_var <- min(variation_vector_filtered)
max_var <- max(variation_vector_filtered)

# Create a data frame with tip trait values and colors
tip_traits <- data.frame(
  node = 1:Ntip(RAT_TREE_pruned),
  label = RAT_TREE_pruned$tip.label,
  trait_value = variation_vector_filtered[RAT_TREE_pruned$tip.label],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

tip_traits$trait_color <- sapply(tip_traits$trait_value, function(x) {
  get_color_for_value(x, min_var, max_var, contmap_obj$cols)
})

# ===== PREPARE DIET DATA FROM YOUR ORIGINAL SCRIPT =====
# First calculate sample sizes
sample_sizes <- sample_data(RAT) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  filter(!Tribe == "Hydromyini") %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarize(SampleSize = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(label = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

# Collapse to Diet level
phyloseq_diet <- tax_glom(RAT, taxrank = "Diet")

# Merge samples by species
phyloseq_species <- merge_samples(phyloseq_diet, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun = mean)

# Calculate relative abundance per species
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_species, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Melt to long format
df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel)

# Clean and prepare data with sample sizes
df_summary <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
  summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(label = Sample) %>%
  left_join(sample_sizes, by = "label")

# Create a separate data frame for sample size labels
size_labels <- df_summary %>% 
  distinct(label, SampleSize)

# ===== PLOT WITH COLORED BRANCHES =====
# Create base tree - we'll color branches using aes_string with the trait values
p <- ggtree(RAT_TREE_pruned, linewidth = 0.8) %<+% tip_traits +
  aes(color = trait_value) +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colors = c("blue", "cyan", "yellow", "orange", "red"),
    name = "Mean Distance\nto Centroid\n(wunifrac with betadisper)",
    limits = c(min_var, max_var)
  ) +
  geom_tiplab(
    align = TRUE,
    linetype = "dotted",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    fontface = 3,
    offset = 0.001,
    size = 14 / .pt,
    color = "black"  # Keep tip labels black
  ) +
  theme_tree2() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  xlim(0, 0.06) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 0.06, by = 0.015))

# Add diet relative abundance bar plot
p2 <- p +
  geom_fruit(
    data = df_summary,
    geom = geom_bar,
    mapping = aes(y = label, x = rel_abundance, fill = Diet),
    stat = "identity",
    orientation = "y",
    offset = 0.35,
    pwidth = 0.8,
    axis.params = list(
      axis = "x",
      text.size = 5,
      text.angle = 90,
      breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1),
      hjust = 1,
    )
  ) +
  # Add sample size labels
  geom_fruit(
    data = size_labels,
    geom = geom_text,
    mapping = aes(y = label, label = paste0("n=", SampleSize)),
    offset = 0.01,
    pwidth = 0.2,
    size = 5,
    hjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Diet",
    values = c(
      "Animal" = "#f8766d",
      "Plant" = "#00ba38",
      "Fungi" = "#619cff"
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14)
  )

# Display plot
p2

# ============================================================================ #
# ====================== RAT INTERINDIVIDUAL VARIANCE TREE AND REL ABUND AND ANCESTRAL STATE===== #
# ============================================================================ #
rm(list = ls())

library(ape)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggtree)
library(dplyr)
library(ggtreeExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(phytools)
library(tidytree)
library(tidyr)
library(viridis)
library(ggnewscale)
# ===== LOAD DATA (from your script) =====
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")

RAT <- prune_samples(!sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)$Tribe == "Hydromyini", 
                     PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)
RAT <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(RAT) > 0, RAT)

# ===== PREPARE DIET DATA =====
phyloseq_diet <- tax_glom(RAT, taxrank = "Diet")
phyloseq_species <- merge_samples(phyloseq_diet, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun = mean)
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_species, function(x) x / sum(x))

df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel)

# Get diet composition per species
diet_by_species <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
    group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
    summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
    rename(Species = Sample) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Diet, values_from = rel_abundance, values_fill = 0)

# Convert to matrix format for easier manipulation
diet_matrix <- as.data.frame(diet_by_species)
rownames(diet_matrix) <- diet_matrix$Species
diet_matrix$Species <- NULL
diet_matrix <- as.matrix(diet_matrix)

cat("Diet matrix:\n")
print(head(diet_matrix))

# ===== LOAD AND PREPARE TREE =====
RAT_TREE_UPT <- ape::read.nexus("D:/Rwd/papuang/ddRAD_trees/new_trees_19.11.2024/figtree_rat.nex")
RAT_TREE_UPT$tip.label <- gsub("_", " ", RAT_TREE_UPT$tip.label)
RAT_TREE_UPT$root.edge <- 0.5

# Get matching species
matching_species <- intersect(RAT_TREE_UPT$tip.label, rownames(diet_matrix))
diet_matrix_filtered <- diet_matrix[matching_species, ]
RAT_TREE_pruned <- keep.tip(RAT_TREE_UPT, matching_species)

cat("\nMatched", nrow(diet_matrix_filtered), "species between tree and diet data\n")

# ===== ANCESTRAL STATE RECONSTRUCTION FOR EACH DIET TYPE =====
# We'll do ASR for each diet category separately using continuous trait method

diet_types <- colnames(diet_matrix_filtered)
asr_results <- list()

cat("\n===== Performing Ancestral State Reconstruction =====\n")

for (diet_type in diet_types) {
    cat("Processing:", diet_type, "\n")
    
    # Get trait values for this diet type
    trait_values <- diet_matrix_filtered[, diet_type]
    names(trait_values) <- rownames(diet_matrix_filtered)
    
    # Perform ASR using phytools::ace (continuous trait, ML)
    asr_obj <- ace(trait_values, 
                   RAT_TREE_pruned, 
                   method = "REML",  # Restricted Maximum Likelihood
                   type = "continuous")
    
    asr_results[[diet_type]] <- asr_obj
}

# ===== CREATE NODE DATA FOR VISUALIZATION =====
# Extract ancestral state estimates for each diet type at each node
node_data <- data.frame(
    node = (Ntip(RAT_TREE_pruned) + 1):max(RAT_TREE_pruned$edge)
)

# Add ancestral state estimates for each diet type
for (diet_type in diet_types) {
    asr_obj <- asr_results[[diet_type]]
    # asr_obj$ace contains estimates for internal nodes
    node_data[[diet_type]] <- asr_obj$ace
}

cat("\nAncestral state estimates at nodes:\n")
print(head(node_data))

# ===== CALCULATE DOMINANT DIET AT EACH NODE =====
# The diet with highest proportion at each node
node_data$dominant_diet <- apply(node_data[, diet_types], 1, function(x) {
    diet_types[which.max(x)]
})

cat("\nDominant diet at ancestral nodes:\n")
print(head(node_data))

# ===== PREPARE DATA FOR PLOTTING =====
# Get interindividual variation data (from previous analysis)
PNG_18S_Order <- tax_glom(RAT, taxrank = "Order")
wunifrac_dist <- phyloseq::distance(PNG_18S_Order, method = "wunifrac")
species_vector <- sample_data(PNG_18S_Order)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others
betadisper_model <- betadisper(wunifrac_dist, species_vector)

individual_distances <- data.frame(
    Sample_ID = names(betadisper_model$distances),
    Species = betadisper_model$group,
    Distance_to_Centroid = betadisper_model$distances,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

species_variation <- individual_distances %>%
    group_by(Species) %>%
    summarise(
        mean_distance = mean(Distance_to_Centroid),
        median_distance = median(Distance_to_Centroid),
        sd_distance = sd(Distance_to_Centroid),
        n_samples = n()
    ) %>%
    ungroup()

# Create named vector for color mapping
variation_vector <- setNames(species_variation$mean_distance, species_variation$Species)
variation_vector_filtered <- variation_vector[matching_species]

# Create contMap for branch coloring
contmap_obj <- contMap(RAT_TREE_pruned, 
                       variation_vector_filtered,
                       plot = FALSE,
                       res = 200)
contmap_obj <- setMap(contmap_obj, colors = c("blue", "cyan", "yellow", "orange", "red"))

# Prepare tip traits for coloring
tip_traits <- data.frame(
    node = 1:Ntip(RAT_TREE_pruned),
    label = RAT_TREE_pruned$tip.label,
    trait_value = variation_vector_filtered[RAT_TREE_pruned$tip.label],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

min_var <- min(variation_vector_filtered)
max_var <- max(variation_vector_filtered)

# ===== PREPARE DIET AND SAMPLE SIZE DATA =====
sample_sizes <- sample_data(RAT) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    filter(!Tribe == "Hydromyini") %>%
    group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
    summarize(SampleSize = n()) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(label = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

df_summary <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
    group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
    summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(label = Sample) %>%
    left_join(sample_sizes, by = "label")

size_labels <- df_summary %>% 
    distinct(label, SampleSize)

# ===== PLOT WITH COLORED BRANCHES AND DIET BARS =====
# Create base tree with branch colors (variation)
p <- ggtree(RAT_TREE_pruned, linewidth = 0.8) %<+% tip_traits +
    aes(color = trait_value) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
        colors = plasma(5),
        name = "Mean Distance\nto Centroid\n(interindividual variation)",
        limits = c(min_var, max_var)
    ) +
    geom_tiplab(
        align = TRUE,
        linetype = "dotted",
        linewidth = 0.5,
        fontface = 3,
        offset = 0.001,
        size = 14 / .pt,
        color = "black"
    ) +
    theme_tree2() +
    theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        text = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    xlim(0, 0.06) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 0.06, by = 0.015))

# Add ancestral state nodes (colored by dominant diet)
diet_colors <- c(
    "Animal" = "#f8766d",
    "Plant" = "#00ba38",
    "Fungi" = "#619cff"
)

# Prepare node data with proper node IDs for ggtree
node_data$node <- (Ntip(RAT_TREE_pruned) + 1):(Ntip(RAT_TREE_pruned) + nrow(node_data))

p <- p %<+% node_data +
    geom_nodepoint(
        aes(fill = dominant_diet),
        shape = 21,
        size = 4,
        color = "black",
        stroke = 0.5
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
        name = "Ancestral Diet",
        values = diet_colors,
        guide = guide_legend(order = 1)
    )

# Add diet relative abundance bar plot
p2 <- p +
    geom_fruit(
        data = df_summary,
        geom = geom_bar,
        mapping = aes(y = label, x = rel_abundance, fill = Diet),
        stat = "identity",
        orientation = "y",
        offset = 0.35,
        pwidth = 0.8,
        axis.params = list(
            axis = "x",
            text.size = 5,
            text.angle = 90,
            breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1),
            hjust = 1,
        )
    ) +
    # Add sample size labels
    geom_fruit(
        data = size_labels,
        geom = geom_text,
        mapping = aes(y = label, label = paste0("n=", SampleSize)),
        offset = 0.01,
        pwidth = 0.2,
        size = 5,
        hjust = 0
    ) +
    new_scale_fill() +
    scale_fill_manual(
        name = "Diet",
        values = diet_colors
    ) +
    theme(
        legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 14)
    )

# Display plot
p2




# ===============================================================================

########################## RAT HABITAT AND ALTITUDE
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtree)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")



## FIX THE NAs ON THE HABITATS
samples_to_fix <- c("MW200.022", "BAI.032", "BAI.036", "MW2200.070")
new_values <- c("synanthropic", "forest", "forest", "synanthropic")

for (i in seq_along(samples_to_fix)) {
sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)[samples_to_fix[i],
"Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda"] <- new_values[i]
}

sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)[samples_to_fix,"Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda"]

unique(sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)$Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda)


save(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")

# ==================================================================

# Use your phyloseq object
PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# Filter to Rattini tribe only
RAT <- prune_samples(!sample_data(PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT)$Tribe == "Hydromyini", PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT)
RAT <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(RAT) > 0, RAT)

# Convert sample_data to data.frame
sample_md <- as(sample_data(RAT), "data.frame")

# Desired order from top to bottom in tree
desired_order <- c(
  "Rattus verecundus",
  "Rattus steini",
  "Rattus niobe",
  "Rattus rattus",
  "Rattus exulans"
)

# Update metadata with forced factor order
sample_md <- sample_md %>%
  mutate(
    label = factor(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others,
                   levels = rev(desired_order)),  # reverse so ggplot matches ggtree top-down
    Habitat = factor(Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda),
    Metalokalita = factor(Metalokalita)          # for faceting
  )

# Define intuitive colors for habitats
habitat_colors <- c(
  "forest" = "#006400",       # dark green
  "grassland" = "#FFD700",    # gold
  "ecotone" = "#8B4513",      # brown
  "synanthropic" = "darkred"  # red
)

# Plot tree
p_tree <- ggtree(RAT_TREE_UPT, size = 0.8) +
  geom_tiplab(
    aes(label = label),
    align = TRUE,
    linetype = "dotted",
    linesize = 0.5,
    fontface = 3,
    offset = 0.001,
    size = 14 / .pt
  ) +
  theme_tree2() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    text = element_text(size = 14)
  ) +
  xlim(0, 0.07)

# Scatter plot with facets in one row
library(ggh4x)
manual_breaks <- c(0, 200, 700, 1200, 1700, 2200, 2700, 3200, 3700)

p_alt <- ggplot(sample_md, aes(x = as.numeric(Alt), y = label, color = Habitat)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0, height = 0.15, size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(desired_order)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = habitat_colors) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(x = "", y = NULL, color = "Habitat") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "cm")  # space between facets
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Metalokalita, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  ggh4x::facetted_pos_scales(
    x = list(
      Metalokalita == "Finisterres" ~ scale_x_continuous(breaks = manual_breaks, limits = c(0, 4000)),
      Metalokalita == "Mt_Wilhelm" ~ scale_x_continuous(breaks = manual_breaks, limits = c(0, 4000))
    )
  )


# Combine tree and faceted scatter plot
final_plot <- (p_tree | p_alt) +
    plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1), guides = "collect") &  # collect legends to far right
    theme(plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10),
          legend.position = "right")

# ----------------------------
# 6. Print final plot
# ----------------------------

print(final_plot)

##############################################################################
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------#
##############################################################################

######____HYDROMYINI ____####
#### prepare tree with rel abundance
# First calculate sample sizes from original data
sample_sizes_hydro <- sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  filter(Tribe == "Hydromyini") %>%  # Only Hydromyini samples
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarize(SampleSize = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(label = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)



# Merge samples by species
phyloseq_species <- merge_samples(HYDRO, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun=mean)

# Collapse to Diet level
phyloseq_diet <- tax_glom(phyloseq_species, taxrank = "Diet")

# Calculate relative abundance per species
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_diet, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Melt to long format
df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel)

# Clean and prepare data with sample sizes
df_summary <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
  summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(label = Sample) %>%
  left_join(sample_sizes_hydro, by = "label")  # Add sample size information

# Create a separate data frame for sample size labels
size_labels_hydro <- df_summary %>% 
  distinct(label, SampleSize)


# Plot base tree
p <- ggtree(HYDR_TREE_UPT, size= 0.8) +
  geom_tiplab(
    align = TRUE,
    linetype = "dotted",
    linesize = 0.5,
    fontface = 3,
    offset = 0.001,
    size = 14 / .pt  # Tip label font size
  ) +
  theme_tree2() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),  # X-axis tick labels
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14), # (if you add x-axis title later)
    text = element_text(size = 14)          # Global text setting fallback
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 0.04, by = 0.01))

# Add diet relative abundance bar plot
p2 <- p +
  geom_fruit(
    data = df_summary,
    geom = geom_bar,
    mapping = aes(y = label, x = rel_abundance, fill = Diet),
    stat = "identity",
    orientation = "y",
    offset = 0.5,  # Adjust as needed
    pwidth = 0.8,   # Adjust width of barplot
    axis.params = list(
      axis = "x",
      text.size = 5,
      text.angle = 90,
      breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1),
      hjust = 1,
    )
  ) +
  geom_fruit(
    data = size_labels_hydro,
    geom = geom_text,
    mapping = aes(y = label, label = paste0("n=", SampleSize)),
    offset = 0.01,
    pwidth = 0.2,
    size = 5,  # Font size for text labels (approx 12 pt)
    hjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Diet",
    values = c(
      "Animal" = "#f8766d",
      "Plant" = "#00ba38",
      "Fungi" = "#619cff"
    )
  )+
  # Add theme settings for legend AFTER geom_fruit and scale_fill_manual
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16), # Adjust size as needed for the title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14)   # Adjust size as needed for the elements
  )


p2

#save it 1600*2000

#save it transparent
p3=p2+theme(panel.background =element_rect(fill = "transparent",color = NA), plot.background =element_rect(fill = "transparent",color = NA), legend.background =element_rect(fill = "transparent",color = NA) )

ggsave("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/plots/trees/hyd_transparent.png", plot = p3, bg = "transparent", width = 16, height = 9, dpi = 300)

########################## HYDROMYINI interindividual and ancestral

# ----------------------------
# Hydromyini phylogeny + altitude scatter
# Tree tip labels colored by spatial niche
# Legends on far right
# ----------------------------

library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtree)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggh4x)

# ----------------------------
# 1. Load phyloseq object
# ----------------------------

PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# Filter to Hydromyini tribe only
HYDRO <- prune_samples(sample_data(PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT)$Tribe == "Hydromyini", PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT)
HYDRO <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(HYDRO) > 0, HYDRO)

# Convert sample_data to data.frame
sample_md <- as(sample_data(HYDRO), "data.frame")

# Desired order from top to bottom in Hydromyini tree
y_orders <- c(
    "Paramelomys rubex_HighMW","Paramelomys rubex_HighFR","Paramelomys rubex_MidMW",
    "Paramelomys rubex_MidFR","Paramelomys platyopsFR","Paramelomys platyopsBAI",
    "Paramelomys platyopsWG","Paramelomys platyopsMW","Melomys low","Melomys midFR",
    "Protochromys fellowsi","Uromys cf_caudimaculatus","Uromys sp1","Uromys anak",
    "Microhydromys richardsoni","Microhydromys argenteus","Pseudohydromys fuscus",
    "Pseudohydromys ellermani","Leptomys ernstmayri","Hydromys sp",
    "Mallomys cf_aroaensis","Mallomys hercules","Mallomys rothschildi","Mallomys istapantap",
    "Abeomelomys spMW","Coccymys shawmayeri","Pogonomys spNorthEastMW","Pogonomys spNorthEastFR",
    "Pogonomys cf_sylvestris","Pogonomys loriae","Hyomys goliath","Macruromys major",
    "Lorentzimys highMW","Lorentzimys highFR","Lorentzimys midMW","Lorentzimys lowMW",
    "Lorentzimys lowFR","Anisomys imitatorMW","Anisomys imitatorFR"
)

# ----------------------------
# 2. Clean and prep metadata
# ----------------------------

sample_md <- sample_md %>%
    filter(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others %in% y_orders) %>%
    mutate(
        Alt = as.numeric(as.character(Alt)),
        Metalokalita = factor(trimws(as.character(Metalokalita)),
                              levels = c("Baitabag", "Wanang", "Finisterres", "Mt_Wilhelm")),
        label = factor(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others,
                       levels = rev(y_orders)),
        Habitat = factor(Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda),
        spatial_niche = factor(spatial_niche, levels = c("terrestrial", "scansorial"))
    )

# Define colors
habitat_colors <- c(
    "forest" = "#006400", "grassland" = "#FFD700",
    "ecotone" = "#8B4513", "synanthropic" = "darkred"
)
niche_colors <- c("terrestrial" = "#5c2010", "scansorial" = "#198f0e")

# ----------------------------
# 3. Plot tree with tip labels colored by spatial niche
# ----------------------------

tip_colors <- sample_md %>%
    select(label, spatial_niche)

p_tree <- ggtree(HYDR_TREE_UPT, size = 0.8) %<+% tip_colors +
    geom_tiplab(aes(label = label, color = spatial_niche),
                align = TRUE, linetype = "dotted", linesize = 0.5,
                fontface = 3, offset = 0.001, size = 14 / .pt) +
    scale_color_manual(name = "Spatial niche", values = niche_colors, na.value = "grey50") +
    theme_tree2() +
    theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        text = element_text(size = 14)
    ) +
    xlim(0, 0.07)

# ----------------------------
# 4. Scatter plot: Altitude per Metalokalita
# ----------------------------
manual_breaks <- c(0, 200, 700, 1200, 1700, 2200, 2700, 3200, 3700)

p_alt <- ggplot(sample_md, aes(x = Alt, y = label, color = Habitat)) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0, height = 0.15, size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(y_orders)) +
    scale_color_manual(name = "Habitat", values = habitat_colors, na.value = "grey50") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    labs(x = "", y = NULL) +
    theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "cm")
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ Metalokalita, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
    ggh4x::facetted_pos_scales(
        x = list(
            Metalokalita == "Finisterres" ~ scale_x_continuous(breaks = manual_breaks, limits = c(0, 4000)),
            Metalokalita == "Mt_Wilhelm" ~ scale_x_continuous(breaks = manual_breaks, limits = c(0, 4000))
        )
    )

# ----------------------------
# 5. Combine tree and scatter plot with unified legends
# ----------------------------

final_plot <- (p_tree | p_alt) +
    plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1), guides = "collect") &  # collect legends to far right
    theme(plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10),
          legend.position = "right")

# ----------------------------
# 6. Print final plot
# ----------------------------

print(final_plot)


# ============================================================================ #
# ========== HYDROMYINI INTERINDIVIDUAL VARIANCE TREE + DIET REL ABUND ======= #
# ============================================================================ #

library(ape)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggtree)
library(dplyr)
library(ggtreeExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(phytools)
library(tidytree)
library(tibble)
library(ggnewscale)

rm(list = ls())

# ===== LOAD DATA =====
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")

# ===== SUBSET TO HYDROMYINI =====
HYDRO <- prune_samples(
  sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean)$Tribe == "Hydromyini",
  PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean
)
HYDRO <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(HYDRO) > 0, HYDRO)

# ===== LOAD AND PREPARE TREE =====
HYDR_TREE_UPT <- ape::read.nexus("D:/Rwd/papuang/ddRAD_trees/new_trees_19.11.2024/figtree_hyd2.nex")
HYDR_TREE_UPT$tip.label <- gsub("'", "", HYDR_TREE_UPT$tip.label)
HYDR_TREE_UPT$tip.label <- gsub("\\.", " ", HYDR_TREE_UPT$tip.label)
HYDR_TREE_UPT$tip.label <- c(
  "Paramelomys platyopsBAI", "Paramelomys platyopsFR", "Paramelomys platyopsWG", "Paramelomys platyopsMW",
  "Paramelomys rubex_MidFR", "Paramelomys rubex_MidMW", "Paramelomys rubex_HighFR", "Paramelomys rubex_HighMW",
  "Melomys midFR", "Melomys low", "Protochromys fellowsi", "Uromys sp1", "Uromys cf_caudimaculatus", "Uromys anak",
  "Pseudohydromys ellermani", "Pseudohydromys fuscus", "Microhydromys argenteus", "Microhydromys richardsoni",
  "Leptomys ernstmayri", "Hydromys sp", "Mallomys hercules", "Mallomys cf_aroaensis", "Mallomys rothschildi",
  "Mallomys istapantap", "Abeomelomys spMW", "Coccymys shawmayeri", "Pogonomys spNorthEastFR", "Pogonomys spNorthEastMW",
  "Pogonomys cf_sylvestris", "Pogonomys loriae", "Hyomys goliath", "Macruromys major", "Lorentzimys highFR",
  "Lorentzimys highMW", "Lorentzimys midMW", "Lorentzimys lowFR", "Lorentzimys lowMW", "Anisomys imitatorFR",
  "Anisomys imitatorMW"
)
HYDR_TREE_UPT$root.edge <- 0.5

# ===== CALCULATE INTERINDIVIDUAL VARIATION =====
# Aggregate at Order (same as script1)
HYDRO_Order <- tax_glom(HYDRO, taxrank = "Order")

# Weighted UniFrac distances
wunifrac_dist <- phyloseq::distance(HYDRO_Order, method = "wunifrac")

species_vector <- sample_data(HYDRO_Order)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others

# betadisper
betadisper_model <- betadisper(wunifrac_dist, species_vector)

individual_distances <- data.frame(
  Sample_ID = names(betadisper_model$distances),
  Species = betadisper_model$group,
  Distance_to_Centroid = betadisper_model$distances,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# mean distance per species and sample counts
species_variation <- individual_distances %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_distance = mean(Distance_to_Centroid),
    median_distance = median(Distance_to_Centroid),
    sd_distance = sd(Distance_to_Centroid),
    n_samples = n()
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ===== PREPARE TRAIT VECTOR AND PRUNE TREE TO MATCH =====
variation_vector <- setNames(species_variation$mean_distance, species_variation$Species)
matching_species <- intersect(HYDR_TREE_UPT$tip.label, names(variation_vector))
variation_vector_filtered <- variation_vector[matching_species]
HYDR_TREE_pruned <- keep.tip(HYDR_TREE_UPT, matching_species)

# ===== PAGEL'S LAMBDA: run only for species with >=5 samples =====
# Determine species with enough samples
species_enough <- species_variation %>% filter(n_samples >= 5) %>% pull(Species)
# Keep only those in the pruned tree
species_enough_in_tree <- intersect(HYDR_TREE_pruned$tip.label, species_enough)

if(length(species_enough_in_tree) >= 3) { # phylosig needs enough tips
  tree_for_lambda <- keep.tip(HYDR_TREE_pruned, species_enough_in_tree)
  vec_for_lambda <- variation_vector_filtered[species_enough_in_tree]
  lambda_result <- phylosig(tree_for_lambda, vec_for_lambda, method = "lambda", test = TRUE)
  cat("\n===== Pagel's Lambda (species with >=5 samples) =====\n")
  cat("Lambda:", round(lambda_result$lambda, 3), "\n")
  cat("P-value:", round(lambda_result$P, 4), "\n\n")
} else {
  cat("\n===== Pagel's Lambda =====\n")
  cat("Not enough species with >=5 samples to compute Pagel's lambda (need >=3).\n\n")
}

# ===== CREATE tip_traits: set trait_value = NA for species with <5 samples =====
tip_traits <- data.frame(
  node = 1:Ntip(HYDR_TREE_pruned),
  label = HYDR_TREE_pruned$tip.label,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Join mean_distance and sample size
tip_traits <- tip_traits %>%
  left_join(
    species_variation %>% select(Species, mean_distance, n_samples),
    by = c("label" = "Species")
  )

# Set trait_value to NA if n_samples < 5 (so they will be plotted black using na.value)
tip_traits <- tip_traits %>%
  mutate(trait_value_plot = ifelse(!is.na(n_samples) & n_samples >= 5, mean_distance, NA_real_))

# Compute min/max over non-NA values for gradient limits
if(all(is.na(tip_traits$trait_value_plot))) {
  stop("All species have <5 samples or no trait values available: cannot build gradient scale.")
}
min_var <- min(tip_traits$trait_value_plot, na.rm = TRUE)
max_var <- max(tip_traits$trait_value_plot, na.rm = TRUE)

# ===== DIET RELATIVE ABUNDANCE (unchanged from your script2) =====
sample_sizes_hydro <- sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  filter(Tribe == "Hydromyini") %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarize(SampleSize = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(label = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

phyloseq_species <- merge_samples(HYDRO, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun = mean)
phyloseq_diet <- tax_glom(phyloseq_species, taxrank = "Diet")
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_diet, function(x) x / sum(x))
df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel)

df_summary <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
  summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(label = Sample) %>%
  left_join(sample_sizes_hydro, by = "label")

size_labels_hydro <- df_summary %>%
  distinct(label, SampleSize)

# ===== PLOT TREE: color mapped to numeric trait_value_plot; na.value = "black" =====
# Note: using scale_color_gradientn with na.value = "black" gives a continuous colorbar and black for NA tips/branches
p <- ggtree(HYDR_TREE_pruned, linewidth = 0.8) %<+% tip_traits +
  aes(color = trait_value_plot) +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colors = c("blue", "cyan", "yellow", "orange", "red"),
    name = "Mean Distance\nto Centroid\n(wunifrac + betadisper)",
    limits = c(min_var, max_var),
    na.value = "black"
  ) +
  geom_tiplab(
    align = TRUE,
    linetype = "dotted",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    fontface = 3,
    offset = 0.001,
    size = 14 / .pt,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  theme_tree2() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  # Do not force xlim; let ggtree decide to avoid clipping internal branches
  scale_x_continuous() 

# Add diet bars and sample size labels (same layout as before)
p2 <- p +
  geom_fruit(
    data = df_summary,
    geom = geom_bar,
    mapping = aes(y = label, x = rel_abundance, fill = Diet),
    stat = "identity",
    orientation = "y",
    offset = 0.35,
    pwidth = 0.8,
    axis.params = list(
      axis = "x",
      text.size = 5,
      text.angle = 90,
      breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1),
      hjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  geom_fruit(
    data = size_labels_hydro,
    geom = geom_text,
    mapping = aes(y = label, label = paste0("n=", SampleSize)),
    offset = 0.01,
    pwidth = 0.2,
    size = 5,
    hjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Diet",
    values = c(
      "Animal" = "#f8766d",
      "Plant" = "#00ba38",
      "Fungi" = "#619cff"
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14)
  )

# ===== DISPLAY FINAL PLOT =====
p2


###### ============================================= ########
# ==================== MERGED CONTRMAP =============== #
##############################################################
library(ape)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggtree)
library(dplyr)
library(vegan)
library(phytools)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidytree)

# ===== LOAD DATA =====
load("D:/Rwd/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# ===== CALCULATE INTERINDIVIDUAL VARIATION (ALL SPECIES) =====
PNG_18S_Order <- tax_glom(PS, taxrank = "Order")
wunifrac_dist <- phyloseq::distance(PNG_18S_Order, method = "wunifrac")
species_vector <- sample_data(PNG_18S_Order)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others

betadisper_model <- betadisper(wunifrac_dist, species_vector)

individual_distances <- data.frame(
  Sample_ID = names(betadisper_model$distances),
  Species = betadisper_model$group,
  Distance_to_Centroid = betadisper_model$distances,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

species_variation <- individual_distances %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_distance = mean(Distance_to_Centroid),
    median_distance = median(Distance_to_Centroid),
    sd_distance = sd(Distance_to_Centroid),
    n_samples = n()
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

species_variation_filtered <- species_variation %>%
  filter(n_samples >= 5)

cat("Species with >=5 samples:", nrow(species_variation_filtered), "\n")

# ===== CREATE DIVISIONS DATA =====
sample_data(PS)$Division <- sample_data(PS)$Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group

sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division %in% c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group "),
  "Rattus invasive",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division == "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) ",
  "Rattus endemic",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

species_divisions <- sample_data(PS) %>%
  as("data.frame") %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarize(Division = first(Division)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(Species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

division_colors <- c(
  "Coccymys division" = "#E60000",
  "Hydromys division" = "#004D99",
  "Mallomys division" = "#33CC33",
  "Pogonomys division" = "#FFCC00",
  "Rattus endemic" = "#660066",
  "Rattus invasive" = "#FF6600",
  "Uromys division" = "#00CCCC"
)

# ===== LOAD AND PREPARE TREE =====
host_tree_raw <- ape::read.tree("D:/Rwd/abdomen/Joined_scaled_RH85.nwk")
host_tree_raw$tip.label <- sub("_.*$", "", host_tree_raw$tip.label)

ps_sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(PS))
ps_sample_data$SampleID <- rownames(ps_sample_data)

tree_tip_species <- sapply(host_tree_raw$tip.label, function(tip) {
  species <- ps_sample_data[ps_sample_data$SampleID == tip, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"]
  if(length(species) > 0) return(as.character(species[1]))
  return(NA)
})

tips_to_keep <- host_tree_raw$tip.label[!is.na(tree_tip_species)]
host_tree_matched <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_raw, tips_to_keep)
kept_species <- tree_tip_species[!is.na(tree_tip_species)]

unique_species <- unique(kept_species)
representative_tips <- sapply(unique_species, function(sp) {
  tips_to_keep[kept_species == sp][1]
})

tree_niche <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_matched, representative_tips)
species_for_tips <- sapply(tree_niche$tip.label, function(tip) {
  sp <- ps_sample_data[ps_sample_data$SampleID == tip, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"]
  if(length(sp) > 0) return(as.character(sp[1]))
  return(NA)
})
tree_niche$tip.label <- species_for_tips

if(!is.rooted(tree_niche)) {
  tree_niche <- root(tree_niche, outgroup = tree_niche$tip.label[1], resolve.root = TRUE)
}

if(!is.binary(tree_niche)) {
  tree_niche <- multi2di(tree_niche)
}

# Make tree ultrametric and scale to 0-1
tree_niche <- chronos(tree_niche, lambda = 0, model = "relaxed")
tree_depth <- max(node.depth.edgelength(tree_niche))
tree_niche$edge.length <- tree_niche$edge.length / tree_depth

# ===== PREPARE DATA =====
variation_vector <- setNames(species_variation_filtered$mean_distance, 
                             species_variation_filtered$Species)

matching_species <- intersect(tree_niche$tip.label, names(variation_vector))
variation_vector_filtered <- variation_vector[matching_species]
tree_niche_pruned <- keep.tip(tree_niche, matching_species)

cat("Species in final tree:", length(matching_species), "\n\n")

# ===== ANCESTRAL STATE RECONSTRUCTION =====
# Use fastAnc to estimate ancestral states at internal nodes
ancestral_states <- fastAnc(tree_niche_pruned, variation_vector_filtered, vars = TRUE, CI = TRUE)

# Create dataframe with trait values at tips (current species)
tip_data <- data.frame(
  node = 1:length(tree_niche_pruned$tip.label),
  label = tree_niche_pruned$tip.label,
  trait = variation_vector_filtered[tree_niche_pruned$tip.label],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Create dataframe with estimated trait values at internal nodes (ancestors)
node_data <- data.frame(
  node = (length(tree_niche_pruned$tip.label) + 1):(length(tree_niche_pruned$tip.label) + tree_niche_pruned$Nnode),
  label = NA,
  trait = ancestral_states$ace,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Combine tip and node data
all_trait_data <- rbind(tip_data, node_data)

# ===== CALCULATE PAGEL'S LAMBDA =====
lambda_result <- phylosig(tree_niche_pruned, 
                          variation_vector_filtered,
                          method = "lambda",
                          test = TRUE)

cat("\n===== Pagel's Lambda for Niche Breadth =====\n")
cat("Lambda:", round(lambda_result$lambda, 3), "\n")
cat("P-value:", round(lambda_result$P, 4), "\n\n")

annotation_text <- paste0("λ = ", round(lambda_result$lambda, 2), 
                          ", p = ", ifelse(lambda_result$P < 0.001, "<0.001", round(lambda_result$P, 3)))

# ===== SCALE TREE TO BALANCE X/Y (like abdomen script) =====
p_tmp <- ggtree(tree_niche_pruned, layout = "rectangular")
tmp_df <- p_tmp$data
x_range <- max(tmp_df$x) - min(tmp_df$x)
y_range <- diff(range(tmp_df$y))
scale_factor <- if (x_range > 0) y_range / x_range else 1

tree_scaled <- tree_niche_pruned
tree_scaled$edge.length <- tree_scaled$edge.length * scale_factor

# ===== GET COORDINATES AND JOIN DATA =====
p_base <- ggtree(tree_scaled, layout = "rectangular")
tree_coords <- p_base$data

# Join trait data with tree coordinates
tree_data_full <- tree_coords %>%
  left_join(all_trait_data, by = "node") %>%
  left_join(species_divisions, by = c("label.x" = "Species"))

# Clean up column names
tree_data_full <- tree_data_full %>%
  mutate(label = ifelse(is.na(label.x), label.y, label.x)) %>%
  select(-label.x, -label.y)

# ===== CREATE DIVISION BACKGROUNDS =====
division_backgrounds <- tree_data_full %>%
  filter(isTip) %>%
  group_by(Division) %>%
  summarize(
    ymin = min(y) - 0.5,
    ymax = max(y) + 0.5,
    xmin = -0.05 * max(tree_coords$x),
    xmax = max(tree_coords$x) * 1.7
  )

division_labels <- division_backgrounds %>%
  mutate(
    y_center = (ymin + ymax) / 2,
    x_position = max(tree_coords$x) * 1.55
  )

# ===== CREATE THE PLOT WITH CONTINUOUS ANCESTRAL STATE COLORS =====
p <- ggtree(tree_scaled, layout = "rectangular", size = 0.8) %<+% 
  tree_data_full +
  aes(color = trait) +
  
  # Add colored backgrounds for divisions
  geom_rect(
    data = division_backgrounds,
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    fill = rep(division_colors[division_backgrounds$Division], length.out = nrow(division_backgrounds)),
    alpha = 0.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Add division labels
  geom_text(
    data = division_labels,
    aes(x = x_position, y = y_center, label = Division),
    size = 3.5,
    hjust = 0.3,
    fontface = "bold",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Dashed root branch
  geom_rootedge(rootedge = tree_scaled$edge.length[1] * 0.3, linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8, color = "black") +
  
# CRITICAL: Enable continuous color mapping along branches
geom_tree(continuous = 'colour', size = 1.2) +

# Color gradient - Inferno palette
scale_color_gradientn(
  colors = c("#000004", "#1B0C41", "#4B0C6B", "#781C6D", "#A52C60", 
             "#CF4446", "#ED6925", "#FB9A06", "#F7D03C", "#FCFFA4"),
  name = "Inter-individual\nvariation",
  na.value = "grey50",
  guide = guide_colorbar(
    barwidth = 10,
    barheight = 0.8,
    title.position = "top",
    title.hjust = 0.5
  )
)+
  
  # Tip labels with sample counts
  geom_tiplab(
    aes(label = paste0(label, " (n=", 
                       species_variation_filtered$n_samples[match(label, species_variation_filtered$Species)], 
                       ")")),
    size = 3.0,
    offset = 0.02,
    hjust = 0,
    fontface = "italic",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  # Lambda annotation
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02 * max(tree_coords$x),
    y = max(tree_coords$y) * 0.98,
    label = annotation_text,
    size = 4,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  xlim(0, max(tree_coords$x) * 1.8) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  
  ggtitle("Phylogenetic Signal in Niche Breadth") +
  
  theme_tree2() +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 15, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 100, 30, 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white")
  ) +
  
  # X-axis with 0-1 scale
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Time (scaled)",
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2) * scale_factor,
    labels = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.20))
  )

print(p)

# ===== SAVE =====
save(tree_niche_pruned, variation_vector_filtered, lambda_result,
     species_divisions, all_trait_data, ancestral_states, p,
     file = "D:/Rwd/contmap/niche_breadth_contmap.RData")

ggsave(
  filename = "D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/2-tree_plots/new_trees/contmap_new.png",
  plot     = p,
  width    = 18,  # Increased width to accommodate division labels
  height   = 9,
  dpi      = 300,
  bg       = "white",
  limitsize = FALSE
)

# Also save as PDF for publication
ggsave(
  filename = "D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/2-tree_plots/new_trees/contmap_new.pdf",
  plot     = p,
  width    = 18,
  height   = 9,
  device   = "pdf",
  limitsize = FALSE
)



# =================== ANCESTRAL RECONSTRUCTION FOR RAO'S Q ===================== #
library(ape)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggtree)
library(dplyr)
library(vegan)
library(phytools)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidytree)

# ===== LOAD DATA =====
load("D:/Rwd/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# ===== LOAD RAO'S Q DATA =====
alpha_div_final <- read.csv("D:/Rwd/alpha_glmm/tables/alpha_div_final_inter.csv")

# Calculate mean Rao's Q per species
species_raos_q <- alpha_div_final %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_raos_q = mean(RaoQ_order, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_raos_q = median(RaoQ_order, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_raos_q = sd(RaoQ_order, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_samples = n()
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(Species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

# Filter species with at least 5 samples
species_raos_q_filtered <- species_raos_q %>%
  filter(n_samples >= 5)

cat("Species with >=5 samples:", nrow(species_raos_q_filtered), "\n")

# ===== CREATE DIVISIONS DATA =====
sample_data(PS)$Division <- sample_data(PS)$Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group

sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division %in% c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group "),
  "Rattus invasive",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division == "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) ",
  "Rattus endemic",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

species_divisions <- sample_data(PS) %>%
  as("data.frame") %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarize(Division = first(Division)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(Species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

division_colors <- c(
  "Coccymys division" = "#E60000",
  "Hydromys division" = "#004D99",
  "Mallomys division" = "#33CC33",
  "Pogonomys division" = "#FFCC00",
  "Rattus endemic" = "#660066",
  "Rattus invasive" = "#FF6600",
  "Uromys division" = "#00CCCC"
)

# ===== LOAD AND PREPARE TREE =====
host_tree_raw <- ape::read.tree("D:/Rwd/abdomen/Joined_scaled_RH85.nwk")
host_tree_raw$tip.label <- sub("_.*$", "", host_tree_raw$tip.label)

ps_sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(PS))
ps_sample_data$SampleID <- rownames(ps_sample_data)

tree_tip_species <- sapply(host_tree_raw$tip.label, function(tip) {
  species <- ps_sample_data[ps_sample_data$SampleID == tip, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"]
  if(length(species) > 0) return(as.character(species[1]))
  return(NA)
})

tips_to_keep <- host_tree_raw$tip.label[!is.na(tree_tip_species)]
host_tree_matched <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_raw, tips_to_keep)
kept_species <- tree_tip_species[!is.na(tree_tip_species)]

unique_species <- unique(kept_species)
representative_tips <- sapply(unique_species, function(sp) {
  tips_to_keep[kept_species == sp][1]
})

tree_niche <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_matched, representative_tips)
species_for_tips <- sapply(tree_niche$tip.label, function(tip) {
  sp <- ps_sample_data[ps_sample_data$SampleID == tip, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"]
  if(length(sp) > 0) return(as.character(sp[1]))
  return(NA)
})
tree_niche$tip.label <- species_for_tips

if(!is.rooted(tree_niche)) {
  tree_niche <- root(tree_niche, outgroup = tree_niche$tip.label[1], resolve.root = TRUE)
}

if(!is.binary(tree_niche)) {
  tree_niche <- multi2di(tree_niche)
}

# ===== PREPARE RAO'S Q DATA (BEFORE MAKING TREE ULTRAMETRIC) =====
raos_q_vector <- setNames(species_raos_q_filtered$mean_raos_q, 
                          species_raos_q_filtered$Species)

# CRITICAL: Find matching species and prune tree BEFORE chronos
matching_species <- intersect(tree_niche$tip.label, names(raos_q_vector))
raos_q_vector_filtered <- raos_q_vector[matching_species]

# Prune the tree to matching species FIRST
tree_niche_pruned <- ape::keep.tip(tree_niche, matching_species)

cat("Species in final tree:", length(matching_species), "\n\n")

# NOW make tree ultrametric and scale to 0-1
tree_niche_pruned <- chronos(tree_niche_pruned, lambda = 0, model = "relaxed")
tree_depth <- max(node.depth.edgelength(tree_niche_pruned))
tree_niche_pruned$edge.length <- tree_niche_pruned$edge.length / tree_depth

# ===== ANCESTRAL STATE RECONSTRUCTION =====
# Use fastAnc to estimate ancestral states at internal nodes
ancestral_states <- fastAnc(tree_niche_pruned, raos_q_vector_filtered, vars = TRUE, CI = TRUE)

# Create dataframe with trait values at tips (current species)
tip_data <- data.frame(
  node = 1:length(tree_niche_pruned$tip.label),
  label = tree_niche_pruned$tip.label,
  trait = raos_q_vector_filtered[tree_niche_pruned$tip.label],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Create dataframe with estimated trait values at internal nodes (ancestors)
node_data <- data.frame(
  node = (length(tree_niche_pruned$tip.label) + 1):(length(tree_niche_pruned$tip.label) + tree_niche_pruned$Nnode),
  label = NA,
  trait = ancestral_states$ace,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Combine tip and node data
all_trait_data <- rbind(tip_data, node_data)

# ===== CALCULATE PAGEL'S LAMBDA =====
lambda_result <- phylosig(tree_niche_pruned, 
                          raos_q_vector_filtered,
                          method = "lambda",
                          test = TRUE)

cat("\n===== Pagel's Lambda for Rao's Q (Niche Breadth) =====\n")
cat("Lambda:", round(lambda_result$lambda, 3), "\n")
cat("P-value:", round(lambda_result$P, 4), "\n\n")

annotation_text <- paste0("λ = ", round(lambda_result$lambda, 2), 
                          ", p = ", ifelse(lambda_result$P < 0.001, "<0.001", round(lambda_result$P, 3)))

# ===== SCALE TREE TO BALANCE X/Y =====
p_tmp <- ggtree(tree_niche_pruned, layout = "rectangular")
tmp_df <- p_tmp$data
x_range <- max(tmp_df$x) - min(tmp_df$x)
y_range <- diff(range(tmp_df$y))
scale_factor <- if (x_range > 0) y_range / x_range else 1

tree_scaled <- tree_niche_pruned
tree_scaled$edge.length <- tree_scaled$edge.length * scale_factor

# ===== GET COORDINATES AND JOIN DATA =====
p_base <- ggtree(tree_scaled, layout = "rectangular")
tree_coords <- p_base$data

# Join trait data with tree coordinates
tree_data_full <- tree_coords %>%
  left_join(all_trait_data, by = "node") %>%
  left_join(species_divisions, by = c("label.x" = "Species"))

# Clean up column names
tree_data_full <- tree_data_full %>%
  mutate(label = ifelse(is.na(label.x), label.y, label.x)) %>%
  select(-label.x, -label.y)

# ===== CREATE DIVISION BACKGROUNDS =====
division_backgrounds <- tree_data_full %>%
  filter(isTip) %>%
  group_by(Division) %>%
  summarize(
    ymin = min(y) - 0.5,
    ymax = max(y) + 0.5,
    xmin = -0.05 * max(tree_coords$x),
    xmax = max(tree_coords$x) * 1.7
  )

division_labels <- division_backgrounds %>%
  mutate(
    y_center = (ymin + ymax) / 2,
    x_position = max(tree_coords$x) * 1.55
  )

# ===== CREATE THE PLOT WITH CONTINUOUS ANCESTRAL STATE COLORS =====
p <- ggtree(tree_scaled, layout = "rectangular", size = 0.8) %<+% 
  tree_data_full +
  aes(color = trait) +
  
  # Add colored backgrounds for divisions
  geom_rect(
    data = division_backgrounds,
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    fill = rep(division_colors[division_backgrounds$Division], length.out = nrow(division_backgrounds)),
    alpha = 0.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Add division labels
  geom_text(
    data = division_labels,
    aes(x = x_position, y = y_center, label = Division),
    size = 3.5,
    hjust = 0.3,
    fontface = "bold",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Dashed root branch
  geom_rootedge(rootedge = tree_scaled$edge.length[1] * 0.3, linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8, color = "black") +
  
  # CRITICAL: Enable continuous color mapping along branches
  geom_tree(continuous = 'colour', size = 1.2) +
  
  # Color gradient - Viridis palette (good for continuous data)
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    name = "Rao's Q\n(Phylogenetic Diversity)",
    option = "plasma",
    na.value = "grey50",
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      barwidth = 10,
      barheight = 0.8,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5
    )
  ) +
  
  # Tip labels with sample counts
  geom_tiplab(
    aes(label = paste0(label, " (n=", 
                       species_raos_q_filtered$n_samples[match(label, species_raos_q_filtered$Species)], 
                       ")")),
    size = 3.0,
    offset = 0.02,
    hjust = 0,
    fontface = "italic",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  # Lambda annotation
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02 * max(tree_coords$x),
    y = max(tree_coords$y) * 0.98,
    label = annotation_text,
    size = 4,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  xlim(0, max(tree_coords$x) * 1.8) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  
  ggtitle("Phylogenetic Signal in Niche Breadth (Rao's Q)") +
  
  theme_tree2() +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 15, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 100, 30, 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white")
  ) +
  
  # X-axis with 0-1 scale
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Time (scaled)",
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2) * scale_factor,
    labels = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.20))
  )

print(p)

ggsave(
  filename = "D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/2-tree_plots/new_trees/phylogenetic_signal_raos_q.png",
  plot     = p,
  width    = 18,  # Increased width to accommodate division labels
  height   = 9,
  dpi      = 300,
  bg       = "white",
  limitsize = FALSE
)


# ================================================================= #
# ===================== Rao vs distance to centroid =============== #
# ================================================================= #
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq) 
library(ggrepel)  

# Check for ggrepel and install if missing
if (!requireNamespace("ggrepel", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("ggrepel")
  library(ggrepel)
} else {
  library(ggrepel)
}

# ===== 1. LOAD DATA & DEFINE FILE PATHS (User must ensure these paths are correct) =====
load("D:/Rwd/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean
alpha_div_final <- read.csv("D:/Rwd/alpha_glmm/tables/alpha_div_final_inter.csv")

# ===== 2. CALCULATE SPECIES MEANS & FILTER =====
species_summary <- alpha_div_final %>%
    group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
    summarise(
        mean_raos_q = mean(RaoQ_order, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_mdc = mean(Distance_to_Centroid, na.rm = TRUE), 
        n_samples = n()
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(Species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

# Filter species with less than 5 samples
species_summary_filtered <- species_summary %>%
    filter(n_samples >= 5)

cat("Species with >=5 samples:", nrow(species_summary_filtered), "\n")

# ===== 3. DEFINE DIVISIONS AND COLORS =====
sample_data(PS)$Division <- sample_data(PS)$Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group
sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
    sample_data(PS)$Division %in% c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group "),
    "Rattus invasive",
    sample_data(PS)$Division
)
sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
    sample_data(PS)$Division == "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) ",
    "Rattus endemic",
    sample_data(PS)$Division
)

species_divisions <- sample_data(PS) %>%
    as("data.frame") %>%
    group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
    summarize(Division = first(Division)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(Species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

# Darkened color for Pogonomys division from #FFCC00 to #CC9900
division_colors <- c(
    "Coccymys division" = "#E60000", 
    "Hydromys division" = "#004D99", 
    "Mallomys division" = "#33CC33",
    "Pogonomys division" = "#CC9900", 
    "Rattus endemic" = "#660066", 
    "Rattus invasive" = "#FF6600",
    "Uromys division" = "#00CCCC"
)

# ===== 4. FINAL DATA PREPARATION (Joining) =====
plot_data <- species_summary_filtered %>%
    inner_join(species_divisions, by = "Species")

# ===== 5. CALCULATE QUADRANT THRESHOLDS (MEDIANS) =====
x_median <- median(plot_data$mean_mdc, na.rm = TRUE)
y_median <- median(plot_data$mean_raos_q, na.rm = TRUE)

# ===== 6. PLOTTING (Quadrant Niche Analysis) =====

p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = mean_mdc, y = mean_raos_q, color = Division)) +
    
    # Add points
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 3) +
    
    # === ADD QUADRANT LINES (Medians) ===
    geom_vline(xintercept = x_median, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = y_median, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.8) +
    
    # === ADD MEDIAN/CENTER ANNOTATION ===
    annotate("text", 
             x = x_median * 0.99, y = y_median * 0.99, 
             label = paste0("Median (X, Y): (", round(x_median, 3), ", ", round(y_median, 3), ")"), 
             hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1.2, size = 3.5, color = "gray20", fontface = "bold") +
    
    # === ADD SPECIES LABELS USING GGREPEL ===
    geom_text_repel(
        aes(label = Species), 
        size = 3.0, 
        fontface = "italic", 
        max.overlaps = 50,
        box.padding = 0.5, 
        point.padding = 0.5,
        segment.color = 'gray50'
    ) +
    
    # Set custom colors for divisions
    scale_color_manual(values = division_colors) +
    
    # === ADD QUADRANT LABELS ===
    annotate("text", x = min(plot_data$mean_mdc), y = max(plot_data$mean_raos_q), 
             label = "Population Generalists", hjust = 0, vjust = 1.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = max(plot_data$mean_mdc), y = max(plot_data$mean_raos_q), 
             label = "Opportunistics", hjust = 1, vjust = 1.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = min(plot_data$mean_mdc), y = min(plot_data$mean_raos_q), 
             label = "Specialists", hjust = 0, vjust = -1.3, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = max(plot_data$mean_mdc), y = min(plot_data$mean_raos_q), 
             label = "Individual Specialists", hjust = 1, vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +

    labs(
        x = "Mean Distance to Centroid (Interindividual Heterogeneity)",
        y = "Mean Rao's Q (Phylogenetic Diversity / Taxonomic Breadth)",
        color = "Host Division"
    ) +
    
    # Expand axes slightly to make room for labels
    expand_limits(
      x = c(min(plot_data$mean_mdc) * 0.95, max(plot_data$mean_mdc) * 1.05),
      y = c(min(plot_data$mean_raos_q) * 0.95, max(plot_data$mean_raos_q) * 1.05)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
    )

print(p)

# Save the plot
ggsave("D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/5-raw_alpha_values/RaoQ_vs_MDC_Quadrant_Niche_Analysis_Final.png", plot = p, width = 12, height = 9, units = "in", dpi = 300)

#save it as 1200*900 manually



# =================== MERGED SAMPLE DISTRIBUTION FIGURE ===================== #
# Load necessary libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats) 
library(tidyr) 

# Load your phyloseq object
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# Extract sample metadata as dataframe
sample_md <- as(sample_data(PHYLOSEQ_NEWMT), "data.frame")

# Define species order (used for X-axis order from Left to Right)
species_order_lookup_reversed <- c(
  "Rattus rattus", "Rattus exulans", "Rattus niobe", "Rattus steini", "Rattus verecundus",
  "Anisomys imitatorFR", "Anisomys imitatorMW", "Lorentzimys lowFR", "Lorentzimys lowMW",
  "Lorentzimys midMW", "Lorentzimys highFR", "Lorentzimys highMW", "Macruromys major",
  "Hyomys goliath", "Pogonomys loriae", "Pogonomys cf_sylvestris", "Pogonomys spNorthEastFR",
  "Pogonomys spNorthEastMW", "Coccymys shawmayeri", "Abeomelomys spMW",
  "Mallomys istapantap", "Mallomys rothschildi", "Mallomys hercules", "Mallomys cf_aroaensis",
  "Hydromys sp", "Leptomys ernstmayri", "Microhydromys argenteus", "Microhydromys richardsoni",
  "Pseudohydromys ellermani", "Pseudohydromys fuscus", "Uromys anak", "Uromys sp1",
  "Uromys cf_caudimaculatus", "Protochromys fellowsii", "Melomys low", "Melomys midFR",
  "Paramelomys platyopsMW", "Paramelomys platyopsWG", "Paramelomys platyopsBAI", "Paramelomys platyopsFR",
  "Paramelomys rubex_MidFR", "Paramelomys rubex_MidMW", "Paramelomys rubex_HighFR", "Paramelomys rubex_HighMW"
)

# Define colors and orders
habitat_colors <- c(
  "forest" = "darkgreen",    
  "grassland" = "yellow", 
  "ecotone" = "darkblue",  
  "synanthropic" = "darkred" 
)
# Define VIBRANT Division Colors
division_colors_palette <- c(
  "Coccymys division" = "#E60000",   
  "Hydromys division" = "#004D99",   
  "Mallomys division" = "#33CC33",   
  "Pogonomys division" = "#FFCC00",  
  "Rattus endemic" = "#660066",      
  "Rattus invasive" = "#FF6600",     
  "Uromys division" = "#00CCCC"      
)
custom_division_order <- names(division_colors_palette)


# --- 1. Prepare Data ---
species_divisions <- sample_md %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarize(Division = first(Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Division = case_when(
      Division %in% c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group ") ~ "Rattus invasive",
      Division == "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) " ~ "Rattus endemic",
      TRUE ~ as.character(Division)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, Division) %>%
  distinct()

plot_data_final <- sample_md %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others), 
         !is.na(Alt),                              
         !is.na(Metalokalita),
         !is.na(Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda)) %>% 
  left_join(species_divisions, by = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others") %>%
  mutate(
    Species = factor(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, levels = species_order_lookup_reversed),
    Mountain_Range = factor(Metalokalita),
    Habitat = factor(Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda),
    Division = factor(Division, levels = custom_division_order)
  ) %>%
  filter(Species %in% species_order_lookup_reversed)

overall_range_data <- plot_data_final %>%
  group_by(Species, Mountain_Range) %>%
  summarise(
    Min_Alt = min(Alt),
    Max_Alt = max(Alt),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

background_rect_data <- plot_data_final %>%
  select(Species, Division) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  group_by(Division) %>%
  summarise(
    xmin = min(as.numeric(Species)) - 0.5,
    xmax = max(as.numeric(Species)) + 0.5
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  tidyr::crossing(Mountain_Range = unique(plot_data_final$Mountain_Range)) %>%
  mutate(fill_color = division_colors_palette[as.character(Division)])

custom_y_breaks <- c(0, 700, 1200, 1700, 2200, 2700, 3200, 3700, 4200, 4500)
vline_positions <- seq(from = 1.5, 
                       to = length(species_order_lookup_reversed) - 0.5, 
                       by = 1)


# --- 2. Create the Final Plot ---
p_habitat_range <- ggplot(plot_data_final, aes(x = Species, y = Alt)) +
  
  # 1. Add background rectangles
  geom_rect(data = background_rect_data, 
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = Division),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, 
            alpha = 0.2) + 

  # Apply the division fill colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = division_colors_palette, name = "Taxonomic Division") +
  
  # 2. Add the OVERALL RANGE Line
  geom_segment(data = overall_range_data,
               aes(x = Species, xend = Species, y = Min_Alt, yend = Max_Alt),
               color = "black",
               size = 4, # Line thickness for range
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  
  # 3. Add Habitat-colored Points (Plot size = 4)
  geom_point(aes(color = Habitat), 
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0), 
             alpha = 0.8, 
             shape = 15, 
             size = 4) + # Point size for plot
  
  # 4. Add thin dashed vertical lines for species separation
  geom_vline(xintercept = vline_positions, 
             linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "gray60", 
             size = 0.3) + 
  
  # 5. Manually set Y-axis breaks
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = custom_y_breaks) + 
  
  # Apply habitat colors
  scale_color_manual(values = habitat_colors, name = "Habitat") +
  
  # Facet by Mountain Range (rows)
  facet_grid(Mountain_Range ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(x = "Species", y = "Elevation [masl]",
       title = "Species Altitude Range and Habitat Occurrence") +
  theme(
    # X-axis labels (Species)
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, face = "italic", size = 8, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), 
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    
    # Grid and axis cleanup
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray", linetype = "dotted"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"), 
    axis.line.x = element_blank(), 
    
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  
  # --- FINAL CHANGE: Override Point Size in the Legend ---
  guides(
    fill = "none", # Hide the Division background legend
    color = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(size = 10) # Set point size in Habitat legend to 10
    )
  )


# Print the plot
print(p_habitat_range)
```

*Indicspecies niche partitioning*
```{r indicspecies, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(indicspecies)
library(phyloseq)
library(ape)
library(ggtree)
library(ShortRead)
library(foreach)
library(doSNOW)

load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PHS<-PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean
taxa_names(PHS)<-paste0("ASC_",1:ntaxa(PHS))

# # FASTA<-refseq(PHS)
# # sum(duplicated(FASTA))
# 
# ###########################################
# #mothur alignment+ silva as a template#####
# ###########################################
# 
# writeFasta(FASTA,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/data/ASV.fasta")
# ~/software/mothur/mothur
# align.seqs(candidate=ASV.fasta, template=~/Downloads/silva.seed_v132/silva.seed_v132.align, processors=10)
# ~/software/iqtree3 -s ASV.align   -m MFP -B 1000 -redo
# 
# ####################
# #Clean alignment####
# ####################
# #some sequences are cleraly missaligned
# #these problematic sequences a more likely problems caused during PCR
# #now I will try to remove these problematic parts
# 
# 
# #1 remove position including 100% of gaps
# aln<-readDNAStringSet("~/data/PNG_diet/data/ASV.align")
# aln_mat <- as.matrix(aln)
# col_gap_threshold <- 1 
# aln_mat<-gsub("[.]","-",aln_mat)
# gap_freq_per_col <- colSums(aln_mat == "-") / nrow(aln_mat)
# cols_to_keep <- gap_freq_per_col != col_gap_threshold
# aln_filtered_cols <- aln_mat[, cols_to_keep, drop = FALSE]
# 
# #2 focus on position with low freqwuency of gaps (10%)
# gap_freq_per_col <- colSums(aln_filtered_cols == "-") / nrow(aln_filtered_cols)
# aln_filtered_cols.sub<-aln_filtered_cols[,gap_freq_per_col<0.1]
# 
# #3 calculate frequency of gaps for each ASV and remove gappy ASV (> 60%)
# ASV_gap_frq<-rowSums(aln_filtered_cols.sub == "-") / ncol(aln_filtered_cols.sub)
# hist(ASV_gap_frq)
# gappy_ASVS<-ASV_gap_frq<0.6
# aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs<-aln_filtered_cols[gappy_ASVS,]
# # dim(aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs)
# # View(aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs)
# 
# #4. remove common (100%) gaps from the final data
# GAPS<-colSums(aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs == "-") / nrow(aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs)
# aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs<-aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs[,GAPS!=1]
# CLEANED <- apply(aln_filtered_cols.bad.ASVs, 1, paste, collapse = "")
# ASV_cleaned.align <- DNAStringSet(CLEANED)
# writeFasta(ASV_cleaned.align,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/data/ASV_cleaned.align")
# 
# #phylogeny
# #~/software/iqtree3 -s ASV_cleaned.align   -m MFP -B 1000 -redo
# # ~/software/FastTree -nt -gtr ASV_cleaned.align > ASV_cleaned_fasttree.nwk
# 
# #############################################
# #########PHS with new phylogeny##############
# #############################################

FASTTREE<-read.tree("~/data/PNG_diet/data/ASV_cleaned_fasttree.nwk")
FASTTREE.tr<-FASTTREE
FASTTREE.tr$edge.length <- sqrt(FASTTREE.tr$edge.length)
ggtree(FASTTREE)
ggtree(FASTTREE.tr)
ggtree(phy_tree(PHS))

PHS.new<-merge_phyloseq(sample_data(PHS),
                        otu_table(PHS),
                        tax_table(PHS),
                        refseq(PHS),
                        FASTTREE)

#relatively moderate reduction of reads
plot(log10(sample_sums(PHS.new)),log10(sample_sums(PHS)))

# Sample for analyzes (6 and more sample)

#rarefy + transform to proportions

#select species with > 5 samples, rerefy and transform
sample_data(PHS.new)$Sp<-sample_data(PHS.new)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others
names(sample_data(PHS))
SUMM<-summary(as.factor(sample_data(PHS.new)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others))
SUMM<-SUMM[SUMM>5]
length(SUMM)
PHS.s<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.new)$Sp%in%names(SUMM),PHS.new)

PHS.s.sr<-rarefy_even_depth(PHS.s)
PHS.s.sr.t<-transform_sample_counts(PHS.s.sr,function(x) x/sum(x))
PHS.s.sr

# Phylogenetic distances

# cophenentic distances sq.rt transgformed - supress effect of long branches

# cophenetic distances
PD <- cophenetic(phy_tree(PHS.s.sr.t))
PD <- PD^0.5        # sq.rt transformation
PD <- PD/max(PD)    # rescale 0~1
PD <- as.dist(PD)

###
# Species list


Sp<-unique(sample_data(PHS.s.sr)$Sp)
# Sp<-sample(Sp)[1:4] #!!!!!!!!! for testing - comment in the final run 


# Species-level - niche breadth 
- complete dataset
- phylogenetic
- mode="single"



#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

NV.phylo<-NULL
NV.phylo <- foreach(i = 1:length(Sp),
                   .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                   .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD"),
                   .options.snow = opts,
                   .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
  
  print(paste("Processing",Sp[i],"Time:",Sys.time()))
  PHS.act<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
  DIET<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act))
  class(DIET)<-"data.frame"
  
  #NV.single[[i]]<-nichevar(P=DIET, mode="single",nboot=300)
  nichevar(P=DIET, mode="single",D=PD,nboot=100)
}
close(pb)            # Close the progress bar
stopCluster(cl)      # Stop the cluster

NV.phylo.df<-data.frame(NV.phylo,Sp)





# Species-level - niche breadth 
- complete dataset
- NONphylogenetic
- mode="single"

#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

NV.nphl<-NULL
NV.nphl <- foreach(i = 1:length(Sp),
                   .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                   .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD"),
                   .options.snow = opts,
                   .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
  
  print(paste("Processing",Sp[i],"Time:",Sys.time()))
  PHS.act<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
  DIET<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act))
  class(DIET)<-"data.frame"
 
  nichevar(P=DIET, mode="single",nboot=300)
  # nichevar(P=DIET, mode="single",D=PD,nboot=100)
}
close(pb)            # Close the progress bar
stopCluster(cl)      # Stop the cluster

NV.nphl.df<-data.frame(NV.nphl,Sp)


# Species-level - niche breadth (subsampled)
- complete dataset
- phylogenetic


ssmpl<-6 #subsampling
rsmpl<-100 # pocet resampling 

#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

NV.phylo.sub<-NULL
NV.phylo.sub <- foreach(i = 1:length(Sp),
                    .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                    .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD"),
                    .options.snow = opts,
                    .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
                      
                      print(paste("Processing",Sp[i],"Time:",Sys.time()))
                      PHS.act<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
                      
                      NICHEVAR<-as.numeric()
```
