```{r indicspecies, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
                      for(j in 1:rsmpl){
                        print(paste("cycle",j))
                        SN<-sample_names(PHS.act)
                        
                        SN.sub<-sample(SN)[1:ssmpl]
                        print(paste("Selected samples", SN.sub))
                        
                        PHS.sub<-prune_samples(sample_names(PHS.act)%in%SN.sub,PHS.act)
                        DIET<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.sub))
                        class(DIET)<-"data.frame"                       
                        NICHEVAR[j]<-as.numeric(nichevar(P=DIET, mode="single",D=PD,nboot=0)[1])
                      }
                      
                      NICHEVAR
                    }
close(pb)            # Close the progress bar
stopCluster(cl)      # Stop the cluster

rownames(NV.phylo.sub)<-Sp

- phylogenetic

# Interindividual level - diversity

- complete dataset
- phylogenetic
- mode="single"



#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

IN.phylo<-NULL
IN.phylo <- foreach(i = 1:length(Sp),
                   .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                   .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD"),
                   .options.snow = opts) %dopar% {
  
  print(paste("Processing",Sp[i],"Time:",Sys.time()))
  PHS.act<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
  DIET<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act))
  class(DIET)<-"data.frame"

  #nichevar(P=DIET, mode="single",nboot=300)
  nichevar(P=DIET, mode="multiple",D=PD,nboot=100)
}
close(pb)            # Close the progress bar
stopCluster(cl)      # Stop the cluster


names(IN.phylo)<-Sp

result <- sapply(IN.phylo, function(df) {
  mean(df[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
})




# Interindividual level - diversity

- complete dataset
- NONphylogenetic
- mode="single"



#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

IN.nphl<-NULL
IN.nphl <- foreach(i = 1:length(Sp),
                   .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                   .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD"),
                   .options.snow = opts) %dopar% {
  
  print(paste("Processing",Sp[i],"Time:",Sys.time()))
  PHS.act<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
  DIET<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act))

  nichevar(P=DIET, mode="multiple",nboot=300)

}
close(pb)            # Close the progress bar

  mean(df[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
})




# Niche overlaps - interspecific

- complete dataset
- phylogenetic


COMBN<-t(combn(Sp, 2))
#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

OV.phylo<-NULL
OV.phylo <- foreach(i = 1:dim(COMBN)[1],
                     .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                     .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD","COMBN"),
                     .options.snow = opts,
                     .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
                        
                       PHS.act.1<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,1],PHS.s.sr.t)
                       PHS.act.2<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,2],PHS.s.sr.t)
                       DIET.1<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.1))
                       DIET.2<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.2))
                       class(DIET.1)<-class(DIET.2)<-"data.frame"
                       
                       nicheoverlap(P1=DIET.1, P2=DIET.2, mode="single",D=PD,nboot=300)

                     }

close(pb)
stopCluster(cl)

OV.phylo.df<-data.frame(OV.phylo,COMBN)



# Niche overlaps - interspecific

- complete dataset
- NONphylogenetic



#combinations of species
COMBN<-t(combn(Sp, 2))

#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

OV.nphl<-NULL
OV.nphl <- foreach(i = 1:dim(COMBN)[1],
                     .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                     .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD","COMBN"),
                     .options.snow = opts,
                     .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
                        
                       PHS.act.1<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,1],PHS.s.sr.t)
                       PHS.act.2<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,2],PHS.s.sr.t)
                       DIET.1<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.1))
                       DIET.2<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.2))
                       class(DIET.1)<-class(DIET.2)<-"data.frame"
                       
                       nicheoverlap(P1=DIET.1, P2=DIET.2, mode="single",nboot=300)

                     }

close(pb)
stopCluster(cl)

OV.nphl.df<-data.frame(OV.nphl,COMBN)



# Niche overlaps - interspecific (resampling)

- complete dataset
- phylogenetic

#combinations of species
COMBN<-t(combn(Sp, 2))

ssmpl<-6 #subsampling
rsmpl<-100 # pocet resampling 


#combinations of species
COMBN<-t(combn(Sp, 2))

#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

OV.phylo.sub<-NULL
OV.phylo.sub <- foreach(i = 1:dim(COMBN)[1],
                    .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                    .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD","COMBN"),
                    .options.snow = opts,
                    .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
                      
                      OVLP<-as.numeric()
                      for(j in 1:rsmpl){
                      print(j)
                      PHS.act.1<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,1],PHS.s.sr.t)
                      PHS.act.2<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,2],PHS.s.sr.t)
                      
                      SN1<-sample_names(PHS.act.1)
                      SN2<-sample_names(PHS.act.2)
                      
                      SN1.sub<-sample(SN1)[1:ssmpl]
                      SN2.sub<-sample(SN2)[1:ssmpl]
                      
                      PHS.act.1.sub<-prune_samples(sample_names(PHS.act.1)%in%SN1.sub,PHS.act.1)
                      PHS.act.2.sub<-prune_samples(sample_names(PHS.act.2)%in%SN2.sub,PHS.act.2)
                      
                      DIET.1<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.1.sub))
                      DIET.2<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.2.sub))
                      
                      class(DIET.1)<-class(DIET.2)<-"data.frame"
                      
                      OVLP[j]<-as.numeric(nicheoverlap(P1=DIET.1, P2=DIET.2, mode="single",D=PD,nboot=0)[1])
                      }
                      OVLP
                    }

close(pb)
stopCluster(cl)

OV.phylo.sub.df<-data.frame(OV.phylo.sub,COMBN)


# Niche overlaps - interspecific (resampling)

- complete dataset
- NONphylogenetic

ssmpl<-6 #subsampling
rsmpl<-100 # pocet resampling 


#combinations of species
COMBN<-t(combn(Sp, 2))

#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)

OV.nphl.sub<-NULL
OV.nphl.sub <- foreach(i = 1:dim(COMBN)[1],
                    .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                    .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD","COMBN"),
                    .options.snow = opts,
                    .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
                      
                      OVLP<-as.numeric()
                      for(j in 1:rsmpl){
                      print(j)
                      PHS.act.1<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,1],PHS.s.sr.t)
                      PHS.act.2<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==COMBN[i,2],PHS.s.sr.t)
                      
                      SN1<-sample_names(PHS.act.1)
                      SN2<-sample_names(PHS.act.2)
                      
                      SN1.sub<-sample(SN1)[1:ssmpl]
                      SN2.sub<-sample(SN2)[1:ssmpl]
                      
                      PHS.act.1.sub<-prune_samples(sample_names(PHS.act.1)%in%SN1.sub,PHS.act.1)
                      PHS.act.2.sub<-prune_samples(sample_names(PHS.act.2)%in%SN2.sub,PHS.act.2)
                      
                      DIET.1<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.1.sub))
                      DIET.2<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.2.sub))
                      
                      class(DIET.1)<-class(DIET.2)<-"data.frame"
                      
                      OVLP[j]<-as.numeric(nicheoverlap(P1=DIET.1, P2=DIET.2, mode="single",nboot=0)[1])
                      }
                      OVLP
                    }

close(pb)

OV.nphl.sub<-data.frame(OV.nphl.sub,COMBN)

# Inche overlap within species

#combinations of species
COMBN<-t(combn(Sp, 2))

#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)


OVind.nphl<-NULL
OVind.nphl <- foreach(i = 1:length(Sp),
                   .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                   .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD"),
                   .options.snow = opts) %dopar% {
                     
                     PHS.act.1<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
                     DIET.1<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.1))
                     nicheoverlap(P1=DIET.1, mode="pairwise",nboot=300)
                     
                  }


close(pb)
stopCluster(cl)


# 
# 
# PHS.act.1<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
# DIET.1<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.1))
# nicheoverlap(P1=DIET.1, mode="pairwise",nboot=300)


# Inche overlap within species (phylogenetic)

#combinations of species
COMBN<-t(combn(Sp, 2))

#clusters
num_cores<-8
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoSNOW(cl)

# Initialize the Progress Bar
iterations <- length(Sp)
pb <- txtProgressBar(max = iterations, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) setTxtProgressBar(pb, n)
opts <- list(progress = progress)


OVind.phylo<-NULL
OVind.phylo <- foreach(i = 1:length(Sp),
                   .packages = c("indicspecies", "ape","phyloseq"),
                   .export = c("PHS.s.sr.t", "Sp", "PD"),
                   .options.snow = opts) %dopar% {
                     
                     PHS.act.1<-prune_samples(sample_data(PHS.s.sr.t)$Sp==Sp[i],PHS.s.sr.t)
                     DIET.1<-data.frame(otu_table(PHS.act.1))
                     nicheoverlap(P1=DIET.1, mode="pairwise",D=PD,nboot=300)
                     
                  }


close(pb)
stopCluster(cl)



#NV.phylo - niche breadth,species
#NV.nphl - niche breadth,species

#IN.phylo - niche breadth,individual
#IN.nphl - niche breadth,individual

#OV.phylo - niche overlap - interspecific
#OV.nphl - niche overlap - interspecific

#OVind.nphl - niche overlap - within-species, between individuals
#OVind.phylo - niche overlap - within-species, between individuals


#NV.phylo.sub - niche breadth,species (based on subsemples, without bootstrap)
#NV.nphl.sub - niche breadth,species (based on subsemples, without bootstrap)

#OV.phylo.sub - niche overlap species (based on subsemples, without bootstrap)
#OV.nphl.sub - niche overlap species (based on subsemples, without bootstrap)


#Save results
# Species - level niche width

write.table(NV.phylo.df,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/NV.phylo.df.txt",sep="\t",quote = F,row.names = F)
write.table(NV.nphl.df,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/NV.nphl.df.txt",sep="\t",quote = F,row.names = F)

# individual level niche breadth

save(IN.phylo,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/IN.phylo.R")
save(IN.nphl,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/IN.nphl.R")

# Niche overlap

write.table(OV.phylo.df,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/OV.phylo.df.txt",sep="\t",quote = F,row.names = F)
write.table(OV.nphl.df,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/OV.nphl.df.txt",sep="\t",quote = F,row.names = F)


# niche overlap - within-species, between individuals
save(OVind.phylo,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/OVind.phylo.R") # TOHLE NENI HOTOVE DODELAT
save(OVind.nphl,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/OVind.nphl.R")


# Species - level niche width (subsampling without bootstrap)
save(NV.phylo.sub,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/NV.phylo.sub.R")
save(NV.nphl.sub,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/NV.nphl.sub.R")

# OV.phylo.sub  - niche overlap -species level subsampling

write.table(OV.phylo.sub.df,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/OV.phylo.sub.df.txt",sep="\t",quote = F,row.names = F)
write.table(OV.nphl.sub.df,file = "~/data/PNG_diet/RESULTS/OV.nphl.sub.df.txt",sep="\t",quote = F,row.names = F)

# })


```

*Indicspecies niche partitioning virsualization*
```{r indicspecies_vir, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
################################################################################
# Indicspecies Phylogenetic Niche Partitioning — Visualization & Tables
#
# Based on results from indicspecies pipeline (indispecies.Rmd)
# Only phylogenetic results are used throughout.
#
# Variable mapping:
#   TNW           = NV.phylo.df$B       (species-level niche breadth, phylogenetic)
#   WIC           = mean(IN.phylo[[i]])  (mean individual-level niche breadth per species)
#   WIC/TNW       = WIC / TNW           (specialization index; 0=specialist, 1=generalist)
#   Inverse BIC   = mean pairwise within-species niche overlap from OVind.phylo
#                   (mean overlap among individuals; higher = less specialization =
#                    individuals more similar to each other = inverse of between-
#                    individual variation. Analogous to 1/BIC in Hill number framework)
#
# Outputs saved to: D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/figures_tables
################################################################################


# ==============================================================================
# 0. LIBRARIES
# ==============================================================================
library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ape)


# ==============================================================================
# 1. PATHS
# ==============================================================================

results_dir <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/RESULTS"
output_dir  <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/figures_tables"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)


# ==============================================================================
# 2. LOAD PHYLOSEQ (for sample_data and Division info)
# ==============================================================================

load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
ps <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))
class(sample_df) <- "data.frame"
#sample_df <- sample_df %>% rownames_to_column("sample_ID")

# Apply Division reclassification (same as iNEXT3D pipeline)
sample_df <- sample_df %>%
  mutate(Division = case_when(
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group %in%
      c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group ") ~ "Rattus invasive",
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group ==
      "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) "       ~ "Rattus endemic",
    TRUE ~ Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group
  ))

# Species -> Division lookup (one row per species)
sp_division <- sample_df %>%
  select(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, Division) %>%
  distinct()


# ==============================================================================
# 3. LOAD INDICSPECIES RESULTS (PHYLOGENETIC ONLY)
# ==============================================================================

# --- 3a. Species-level niche breadth (TNW) ---
NV.phylo.df <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, "NV.phylo.df.txt"))
# Columns: B (niche breadth = TNW), LL, UL, se, p.value, Sp
cat("Species-level niche breadth loaded:", nrow(NV.phylo.df), "species\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(colnames(NV.phylo.df), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# --- 3b. Individual-level niche breadth (WIC) ---
load(file.path(results_dir, "IN.phylo.R"))
# IN.phylo: named list, one element per species
# Each element is a data frame; first column = individual niche breadth values

IN.phylo.mean <- sapply(IN.phylo, function(df) {
  mean(df[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
})
cat("\nIndividual-level niche breadth computed for", length(IN.phylo.mean), "species\n")

# Confirm species order matches NV.phylo.df
if (!all(names(IN.phylo.mean) == NV.phylo.df$Sp)) {
  cat("WARNING: Species order mismatch between IN.phylo and NV.phylo.df — reordering.\n")
  IN.phylo.mean <- IN.phylo.mean[NV.phylo.df$Sp]
}

# --- 3c. Within-species pairwise niche overlap (Inverse BIC) ---
load(file.path(results_dir, "OVind.phylo.R"))
# OVind.phylo: named list, one element per species
# Each element is a pairwise overlap matrix among individuals

OVind.phylo.means <- sapply(OVind.phylo, function(mat) {
  mean(mat[lower.tri(mat, diag = FALSE)], na.rm = TRUE)
})
cat("Within-species overlap computed for", length(OVind.phylo.means), "species\n")

if (!all(names(OVind.phylo.means) == NV.phylo.df$Sp)) {
  cat("WARNING: Species order mismatch in OVind.phylo — reordering.\n")
  OVind.phylo.means <- OVind.phylo.means[NV.phylo.df$Sp]
}


# ==============================================================================
# 4. BUILD SPECIES-LEVEL SUMMARY TABLE
# ==============================================================================

species_summary <- data.frame(
  species     = NV.phylo.df$Sp,
  TNW         = NV.phylo.df$B,
  TNW_LC      = NV.phylo.df$LC,
  TNW_UC      = NV.phylo.df$UC,
  mean_WIC    = as.numeric(IN.phylo.mean),
  Inverse_BIC = as.numeric(OVind.phylo.means)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Spec_index = mean_WIC / TNW
  ) %>%
  left_join(sp_division, by = c("species" = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"))

cat("\nSpecies summary (n =", nrow(species_summary), "):\n")
print(species_summary %>% select(species, TNW, mean_WIC, Spec_index, Inverse_BIC, Division))


# ==============================================================================
# 5. BUILD INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL TABLE (sample_data + metrics)
# ==============================================================================

# Expand individual WIC values with sample IDs
ind_wic_list <- lapply(names(IN.phylo), function(sp) {
  df <- IN.phylo[[sp]]
  data.frame(
    sample_ID = rownames(df),
    species   = sp,
    WIC       = df[[1]],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})
ind_wic_df <- do.call(rbind, ind_wic_list)

# Merge sample_data with individual WIC and species-level metrics
individual_table <- sample_df %>%
  left_join(ind_wic_df, by = c("sample_ID", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others" = "species")) %>%
  left_join(
    species_summary %>% select(species, TNW, mean_WIC, Spec_index, Inverse_BIC),
    by = c("Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others" = "species")
  )

cat("\nIndividual table rows:", nrow(individual_table), "\n")

# Save table
write.csv(individual_table,
          file.path(output_dir, "individual_niche_table_phylo.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(species_summary,
          file.path(output_dir, "species_niche_summary_phylo.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("Tables saved to:", output_dir, "\n")


# ==============================================================================
# 6. COLORS AND THEME
# ==============================================================================

division_colors <- c(
  "Coccymys division"  = "#E60000",
  "Hydromys division"  = "#004D99",
  "Mallomys division"  = "#33CC33",
  "Pogonomys division" = "#FFCC00",
  "Rattus endemic"     = "#660066",
  "Rattus invasive"    = "#FF6600",
  "Uromys division"    = "#00CCCC"
)

base_theme <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y    = element_text(size = 7),
    strip.text.y   = element_text(size = 7, angle = 0),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title     = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle  = element_text(size = 8)
  )


# ==============================================================================
# 7. BARPLOTS — 4 FIGURES
# ==============================================================================

# Helper: horizontal barplot ordered by metric, faceted by Division
make_barplot <- function(data, x_var, x_label, title, subtitle,
                         vline = NULL) {
  p <- ggplot(data,
              aes(x = reorder(species, .data[[x_var]]),
                  y = .data[[x_var]],
                  fill = Division)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.7) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = division_colors) +
    facet_grid(Division ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
    labs(title = title, subtitle = subtitle,
         x = "Species", y = x_label) +
    base_theme
  if (!is.null(vline)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = vline, linetype = "dotted",
                        color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
  }
  p
}


# ---- Figure 1: TNW ----
p_tnw <- make_barplot(
  species_summary, "TNW",
  x_label  = "Total Niche Width (TNW)",
  title    = "Total Niche Width per species (phylogenetic)",
  subtitle = "Species-level niche breadth from indicspecies::nichevar(mode='single', D=PD)"
)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Fig1_TNW_barplot.png"),
       p_tnw, width = 10, height = 12, dpi = 300)
print(p_tnw)


# ---- Figure 2: Mean WIC ----
p_wic <- make_barplot(
  species_summary, "mean_WIC",
  x_label  = "Mean WIC (individual-level niche breadth)",
  title    = "Mean Within-Individual Component (WIC) per species (phylogenetic)",
  subtitle = "Mean individual niche breadth from indicspecies::nichevar() applied per individual"
)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Fig2_WIC_barplot.png"),
       p_wic, width = 10, height = 12, dpi = 300)
print(p_wic)


# ---- Figure 3: WIC/TNW specialization index ----
p_spec <- make_barplot(
  species_summary, "Spec_index",
  x_label  = "Specialization Index (WIC / TNW)",
  title    = "Individual Specialization Index per species (phylogenetic)",
  subtitle = "WIC/TNW: closer to 0 = specialist (individuals use small fraction of species niche)\ncloser to 1 = generalist (individuals use most of species niche)",
  vline    = 1
)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Fig3_SpecializationIndex_barplot.png"),
       p_spec, width = 10, height = 12, dpi = 300)
print(p_spec)


# ---- Figure 4: Inverse BIC (mean within-species pairwise overlap) ----
p_ibic <- make_barplot(
  species_summary, "Inverse_BIC",
  x_label  = "Inverse BIC (mean within-species pairwise niche overlap)",
  title    = "Inverse BIC per species (phylogenetic)",
  subtitle = "Mean pairwise niche overlap among individuals within species\nhigher = individuals more similar (less between-individual variation)"
)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Fig4_InverseBIC_barplot.png"),
       p_ibic, width = 10, height = 12, dpi = 300)
print(p_ibic)



### COMBINED FIGURE FOR SUPPLEMENTARY
# ==============================================================================
# COMBINED BARPLOT FOR TNW, mean_WIC, and Spec_index (ordered by division)
# ==============================================================================

library(patchwork)   # if not already loaded

# Define the order of divisions (customize as needed)
division_order <- c("Pogonomys division", "Uromys division", "Coccymys division",
                    "Hydromys division", "Mallomys division", "Rattus invasive",
                    "Rattus endemic")

# Create a new column with Division as a factor with the specified order
species_summary <- species_summary %>%
  mutate(Division = factor(Division, levels = division_order))

# For each division, order species by TNW (descending)
species_order <- species_summary %>%
  arrange(Division, desc(TNW)) %>%
  pull(species)

# Function to create a barplot with a fixed species order
make_combined_barplot <- function(data, x_var, x_label, fill_var = "Division") {
  p <- ggplot(data,
              aes(x = factor(species, levels = species_order),
                  y = .data[[x_var]],
                  fill = .data[[fill_var]])) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.7) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = division_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = x_label) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y    = element_text(size = 7, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.title.x   = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.margin    = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
    )
  return(p)
}

# Create individual plots
p_tnw_comb <- make_combined_barplot(species_summary, "TNW", "Total Niche Width (TNW)")
p_wic_comb <- make_combined_barplot(species_summary, "mean_WIC", "Mean WIC")
p_spec_comb <- make_combined_barplot(species_summary, "Spec_index", "Specialization Index")

# Remove y‑axis labels from the right two panels
p_wic_comb <- p_wic_comb + theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank())
p_spec_comb <- p_spec_comb + theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank())

# Combine with patchwork, collect legends, and place legend on the right
combined_plot <- (p_tnw_comb | p_wic_comb | p_spec_comb) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Niche Partitioning Metrics per Species",
    subtitle = "Species ordered by Division and within each division by Total Niche Width (TNW) | Bars coloured by Division"
  ) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Save combined figure
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Combined_Niche_Barplots.png"),
       combined_plot, width = 14, height = 12, dpi = 300)
cat("Combined barplot saved: Combined_Niche_Barplots.png\n")



# ==============================================================================
# 8. SCATTER PLOT: TNW vs mean WIC
# ==============================================================================

# Count individuals per species for point size
n_ind <- sample_df %>%
  count(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, name = "n_individuals")

species_summary <- species_summary %>%
  left_join(n_ind, by = c("species" = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"))

p_tnw_wic <- ggplot(species_summary,
                    aes(x = mean_WIC, y = TNW,
                        color = Division, size = n_individuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = species), size = 2.8,
                  max.overlaps = 20, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed",
              se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = mean_WIC, y = TNW)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = division_colors) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title    = "Niche breadth (species level) vs Diversity (individual level)",
       subtitle = "TNW = species-level niche breadth; WIC = mean individual niche breadth\nlog10 axes",
       x        = "Mean WIC — Individual-level diversity (log10)",
       y        = "TNW — Total Niche Width (log10)",
       color    = "Division", size = "N individuals") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8))

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Fig5_TNW_vs_WIC_scatter.png"),
       p_tnw_wic, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)
print(p_tnw_wic)


# ==============================================================================
# 9. SCATTER PLOT: TNW vs WIC/TNW (specialization index)
# ==============================================================================

p_tnw_spec <- ggplot(species_summary,
                     aes(x = TNW, y = Spec_index,
                         color = Division, size = n_individuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = species), size = 2.8,
                  max.overlaps = 20, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed",
              se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = TNW, y = Spec_index)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "gray50") +
  scale_color_manual(values = division_colors) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title    = "Niche breadth (species level) vs Individual Specialization",
       subtitle = "WIC/TNW: closer to 0 = specialist; closer to 1 = generalist\nTNW on log10 axis",
       x        = "TNW — Total Niche Width (log10)",
       y        = "Specialization Index (WIC / TNW)",
       color    = "Division", size = "N individuals") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8))

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Fig6_TNW_vs_SpecIndex_scatter.png"),
       p_tnw_spec, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)
print(p_tnw_spec)


# ==============================================================================
# 10. PCoA — PHYLOGENETIC NICHE OVERLAP
# ==============================================================================

# ==============================================================================
# 10. PCoA — PHYLOGENETIC NICHE OVERLAP WITH PIE CHART SPECIES POINTS
# ==============================================================================

OV <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, "OV.phylo.df.txt"))

# Mirror to symmetric matrix
symmetric_df <- bind_rows(
  OV %>% select(X1, X2, O),
  OV %>% select(X1 = X2, X2 = X1, O = O)
) %>%
  distinct(X1, X2, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Reshape to square matrix
square_matrix <- symmetric_df %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = X1, values_from = O) %>%
  column_to_rownames("X2") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Align rows and columns
all_species   <- sort(union(symmetric_df$X1, symmetric_df$X2))
square_matrix <- square_matrix[all_species, all_species]
diag(square_matrix) <- 1

# Distance matrix (sqrt-transformed as in Jakub's script)
DIST <- 1 - square_matrix
PCOA <- pcoa(DIST^0.5)

DD <- data.frame(PCOA$vectors) %>%
  rownames_to_column("species") %>%
  left_join(species_summary %>% select(species, Division, n_individuals, TNW),
            by = "species")

pct_var <- round(PCOA$values$Relative_eig[1:2] * 100, 1)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build diet composition per species for pie charts
# (same logic as nmds_piecharts.txt)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Load phyloseq and aggregate to Diet level
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS_pie <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean
PS_pie <- tax_glom(PS_pie, taxrank = "Diet")

phyloseq_species     <- merge_samples(PS_pie, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun = mean)
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_species, function(x) x / sum(x))

diet_df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
  summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  rename(species = Sample) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Diet, values_from = rel_abundance, values_fill = 0)

# Ensure all three diet columns exist
for (d in c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")) {
  if (!d %in% colnames(diet_df)) diet_df[[d]] <- 0
}

# Normalize to sum to 1
diet_df <- diet_df %>%
  mutate(total = Animal + Plant + Fungi,
         Animal = Animal / total,
         Plant  = Plant  / total,
         Fungi  = Fungi  / total) %>%
  select(species, Animal, Plant, Fungi)

# Merge diet composition into PCoA coordinates
DD <- DD %>% left_join(diet_df, by = "species")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build pie slice polygons
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

create_pie_slices <- function(data, x_col, y_col, diets, radius, n_points = 30) {
  pie_list <- list()
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(data))) {
    xc   <- data[[x_col]][i]
    yc   <- data[[y_col]][i]
    sp   <- data$species[i]
    props <- as.numeric(data[i, diets])
    props[is.na(props)] <- 0
    props[props < 0.01] <- 0
    if (sum(props) == 0) next
    props <- props / sum(props)
    cum   <- c(0, cumsum(props))
    for (j in seq_along(diets)) {
      if (props[j] == 0) next
      angles <- seq(2 * pi * cum[j], 2 * pi * cum[j + 1], length.out = n_points)
      pie_list[[length(pie_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        species = sp,
        Diet    = diets[j],
        x       = c(xc, xc + radius * cos(angles)),
        y       = c(yc, yc + radius * sin(angles)),
        xc      = xc,
        yc      = yc
      )
    }
  }
  bind_rows(pie_list)
}

# Set radius relative to PCoA axis range
pcoa_range <- diff(range(DD$Axis.1))
pie_radius <- pcoa_range * 0.015

pie_data <- create_pie_slices(
  data     = DD,
  x_col    = "Axis.1",
  y_col    = "Axis.2",
  diets    = c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi"),
  radius   = pie_radius,
  n_points = 200        # was 30
)

diet_colors <- c(
  "Animal" = "#E31A1C",
  "Plant"  = "#33A02C",
  "Fungi"  = "#1F78B4"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

p_pcoa <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = pie_data,
    aes(x = x, y = y, fill = Diet, group = interaction(species, Diet)),
    color = NA, alpha = 0.9
  ) +
geom_point(
  data = DD,
  aes(x = Axis.1, y = Axis.2),
  shape  = 1,        # hollow circle
  size   = pie_radius * 300,   # tune this multiplier to match your pie size
  color  = "black",
  stroke = 0.4
) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = DD,
    aes(x = Axis.1, y = Axis.2, label = species),
    size              = 2.6,
    max.overlaps      = 20,
    box.padding       = 1.2,
    point.padding     = pie_radius * 150,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    segment.size      = 0.3,
    segment.color     = "grey40",
    seed              = 42
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = diet_colors, name = "Diet") +
  labs(
    title    = "PCoA — Phylogenetic niche overlap between species",
    subtitle = sprintf("Distance = 1 - overlap (sqrt-transformed) | Pie charts = diet composition"),
    x        = sprintf("PCoA Axis 1 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[1]),
    y        = sprintf("PCoA Axis 2 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[2])
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  coord_fixed()

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Fig7_PCoA_niche_overlap.png"),
       p_pcoa, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
print(p_pcoa)


################################################################################
# Non-Phylogenetic PCoA — Niche Overlap with Diet Pie Charts
#
#
# Inputs (all from results_dir):
#   OV.nphl.df.txt   — pairwise non-phylogenetic niche overlap from indicspecies
#
# Also requires:
#   PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R  (phyloseq object)
#   species_niche_summary_phylo.csv  (species summary with Division column)
#
# Output:
#   PCoA_nonphylogenetic_piecharts.png / .pdf
################################################################################


# ==============================================================================
# 0. LIBRARIES
# ==============================================================================

library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ape)


# ==============================================================================
# 1. PATHS  —  adjust to your machine
# ==============================================================================

results_dir <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/RESULTS"
output_dir  <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/figures_tables"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

phyloseq_path <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R"
summary_path  <- file.path(output_dir, "species_niche_summary_phylo.csv")


# ==============================================================================
# 2. LOAD PHYLOSEQ  (for Division labels and diet composition)
# ==============================================================================

load(phyloseq_path)
ps <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))
class(sample_df) <- "data.frame"

# Division reclassification — identical to main script
sample_df <- sample_df %>%
  mutate(Division = case_when(
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group %in%
      c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group ")        ~ "Rattus invasive",
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group ==
      "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) "             ~ "Rattus endemic",
    TRUE ~ Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group
  ))

sp_division <- sample_df %>%
  select(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, Division) %>%
  distinct()

n_ind <- sample_df %>%
  count(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, name = "n_individuals")


# ==============================================================================
# 3. LOAD SPECIES SUMMARY  (for Division and n_individuals per species)
# ==============================================================================

species_summary <- read.csv(summary_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

if (!"Division" %in% colnames(species_summary)) {
  species_summary <- species_summary %>%
    left_join(sp_division, by = c("species" = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"))
}
if (!"n_individuals" %in% colnames(species_summary)) {
  species_summary <- species_summary %>%
    left_join(n_ind, by = c("species" = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"))
}


# ==============================================================================
# 4. LOAD NON-PHYLOGENETIC NICHE OVERLAP  &  BUILD DISTANCE MATRIX
# ==============================================================================

OV <- read.delim(file.path(results_dir, "OV.nphl.df.txt"))

# Mirror to symmetric long-format
symmetric_df <- bind_rows(
  OV %>% select(X1, X2, O),
  OV %>% select(X1 = X2, X2 = X1, O = O)
) %>%
  distinct(X1, X2, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Reshape to square matrix
square_matrix <- symmetric_df %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = X1, values_from = O) %>%
  column_to_rownames("X2") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Align rows and columns
all_species   <- sort(union(symmetric_df$X1, symmetric_df$X2))
square_matrix <- square_matrix[all_species, all_species]
diag(square_matrix) <- 1

# Distance matrix (sqrt-transformed, identical to phylogenetic PCoA)
DIST <- 1 - square_matrix
PCOA <- pcoa(DIST^0.5)

DD <- data.frame(PCOA$vectors) %>%
  rownames_to_column("species") %>%
  left_join(
    species_summary %>% select(species, Division, n_individuals, TNW),
    by = "species"
  )

pct_var <- round(PCOA$values$Relative_eig[1:2] * 100, 1)
cat(sprintf("Axis 1: %.1f%%  |  Axis 2: %.1f%%\n", pct_var[1], pct_var[2]))
cat("Species in PCoA:", nrow(DD), "\n")


# ==============================================================================
# 5. DIET COMPOSITION PER SPECIES  (for pie charts)
# ==============================================================================

PS_pie <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank = "Diet")

phyloseq_species     <- merge_samples(PS_pie,
                                      "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others",
                                      fun = mean)
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_species,
                                                function(x) x / sum(x))

diet_df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
  summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  rename(species = Sample) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from  = Diet,
              values_from = rel_abundance,
              values_fill = 0)

# Ensure all three diet columns exist
for (d in c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")) {
  if (!d %in% colnames(diet_df)) diet_df[[d]] <- 0
}

# Normalise to sum to 1
diet_df <- diet_df %>%
  mutate(total  = Animal + Plant + Fungi,
         Animal = Animal / total,
         Plant  = Plant  / total,
         Fungi  = Fungi  / total) %>%
  select(species, Animal, Plant, Fungi)

# Merge diet composition into PCoA coordinates
DD <- DD %>% left_join(diet_df, by = "species")

cat("Missing diet data:", sum(is.na(DD$Animal)), "species\n")


# ==============================================================================
# 6. COLOURS
# ==============================================================================

diet_colors <- c(
  "Animal" = "#E31A1C",
  "Plant"  = "#33A02C",
  "Fungi"  = "#1F78B4"
)


# ==============================================================================
# 7. PIE SLICE POLYGON BUILDER  (identical to Section 10 of main script)
# ==============================================================================

create_pie_slices <- function(data, x_col, y_col, diets, radius, n_points = 30) {
  pie_list <- list()
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(data))) {
    xc    <- data[[x_col]][i]
    yc    <- data[[y_col]][i]
    sp    <- data$species[i]
    props <- as.numeric(data[i, diets])
    props[is.na(props)] <- 0
    props[props < 0.01] <- 0
    if (sum(props) == 0) next
    props <- props / sum(props)
    cum   <- c(0, cumsum(props))
    for (j in seq_along(diets)) {
      if (props[j] == 0) next
      angles <- seq(2 * pi * cum[j],
                    2 * pi * cum[j + 1],
                    length.out = n_points)
      pie_list[[length(pie_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        species = sp,
        Diet    = diets[j],
        x       = c(xc, xc + radius * cos(angles)),
        y       = c(yc, yc + radius * sin(angles)),
        xc      = xc,
        yc      = yc
      )
    }
  }
  bind_rows(pie_list)
}


# ==============================================================================
# 8. SET RADIUS AND BUILD PIE DATA
# ==============================================================================

# Radius relative to Axis 1 range — identical to Section 10
pcoa_range <- diff(range(DD$Axis.1))
pie_radius <- pcoa_range * 0.015

pie_data <- create_pie_slices(
  data     = DD,
  x_col    = "Axis.1",
  y_col    = "Axis.2",
  diets    = c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi"),
  radius   = pie_radius,
  n_points = 200
)


# ==============================================================================
# 9. PLOT
# ==============================================================================

p_pcoa_nphl <- ggplot() +

  # Pie chart polygons
  geom_polygon(
    data  = pie_data,
    aes(x = x, y = y,
        fill  = Diet,
        group = interaction(species, Diet)),
    color = NA,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +

  # Hollow circle outline around each pie (identical to Section 10)
  geom_point(
    data   = DD,
    aes(x = Axis.1, y = Axis.2),
    shape  = 1,
    size   = pie_radius * 300,
    color  = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +

  # Species labels
  geom_text_repel(
    data               = DD,
    aes(x = Axis.1, y = Axis.2, label = species),
    size               = 2.6,
    fontface           = "italic",
    max.overlaps       = 20,
    box.padding        = 1.2,
    point.padding      = pie_radius * 150,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    segment.size       = 0.3,
    segment.color      = "grey40",
    seed               = 42
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = diet_colors, name = "Diet") +

  labs(
    title    = "PCoA — Non-phylogenetic niche overlap between species",
    subtitle = "Distance = 1 - overlap (sqrt-transformed) | Pie charts = diet composition",
    x        = sprintf("PCoA Axis 1 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[1]),
    y        = sprintf("PCoA Axis 2 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[2])
  ) +

  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +

  coord_fixed()

print(p_pcoa_nphl)


# ==============================================================================
# 10. SAVE
# ==============================================================================

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "PCoA_nonphylogenetic_piecharts.png"),
  p_pcoa_nphl,
  width  = 12,
  height = 10,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "PCoA_nonphylogenetic_piecharts.pdf"),
  p_pcoa_nphl,
  width  = 12,
  height = 10,
  device = "pdf"
)

cat("\nSaved to:", output_dir, "\n")
cat("Done.\n")










################################################################################
# TNW vs WIC Scatter Plot with Diet Pie Charts and Correlation Line
# v5: clean round-number axis breaks on back-transformed scale
################################################################################

library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

# ==============================================================================
# 1. PATHS
# ==============================================================================

output_dir  <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/figures_tables"
results_dir <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/RESULTS"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ==============================================================================
# 2. LOAD DATA
# ==============================================================================

load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
ps <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))
class(sample_df) <- "data.frame"

sample_df <- sample_df %>%
  mutate(Division = case_when(
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group %in%
      c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group ") ~ "Rattus invasive",
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group ==
      "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) "       ~ "Rattus endemic",
    TRUE ~ Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group
  ))

sp_division <- sample_df %>%
  select(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, Division) %>%
  distinct()

n_ind <- sample_df %>%
  count(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, name = "n_individuals")

species_summary <- read.csv(
  file.path(output_dir, "species_niche_summary_phylo.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

if (!"Division" %in% colnames(species_summary)) {
  species_summary <- species_summary %>%
    left_join(sp_division,
              by = c("species" = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"))
}
if (!"n_individuals" %in% colnames(species_summary)) {
  species_summary <- species_summary %>%
    left_join(n_ind,
              by = c("species" = "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"))
}

# Diet composition per species
PS_pie <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank = "Diet")
phyloseq_species     <- merge_samples(PS_pie,
                                       "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others",
                                       fun = mean)
phyloseq_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(phyloseq_species,
                                                 function(x) x / sum(x))

diet_df <- psmelt(phyloseq_species_rel) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Diet)) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Diet) %>%
  summarise(rel_abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  rename(species = Sample) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from  = Diet,
              values_from = rel_abundance,
              values_fill = 0)

for (d in c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")) {
  if (!d %in% colnames(diet_df)) diet_df[[d]] <- 0
}

diet_df <- diet_df %>%
  mutate(total  = Animal + Plant + Fungi,
         Animal = Animal / total,
         Plant  = Plant  / total,
         Fungi  = Fungi  / total) %>%
  select(species, Animal, Plant, Fungi)

# ==============================================================================
# 3. MERGE
# ==============================================================================

plot_df <- species_summary %>%
  left_join(diet_df, by = "species") %>%
  filter(!is.na(TNW), !is.na(mean_WIC),
         !is.na(Animal), !is.na(Plant), !is.na(Fungi)) %>%
  mutate(
    log_WIC = log10(mean_WIC),
    log_TNW = log10(TNW)
  )

cat("Species for plot:", nrow(plot_df), "\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 4. COLOURS
# ==============================================================================

division_colors <- c(
  "Coccymys division"  = "#E60000",
  "Hydromys division"  = "#004D99",
  "Mallomys division"  = "#33CC33",
  "Pogonomys division" = "#FFCC00",
  "Rattus endemic"     = "#660066",
  "Rattus invasive"    = "#FF6600",
  "Uromys division"    = "#00CCCC"
)

diet_colors <- c(
  "Animal" = "#E31A1C",
  "Plant"  = "#33A02C",
  "Fungi"  = "#1F78B4"
)

# ==============================================================================
# 5. FIX 2: ASPECT RATIO CORRECTION FOR PIE SHAPES
# Pies drawn in data coordinates look stretched because x and y axes span
# different ranges. We must scale the radius differently on each axis so
# the pies appear circular in the rendered plot.
#
# Strategy: compute the axis ranges, then determine the data-to-inches
# ratio for each axis given the intended plot dimensions, and apply a
# correction factor to the y-radius so circles appear circular.
# ==============================================================================

plot_width_in  <- 13   # inches — must match ggsave width
plot_height_in <- 10   # inches — must match ggsave height

# Margins consume roughly 1.5 inches on each side (left/right) and
# 1.2 inches on top/bottom for axes, titles, legends
panel_width_in  <- plot_width_in  - 3.5
panel_height_in <- plot_height_in - 2.8

log_wic_range <- diff(range(plot_df$log_WIC))
log_tnw_range <- diff(range(plot_df$log_TNW))

# Data units per inch for each axis
x_units_per_inch <- log_wic_range / panel_width_in
y_units_per_inch <- log_tnw_range / panel_height_in

# To draw a circle: if radius_x in data units, radius_y must be:
# radius_y = radius_x * (y_units_per_inch / x_units_per_inch)
aspect_correction <- y_units_per_inch / x_units_per_inch

cat(sprintf("Aspect correction factor: %.3f\n", aspect_correction))

# FIX 1: 40% smaller than v1 — use 2.4% of x-range instead of 4%
base_radius_x <- log_wic_range * 0.014
base_radius_y <- base_radius_x * aspect_correction

cat(sprintf("Base radius x: %.4f | y: %.4f\n", base_radius_x, base_radius_y))

# ==============================================================================
# 6. PIE SLICE POLYGON BUILDER
# FIX 2: separate x_radius and y_radius for correct circular appearance
# FIX 3: outline circle will be drawn AROUND the pie using the same radii
# ==============================================================================

create_pie_slices <- function(data, x_col, y_col,
                               diets, x_radius, y_radius,
                               n_points = 200) {
  pie_list <- list()
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(data))) {
    xc    <- data[[x_col]][i]
    yc    <- data[[y_col]][i]
    sp    <- data$species[i]
    props <- as.numeric(data[i, diets])
    props[is.na(props)] <- 0
    props[props < 0.01] <- 0
    if (sum(props) == 0) next
    props <- props / sum(props)
    cum   <- c(0, cumsum(props))
    for (j in seq_along(diets)) {
      if (props[j] == 0) next
      angles <- seq(2 * pi * cum[j],
                    2 * pi * cum[j + 1],
                    length.out = n_points)
      pie_list[[length(pie_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        species = sp,
        Diet    = diets[j],
        # FIX 2: use separate x and y radii
        x       = c(xc, xc + x_radius * cos(angles)),
        y       = c(yc, yc + y_radius * sin(angles)),
        xc      = xc,
        yc      = yc
      )
    }
  }
  bind_rows(pie_list)
}

# Build outline circle points (full circle for division colour ring)
create_circle_outline <- function(data, x_col, y_col,
                                   x_radius, y_radius,
                                   n_points = 200) {
  circle_list <- list()
  angles <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n_points)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(data))) {
    xc <- data[[x_col]][i]
    yc <- data[[y_col]][i]
    sp <- data$species[i]
    circle_list[[i]] <- data.frame(
      species = sp,
      x       = xc + x_radius * cos(angles),
      y       = yc + y_radius * sin(angles)
    )
  }
  bind_rows(circle_list)
}

pie_data <- create_pie_slices(
  data      = plot_df,
  x_col     = "log_WIC",
  y_col     = "log_TNW",
  diets     = c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi"),
  x_radius  = base_radius_x,
  y_radius  = base_radius_y,
  n_points  = 200
)

# FIX 3: outline circle drawn around the pie at the same radius
outline_data <- create_circle_outline(
  data     = plot_df,
  x_col    = "log_WIC",
  y_col    = "log_TNW",
  x_radius = base_radius_x,
  y_radius = base_radius_y,
  n_points = 200
)

# ==============================================================================
# 7. CORRELATION LINE
# ==============================================================================

spearman_r <- cor(plot_df$TNW, plot_df$mean_WIC, method = "spearman")
spearman_p <- cor.test(plot_df$TNW, plot_df$mean_WIC,
                        method = "spearman")$p.value

lm_fit  <- lm(log_TNW ~ log_WIC, data = plot_df)
lm_pred <- data.frame(
  log_WIC = seq(min(plot_df$log_WIC) - base_radius_x,
                max(plot_df$log_WIC) + base_radius_x,
                length.out = 200)
)
lm_pred$log_TNW <- predict(lm_fit, newdata = lm_pred)

p_label <- ifelse(spearman_p < 0.001, "p < 0.001",
                   paste0("p = ", round(spearman_p, 3)))
cor_label <- sprintf("Spearman's rho = %.3f, %s", spearman_r, p_label)

# ==============================================================================
# 8. BUILD PLOT
# FIX 4: axis and subtitle labels use plain ASCII "log10" not unicode
# ==============================================================================

p_tnw_wic_pie <- ggplot() +

  # Correlation line (behind pies)
  geom_line(
    data      = lm_pred,
    aes(x = log_WIC, y = log_TNW),
    linetype  = "dashed",
    color     = "gray40",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +

  # Pie chart polygons
  geom_polygon(
    data = pie_data,
    aes(x = x, y = y,
        fill  = Diet,
        group = interaction(species, Diet)),
    color = NA,
    alpha = 0.92
  ) +

  # Black outline around pie
  geom_path(
    data = outline_data,
    aes(x = x, y = y,
        group = species),
    color     = "black",
    linewidth = 0.6,
    alpha     = 0.9
  ) +

  # Species labels
  geom_text_repel(
    data               = plot_df,
    aes(x = log_WIC, y = log_TNW, label = species),
    size               = 3.5,
    fontface           = "italic",
    max.overlaps       = 30,
    box.padding        = 1.2,
    point.padding      = 0.8,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    segment.size       = 0.4,
    segment.color      = "gray30",
    segment.linetype   = "solid",
    arrow              = arrow(length = unit(0.01, "npc"), type = "open"),
    force              = 3,
    force_pull         = 0.5,
    seed               = 42
  ) +

  # Correlation annotation
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = min(plot_df$log_WIC) + 0.02 * log_wic_range,
    y        = max(plot_df$log_TNW) - 0.04 * log_tnw_range,
    label    = cor_label,
    size     = 3.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    fontface = "bold",
    color    = "gray20"
  ) +

  # Scales
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = diet_colors,
    name   = "Diet",
    guide  = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(size = 5, shape = 15),
      order = 1
    )
  ) +


  # FIX 4: plain "log10" labels — no unicode superscripts
  scale_x_continuous(
    name   = "Mean WIC — Individual-level niche breadth (log10 scale)",
    breaks = log10(c(0.01, 0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.08, 0.10)),
    labels =        c(0.01, 0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.08, 0.10),
    limits = range(plot_df$log_WIC) + c(-base_radius_x * 2,
                                         base_radius_x * 8)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name   = "TNW — Total Niche Width (log10 scale)",
    breaks = log10(c(0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.07, 0.10)),
    labels =        c(0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.07, 0.10),
    limits = range(plot_df$log_TNW) + c(-base_radius_y * 2,
                                         base_radius_y * 8)
  ) +

  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 8, color = "gray30", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position  = "right",
    legend.title     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  ) +

  labs(
    title    = "Species-level niche breadth: Total (TNW) vs Individual (WIC)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Pie charts = mean diet composition (Animal / Plant / Fungi) | ",
      "Both axes on log10 scale"
    )
  )

print(p_tnw_wic_pie)

# ==============================================================================
# 9. SAVE — dimensions must match panel_width_in / panel_height_in above
# ==============================================================================

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "TNW_vs_WIC_PieChart_Scatter_v5.png"),
  p_tnw_wic_pie,
  width  = plot_width_in,
  height = plot_height_in,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "TNW_vs_WIC_PieChart_Scatter_v5.pdf"),
  p_tnw_wic_pie,
  width  = plot_width_in,
  height = plot_height_in,
  device = "pdf"
)

cat("\nSaved to:", output_dir, "\n")
cat("Done.\n")



```


*ancestral reconstruction of niche partitioning*
```{r ASC, warning=F, message=F}
################################################################################
# NICHE PARTITIONING — ANCESTRAL STATE RECONSTRUCTION & GLMM ANALYSES
# Pipeline: indicspecies (phylogenetic, complete dataset)
#
# Input files (from indisp_postprocess_halil.txt output):
#   - species_niche_summary_phylo.csv   : species-level metrics (n = 22 species,
#                                         i.e. only species with >= 6 individuals)
#   - individual_niche_table_phylo.csv  : individual-level metrics + sample metadata
#                                         (n = 819 rows, 44 species total)
#
# Key columns used:
#   species_niche_summary_phylo.csv:
#     species, TNW, TNW_LC, TNW_UC, mean_WIC, Inverse_BIC, Spec_index,
#     Division, n_individuals
#
#   individual_niche_table_phylo.csv:
#     sample_ID, Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, WIC, TNW, Spec_index,
#     Inverse_BIC, Division, Alt, Loc, Tribe, spatial_niche, mean_mass,
#     Metalokalita, Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, ...
#
# FILTERING LOGIC:
#   species_niche_summary_phylo.csv contains only the 22 species with >= 6
#   individuals. ALL analyses (ASR, TNW model, individual-level GLMMs) are
#   restricted to these 22 species. The script reports which species are
#   excluded BEFORE dropping any data, so you can review first.
#
# Steps:
#   PART 1 — Ancestral State Reconstruction (ASR) for TNW, mean_WIC,
#             Spec_index, Inverse_BIC using phytools::fastAnc
#   PART 2 — Models:
#             (a) AIC-based model selection for TNW (species-level, Gaussian)
#                 global: TNW ~ spatial_niche + Tribe + mean_mass + Range_Type
#                 max 3 fixed predictors via MuMIn::dredge()
#             (b) Beta-GLMM for Spec_index / WIC/TNW (individual-level)
#                 linear and non-linear altitude versions
#             (c) Gamma-GLMM for WIC (individual-level)
#   PART 3 — Publication-ready tables via sjPlot::tab_model(), saved as HTML
#             individually and combined into one Word document
################################################################################


# ==============================================================================
# 0. PACKAGES
# ==============================================================================

library(ape)
library(phytools)
library(ggtree)
library(tidytree)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(viridis)
library(glmmTMB)
library(splines)
library(MuMIn)
library(broom)
library(broom.mixed)
library(sjPlot)
library(ggeffects)
library(performance)   # r2() for glmmTMB — avoids MuMIn R2=1 bug
library(officer)       # Word document assembly
library(webshot2)      # renders HTML tables to PNG for Word export


# ==============================================================================
# 1. PATHS  — edit these to match your system
# ==============================================================================

tables_dir     <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/figures_tables"
host_tree_path <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/ddRAD_trees/new_merged_trees_10.10.2024/Joined_scaled_RH85.nwk"
asr_out_dir    <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/ASR"
glmm_out_dir   <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/GLMM"

dir.create(asr_out_dir,  showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(glmm_out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)


# ==============================================================================
# 2. LOAD DATA
# ==============================================================================

species_summary  <- read.csv(
  file.path(tables_dir, "species_niche_summary_phylo.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
individual_table <- read.csv(
  file.path(tables_dir, "individual_niche_table_phylo.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("=== DATA LOADED ===\n")
cat("species_summary:  ", nrow(species_summary),  "species |",
    ncol(species_summary),  "columns\n")
cat("individual_table: ", nrow(individual_table), "rows    |",
    ncol(individual_table), "columns\n")

# Report species present / absent before any filtering
all_ind_species  <- sort(unique(individual_table$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others))
summary_species  <- sort(species_summary$species)
excluded_species <- setdiff(all_ind_species, summary_species)

cat("\nSpecies in species_summary (n =", length(summary_species),
    ") — used in all analyses:\n")
print(summary_species)

cat("\nSpecies in individual_table NOT in species_summary",
    "(n =", length(excluded_species), ") — excluded (< 6 individuals):\n")
print(excluded_species)


# ==============================================================================
# 3. DIVISION COLOURS  (consistent with indisp_postprocess_halil.txt)
# ==============================================================================

division_colors <- c(
  "Coccymys division"  = "#E60000",
  "Hydromys division"  = "#004D99",
  "Mallomys division"  = "#33CC33",
  "Pogonomys division" = "#FFCC00",
  "Rattus endemic"     = "#660066",
  "Rattus invasive"    = "#FF6600",
  "Uromys division"    = "#00CCCC"
)


################################################################################
# PART 1: ANCESTRAL STATE RECONSTRUCTION (ASR)
################################################################################

# ==============================================================================
# 4. LOAD AND PREPARE HOST PHYLOGENY
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== PART 1: ASR ===\n")
cat("Loading host tree:", host_tree_path, "\n")
host_tree_raw <- ape::read.tree(host_tree_path)

# Strip suffix after first underscore to recover individual sample IDs
host_tree_raw$tip.label <- sub("_.*$", "", host_tree_raw$tip.label)

# Map tip labels -> species names via individual_table
tip_to_species <- setNames(
  individual_table$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others[
    match(host_tree_raw$tip.label, individual_table$sample_ID)
  ],
  host_tree_raw$tip.label
)

cat("Tips matched to a species: ",
    sum(!is.na(tip_to_species)), "/", length(tip_to_species), "\n")

# Keep only tips that have a species match AND that species is in species_summary
tips_to_keep      <- names(tip_to_species)[
  !is.na(tip_to_species) & tip_to_species %in% species_summary$species
]
host_tree_matched <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_raw, tips_to_keep)
kept_species      <- tip_to_species[tips_to_keep]

# One representative tip per species
unique_species      <- unique(kept_species)
representative_tips <- sapply(unique_species,
                               function(sp) tips_to_keep[kept_species == sp][1])
tree_niche          <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_matched, representative_tips)
tree_niche$tip.label <- as.character(tip_to_species[tree_niche$tip.label])

cat("Tree pruned to", Ntip(tree_niche),
    "species (matching species_summary)\n")

# Filter species_summary to tree tips and keep same order
species_div_final <- species_summary %>%
  filter(species %in% tree_niche$tip.label) %>%
  arrange(match(species, tree_niche$tip.label))

# Species -> Division lookup for plot annotations
species_divisions <- species_div_final %>%
  select(Species = species, Division)

# Root, resolve polytomies, ultrametricise, scale 0-1
if (!is.rooted(tree_niche))
  tree_niche <- root(tree_niche,
                     outgroup     = tree_niche$tip.label[1],
                     resolve.root = TRUE)
if (!is.binary(tree_niche))
  tree_niche <- multi2di(tree_niche)
tree_niche <- chronos(tree_niche, lambda = 0, model = "relaxed")
tree_niche$edge.length <- tree_niche$edge.length /
  max(node.depth.edgelength(tree_niche))

cat("Tree prepared for ASR (n tips =", Ntip(tree_niche), ")\n")


# ==============================================================================
# 5. ASR WRAPPER FUNCTION
# ==============================================================================

run_asr_pipeline <- function(trait_col, trait_label, output_path,
                              palette_option = "turbo") {

  # ---- 5a. Extract and align trait vector ----
  trait_vec <- setNames(species_div_final[[trait_col]],
                        species_div_final$species)
  trait_vec <- trait_vec[tree_niche$tip.label]

  # Report NAs before dropping — do not silently remove
  if (any(is.na(trait_vec))) {
    n_miss     <- sum(is.na(trait_vec))
    miss_names <- names(trait_vec)[is.na(trait_vec)]
    warning(sprintf(
      "[ASR] '%s': %d tip(s) with NA will be dropped from this ASR: %s",
      trait_col, n_miss, paste(miss_names, collapse = ", ")
    ))
    tips_ok   <- names(trait_vec)[!is.na(trait_vec)]
    tr_tmp    <- ape::keep.tip(tree_niche, tips_ok)
    trait_vec <- trait_vec[tips_ok]
    sd_tmp    <- species_divisions %>% filter(Species %in% tips_ok)
    sf_tmp    <- species_div_final  %>% filter(species %in% tips_ok)
  } else {
    tr_tmp <- tree_niche
    sd_tmp <- species_divisions
    sf_tmp <- species_div_final
  }

  # ---- 5b. Phylogenetic signal (Pagel's lambda) ----
  lambda_result   <- phytools::phylosig(tr_tmp, trait_vec,
                                        method = "lambda", test = TRUE)
  annotation_text <- paste0(
    "\u03bb = ", round(lambda_result$lambda, 2),
    ", p = ",
    ifelse(lambda_result$P < 0.001, "<0.001", round(lambda_result$P, 3))
  )
  cat(sprintf("[ASR] %s | %s\n", trait_label, annotation_text))

  # ---- 5c. Ancestral state reconstruction ----
  asr <- phytools::fastAnc(tr_tmp, trait_vec, vars = TRUE, CI = TRUE)

  all_trait_data <- rbind(
    data.frame(node  = seq_len(Ntip(tr_tmp)),
               trait = as.numeric(trait_vec)),
    data.frame(node  = (Ntip(tr_tmp) + 1):(Ntip(tr_tmp) + tr_tmp$Nnode),
               trait = as.numeric(asr$ace))
  )

  # ---- 5d. Scale tree for equal-area layout ----
  p_tmp        <- ggtree(tr_tmp)
  scale_factor <- max(p_tmp$data$y) / max(p_tmp$data$x)
  tree_scaled  <- tr_tmp
  tree_scaled$edge.length <- tree_scaled$edge.length * scale_factor

  # ---- 5e. Merge trait + division onto tree data ----
  tree_data_full <- as.data.frame(ggtree(tree_scaled)$data) %>%
    mutate(label = as.character(label)) %>%
    left_join(all_trait_data, by = "node") %>%
    left_join(sd_tmp, by = c("label" = "Species"))

  # ---- 5f. Division background rectangles ----
  division_backgrounds <- tree_data_full %>%
    filter(isTip, !is.na(Division)) %>%
    group_by(Division) %>%
    summarize(
      ymin = min(y) - 0.5,
      ymax = max(y) + 0.5,
      xmin = -0.05 * max(tree_data_full$x),
      xmax =  max(tree_data_full$x) * 1.7,
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  division_labels <- division_backgrounds %>%
    mutate(
      y_center   = (ymin + ymax) / 2,
      x_position = max(tree_data_full$x) * 1.55
    )

  # ---- 5g. Build plot ----
  p <- ggtree(tree_scaled, layout = "rectangular", linewidth = 0.8) %<+%
    tree_data_full +
    aes(color = trait) +
    geom_rect(
      data        = division_backgrounds,
      aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
      fill        = division_colors[division_backgrounds$Division],
      alpha       = 0.2,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data        = division_labels,
      aes(x = x_position, y = y_center, label = Division),
      size        = 3.5,
      fontface    = "bold",
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_rootedge(
      rootedge  = max(tree_scaled$edge.length) * 0.1,
      linetype  = "dashed",
      linewidth = 0.8,
      color     = "black"
    ) +
    geom_tree(continuous = "colour", linewidth = 1.2) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(
      name   = trait_label,
      option = palette_option,
      guide  = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 10, barheight = 0.8,
                              title.position = "top")
    ) +
    geom_tiplab(
      aes(label = ifelse(
        isTip,
        paste0(label, "",
               sf_tmp$n_individuals[match(label, sf_tmp$species)], ""),
        ""
      )),
      size     = 3.0,
      offset   = 0.02,
      fontface = "italic",
      color    = "black"
    ) +
    annotate("text",
             x        = 0.02,
             y        = max(tree_data_full$y),
             label    = annotation_text,
             size     = 4.5,
             fontface = "bold",
             hjust    = 0) +
    xlim(0, max(tree_data_full$x) * 1.8) +
    ggtitle(paste("Ancestral State Reconstruction \u2014", trait_label)) +
    theme_tree2() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      plot.title      = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 15, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin     = margin(10, 100, 30, 10)
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      name   = "Evolutionary Time (scaled 0\u20131)",
      breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2) * scale_factor,
      labels = seq(0, 1, 0.2)
    )

  ggsave(output_path, p, width = 14, height = 9, dpi = 300)
  cat("  Saved:", output_path, "\n")
  return(invisible(p))
}


# ==============================================================================
# 6. RUN ASR FOR ALL FOUR METRICS
# ==============================================================================

run_asr_pipeline("TNW",         "Total Niche Width (TNW)",
                 file.path(asr_out_dir, "ASR_TNW.png"))
run_asr_pipeline("mean_WIC",    "Mean Within-Individual Component (WIC)",
                 file.path(asr_out_dir, "ASR_WIC.png"))
run_asr_pipeline("Spec_index",  "Specialization Index (WIC / TNW)",
                 file.path(asr_out_dir, "ASR_SpecIndex.png"))
run_asr_pipeline("Inverse_BIC", "Inverse BIC (mean within-species niche overlap)",
                 file.path(asr_out_dir, "ASR_InverseBIC.png"))

cat("All 4 ASR plots saved.\n")


################################################################################
# PART 2: GLMM ANALYSES
################################################################################


# ==============================================================================
# 7. PREPARE DATA
# ==============================================================================
 
cat("\n=== PART 2: DATA PREPARATION FOR MODELS ===\n")
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7a. Filter individual_table to the 22 species in species_summary
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
cat("Rows in individual_table (all species):          ", nrow(individual_table), "\n")
 
data_ind_all <- individual_table %>%
  filter(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others %in% species_summary$species)
 
cat("Rows after restricting to species_summary species:", nrow(data_ind_all), "\n")
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7b. NA report for key model variables BEFORE any further filtering
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
key_vars <- c("TNW", "Spec_index", "WIC", "Alt", "mean_mass",
              "Tribe", "spatial_niche", "Metalokalita", "Loc")
 
cat("\n--- NA counts in key model variables (post species-filter) ---\n")
na_report <- data_ind_all %>%
  select(all_of(key_vars)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "N_missing")
print(na_report)
cat("--- Review the above before proceeding ---\n\n")
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7c. Type coercion and derived variables — individual-level table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
data_ind <- data_ind_all %>%
  mutate(
    Alt        = as.numeric(Alt),
    mean_mass  = as.numeric(mean_mass),
    Tribe      = as.factor(Tribe),
    spatial_niche                     = as.factor(spatial_niche),
    Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others = as.factor(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others),
    Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others   = as.factor(Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others),
    Metalokalita = as.factor(Metalokalita),
    Loc          = as.factor(Loc),
 
    # Merge Metalokalita sub-sites into two main mountain ranges
    Metalokalita = case_when(
      as.character(Metalokalita) %in%
        c("Baitabag", "Wanang", "Mt_Wilhelm") ~ "Mt_Wilhelm",
      TRUE ~ as.character(Metalokalita)
    ) %>% as.factor(),
 
    # Scaled continuous predictors
    Alt_s  = as.numeric(scale(Alt)),
    Mass_s = as.numeric(scale(mean_mass)),
 
    # WIC: Gamma regression requires strictly positive values
    WIC_pos = ifelse(is.na(WIC) | WIC <= 0, NA_real_, WIC)
  )
 
# Set reference levels
data_ind$spatial_niche <- relevel(data_ind$spatial_niche, ref = "terrestrial")
data_ind$Tribe         <- relevel(data_ind$Tribe,         ref = "Rattini")
data_ind$Metalokalita  <- relevel(data_ind$Metalokalita,  ref = "Mt_Wilhelm")
 
cat("Individual-level data ready: n =", nrow(data_ind),
    "| species =", nlevels(data_ind$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others), "\n")
cat("Metalokalita level counts:\n")
print(table(data_ind$Metalokalita))
cat("WIC_pos range:", range(data_ind$WIC_pos, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("NA in WIC_pos:", sum(is.na(data_ind$WIC_pos)), "\n")
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7d. Species-level dataset
#     Used for: Model A (TNW) and Model B (Spec_index)
#     Base: species_summary (already filtered to >= 6 individuals)
#     Add:  Range_Type, Tribe, spatial_niche, scaled mean_mass, altitude
#           summaries derived from data_ind
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
range_lookup <- data_ind %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarise(
    n_ranges       = n_distinct(as.character(Metalokalita)),
    ranges_present = paste(sort(unique(as.character(Metalokalita))),
                           collapse = "_"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Range_Type = case_when(
      n_ranges > 1                    ~ "Both",
      ranges_present == "Finisterres" ~ "Finisterres",
      ranges_present == "Mt_Wilhelm"  ~ "Mt_Wilhelm",
      TRUE                            ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, Range_Type)
 
trait_lookup <- data_ind %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarise(
    Tribe         = first(as.character(Tribe)),
    spatial_niche = first(as.character(spatial_niche)),
    mean_mass_raw = first(mean_mass),
    Alt_mean_raw  = mean(Alt, na.rm = TRUE),
    Alt_range_raw = max(Alt, na.rm = TRUE) - min(Alt, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups       = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)
 
data_species <- species_summary %>%
  left_join(range_lookup, by = "species") %>%
  left_join(trait_lookup, by = "species") %>%
  mutate(
    mean_mass = as.numeric(scale(mean_mass_raw)),
    Alt_mean  = as.numeric(scale(Alt_mean_raw)),
    Alt_range = as.numeric(scale(Alt_range_raw)),
    Tribe         = relevel(as.factor(Tribe),         ref = "Rattini"),
    spatial_niche = relevel(as.factor(spatial_niche), ref = "terrestrial"),
    Range_Type    = relevel(as.factor(Range_Type),    ref = "Both"),
    Metalokalita  = relevel(as.factor(Range_Type),    ref = "Both"),
 
    # Logit-transform Spec_index for Model B
    # Spec_index is confirmed within (0,1) — check boundaries before transforming
```
