```{r ASC, warning=F, message=F}
    Spec_index_logit = log(Spec_index / (1 - Spec_index))
  )
 
cat("\nSpecies-level data: n =", nrow(data_species), "species\n")
cat("Spec_index range:", range(data_species$Spec_index, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Spec_index_logit range:", round(range(data_species$Spec_index_logit,
                                           na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
cat("Alt_mean_raw range (metres):",
    round(range(data_species$Alt_mean_raw, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n")
cat("Alt_range_raw range (metres):",
    round(range(data_species$Alt_range_raw, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n")
 
# NA report for species-level model variables
cat("\n--- NA counts in species-level model variables ---\n")
na_sp <- data_species %>%
  select(TNW, Spec_index_logit, spatial_niche, Tribe, mean_mass,
         Range_Type, Metalokalita, Alt_mean, Alt_range) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "N_missing")
print(na_sp)
cat("--- Rows with any NA will be dropped automatically by lm() ---\n\n")
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7e. Unique-species table for Model B
#     One row per species, confirmed — this is what lm() needs.
#     Equivalent to data_species, but we create data_ind.unq explicitly
#     to match supervisor's variable name in case it is referenced downstream.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
data_ind.unq <- data_species   # alias: species_summary + derived predictors
 
 
# ==============================================================================
# 8. MODEL A: TNW model
# ==============================================================================
 
vars_tnw <- c("TNW", "spatial_niche", "Tribe", "mean_mass",
              "Range_Type", "Alt_mean", "Alt_range")
data_species_cc <- data_species %>%
  select(all_of(vars_tnw)) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.))
 
n_dropped <- nrow(data_species) - nrow(data_species_cc)
cat("Complete cases: n =", nrow(data_species_cc),
    "(", n_dropped, "species dropped due to NA)\n")
 
# Full model for side-by-side comparison in tab_model
model_tnw_full <- lm(
  TNW ~ spatial_niche + Tribe + mean_mass,
  data = data_species_cc
)
 
model_tnw_primary=model_tnw_full
# ==============================================================================
# Forest plot for TNW (primary model)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n--- Generating forest plot for TNW primary model ---\n")

# Extract coefficients with confidence intervals (lm object)
tidy_tnw <- broom::tidy(model_tnw_primary, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      term == "Range_TypeFinisterres"    ~ "Range: only Finisterres",
      term == "Range_TypeMt_Wilhelm"     ~ "Range: only Mt. Wilhelm",
      term == "spatial_nichescansorial"  ~ "Spatial Niche: Scansorial",
      term == "TribeHydromyini"          ~ "Tribe: Hydromyini",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    sig = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ "\u2020",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(Predictor = factor(Predictor, levels = rev(Predictor)))   # order for plot

p_tnw_forest <- ggplot(tidy_tnw, aes(x = estimate, y = Predictor)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotdash", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.25, linewidth = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(fill = estimate > 0),
             shape = 21, size = 4, stroke = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(x = conf.high, label = sig),
            hjust = -0.3, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#2c7bb6", "FALSE" = "#d7191c")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Drivers of Total Niche Width (TNW)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Species-level lm | n = 22 species | ",
      "Reference: Terrestrial, Rattini, Both ranges"
    ),
    x = "Estimate (TNW units)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = c(min(tidy_tnw$conf.low, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.01,
                      max(tidy_tnw$conf.high, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.03))

print(p_tnw_forest)
ggsave(file.path(glmm_out_dir, "TNW_Forest_Plot_new.png"),
       p_tnw_forest, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
cat("Saved: TNW_Forest_Plot.png\n")




# ==============================================================================
# 9. MODEL B: lm() FOR Spec_index — species-level, logit-transformed
#    REVISED: formula matched to TNW primary model
#    Spec_index_logit ~ Range_Type + spatial_niche + Tribe
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== MODEL B: Spec_index (species-level lm, logit response) ===\n")
cat("n =", sum(!is.na(data_ind.unq$Spec_index_logit)), "species\n")
cat("Spec_index range:", range(data_ind.unq$Spec_index, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Spec_index_logit range:",
    round(range(data_ind.unq$Spec_index_logit, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

# Check for boundary values that would break logit
if (any(data_ind.unq$Spec_index <= 0 | data_ind.unq$Spec_index >= 1,
        na.rm = TRUE)) {
  stop("Spec_index contains values <= 0 or >= 1 — logit transform will produce",
       " Inf/-Inf. Inspect and apply boundary correction before proceeding.")
}

# Primary model — same formula as TNW primary model
model_spec <- lm(
  Spec_index_logit ~ Range_Type + spatial_niche + Tribe,
  data = data_ind.unq
)

cat("\nModel B summary:\n")
print(summary(model_spec))

cat("\nVIF:\n")
print(vif(model_spec))

# ---- Forest plot for Model B ----

tidy_spec <- broom::tidy(model_spec, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      term == "Range_TypeFinisterres"   ~ "Range: only Finisterres",
      term == "Range_TypeMt_Wilhelm"    ~ "Range: only Mt. Wilhelm",
      term == "spatial_nichescansorial" ~ "Spatial Niche: Scansorial",
      term == "TribeHydromyini"         ~ "Tribe: Hydromyini",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    sig = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ "\u2020",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(Predictor = factor(Predictor, levels = rev(Predictor)))

p_spec_forest <- ggplot(tidy_spec, aes(x = estimate, y = Predictor)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotdash",
             color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.25, linewidth = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(fill = estimate > 0),
             shape = 21, size = 4, stroke = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(x = conf.high, label = sig),
            hjust = -0.3, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#2c7bb6", "FALSE" = "#d7191c")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Drivers of Specialization Index (WIC/TNW)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Species-level lm | logit(Spec_index) | n = 22 species | ",
      "Reference: Terrestrial, Rattini, Both ranges"
    ),
    x = "Estimate (logit scale)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = c(min(tidy_spec$conf.low,  na.rm = TRUE) - 0.3,
                      max(tidy_spec$conf.high, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.6))

print(p_spec_forest)
ggsave(file.path(glmm_out_dir, "SpecIndex_Forest_Plot_revised.png"),
       p_spec_forest, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
cat("Saved: SpecIndex_Forest_Plot_revised.png\n")
 
 
# ==============================================================================
# 10. MODEL C: GAMMA-GLMM FOR WIC — individual-level niche breadth
#     WIC is strictly positive and right-skewed — Gamma with log link.
#     Random effect: (1 | Genus) captures phylogenetic clustering without
#     conflicting with species-level fixed effects.
#     Two versions: linear and non-linear altitude (natural spline df=3).
# ==============================================================================
 
cat("\n=== MODEL C: WIC (Gamma GLMM, log link) ===\n")
cat("WIC_pos range:", range(data_ind$WIC_pos, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("NA in WIC_pos:", sum(is.na(data_ind$WIC_pos)), "\n")
 
model_wic_linear <- glmmTMB(
  WIC_pos ~ spatial_niche + Tribe + Mass_s + Metalokalita +
    Alt_s +
    (1 | Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others),
  data   = data_ind,
  family = Gamma(link = "log")
)
 
model_wic_nonlinear <- glmmTMB(
  WIC_pos ~ spatial_niche + Tribe + Mass_s + Metalokalita +
    ns(Alt_s, df = 3) +
    (1 | Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others),
  data   = data_ind,
  family = Gamma(link = "log")
)
 
cat("\nModel C (linear altitude) summary:\n")
print(summary(model_wic_linear))
cat("\nModel C (non-linear altitude) summary:\n")
print(summary(model_wic_nonlinear))
cat("\nAIC comparison (linear vs non-linear altitude):\n")
print(AIC(model_wic_linear, model_wic_nonlinear))
 
cat("\nR2 (non-linear model):\n")
print(performance::r2(model_wic_nonlinear))
cat("\nR2 (linear model):\n")
print(performance::r2(model_wic_linear))
 
# ---- 10a. Combined forest plot ----
 
tidy_wic_nonlin <- broom.mixed::tidy(
  model_wic_nonlinear, effects = "fixed",
  conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "wald"
) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      term == "spatial_nichescansorial"  ~ "Spatial Niche: Scansorial",
      term == "spatial_nichearboreal"    ~ "Spatial Niche: Arboreal",
      term == "TribeHydromyini"          ~ "Tribe: Hydromyini",
      term == "Mass_s"                   ~ "Body Mass (scaled)",
      term == "MetalokalitaFinisterres"  ~ "Metalokalita: Finisterres",
      str_detect(term, "ns\\(Alt_s.*1$") ~ "Altitude spline (basis 1)",
      str_detect(term, "ns\\(Alt_s.*2$") ~ "Altitude spline (basis 2)",
      str_detect(term, "ns\\(Alt_s.*3$") ~ "Altitude spline (basis 3)",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    model_source = "Non-linear (spline)",
    sig          = sig_label(p.value)
  )
 
tidy_wic_lin_alt <- broom.mixed::tidy(
  model_wic_linear, effects = "fixed",
  conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "wald"
) %>%
  filter(term == "Alt_s") %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor    = "Altitude (linear)",
    model_source = "Linear",
    sig          = sig_label(p.value)
  )
 
forest_wic <- bind_rows(tidy_wic_nonlin, tidy_wic_lin_alt) %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor    = factor(Predictor, levels = rev(unique(Predictor))),
    model_source = factor(model_source, levels = c("Non-linear (spline)", "Linear"))
  )
 
p_wic_forest <- ggplot(forest_wic,
                       aes(x = estimate, y = Predictor)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotdash",
             color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.25, linewidth = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(fill = estimate > 0),
             shape = 21, size = 4, stroke = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(x = conf.high, label = sig),
            hjust = -0.3, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#2c7bb6", "FALSE" = "#d7191c")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Drivers of Individual Niche Breadth (WIC)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Gamma GLMM (log link) | Random: (1|Genus) | ",
      "Reference: Terrestrial, Rattini, Mt. Wilhelm\n",
      "Spline rows: non-linear model | ",
      "Altitude (linear): linear model estimate"
    ),
    x = "Estimate (log scale)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = c(min(forest_wic$conf.low,  na.rm = TRUE) - 0.3,
                      max(forest_wic$conf.high, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.6))
 
print(p_wic_forest)
ggsave(file.path(glmm_out_dir, "WIC_Forest_Plot.png"),
       p_wic_forest, width = 11, height = 8, dpi = 300)
cat("Saved: WIC_Forest_Plot.png\n")
 
# ---- 10b. Marginal effect of altitude on WIC (non-linear model) ----
pred_wic_alt <- ggeffects::ggpredict(model_wic_nonlinear, terms = "Alt_s [all]")
 
p_wic_alt <- ggplot(pred_wic_alt, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              fill = "#d95f02", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(color = "#d95f02", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_jitter(data       = data_ind,
              aes(x = Alt_s, y = WIC_pos),
              alpha       = 0.1, width = 0.02,
              height      = 0,   size  = 1,
              inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title    = "Marginal Effect of Altitude on Individual Niche Breadth (WIC)",
    subtitle = "Gamma GLMM, natural spline (df=3) | raw points jittered",
    x        = "Altitude (standardized)",
    y        = "WIC (individual niche breadth)"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  )
 
print(p_wic_alt)
ggsave(file.path(glmm_out_dir, "WIC_Altitude_Marginal_Effect.png"),
       p_wic_alt, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)
cat("Saved: WIC_Altitude_Marginal_Effect.png\n")
 

library(gridExtra)
# Combine WIC forest plot and altitude marginal effect side by side
p_wic_combined <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  p_wic_forest, p_wic_alt,
  ncol = 2,
  widths = c(0.55, 0.45)
)

ggsave(file.path(glmm_out_dir, "WIC_Combined_Figure.png"),
       p_wic_combined, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
cat("Saved: WIC_Combined_Figure.png\n")


################################################################################
# PART 3: PUBLICATION-READY TABLES (sjPlot::tab_model)
################################################################################

# ==============================================================================
# 11. PREDICTOR LABELS — shared across all tables for consistency
# ==============================================================================

pred_labels_species <- c(
  "spatial_nichescansorial" = "Spatial Niche: Scansorial",
  "spatial_nichearboreal"   = "Spatial Niche: Arboreal",
  "TribeHydromyini"         = "Tribe: Hydromyini",
  "mean_mass"               = "Mean Body Mass (scaled)",
  "Range_TypeFinisterres"   = "Range: only Finisterres",
  "Range_TypeMt_Wilhelm"    = "Range: only Mt. Wilhelm",
  "Alt_mean"                = "Mean Altitude (scaled)",
  "Alt_range"               = "Altitude Range (scaled)"
)

pred_labels_ind <- c(
  "spatial_nichescansorial"  = "Spatial Niche: Scansorial",
  "spatial_nichearboreal"    = "Spatial Niche: Arboreal",
  "TribeHydromyini"          = "Tribe: Hydromyini",
  "Mass_s"                   = "Body Mass (scaled)",
  "MetalokalitaFinisterres"  = "Metalokalita: Finisterres",
  "Alt_s"                    = "Altitude (linear, scaled)",
  "ns(Alt_s, df = 3)1"       = "Altitude spline (basis 1)",
  "ns(Alt_s, df = 3)2"       = "Altitude spline (basis 2)",
  "ns(Alt_s, df = 3)3"       = "Altitude spline (basis 3)"
)


# ==============================================================================
# 12-15. PUBLICATION-READY TABLES via sjPlot::tab_model()
#
# NOTE: tab_model() uses match.call() internally to name model objects, which
# breaks when models are passed via do.call() or a wrapper function — the data
# rows get counted as arguments instead of the two model objects. Each table
# must therefore be produced with a direct tab_model() call. The shared display
# options (r2, icc, aic, etc.) are repeated explicitly in each call.
# ==============================================================================

# Common display options — defined once for readability
tab_opts <- list(
  show.r2      = TRUE,
  show.icc     = TRUE,
  show.re.var  = TRUE,
  show.ngroups = TRUE,
  show.obs     = TRUE,
  show.aic     = TRUE,
  digits       = 3,
  p.style      = "stars"
)

# ==============================================================================
# 13. TABLE A: TNW — primary model vs full model
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n--- Saving Table A: TNW ---\n")
tab_model(
  model_tnw_primary,
  pred.labels  = pred_labels_species,
  dv.labels    = c("Primary model (TNW ~ Range_Type + spatial_niche + Tribe)",
                   "Full model"),
  title        = "Table A. Drivers of Total Niche Width (TNW)",
  show.r2      = tab_opts$show.r2,
  show.icc     = tab_opts$show.icc,
  show.re.var  = tab_opts$show.re.var,
  show.ngroups = tab_opts$show.ngroups,
  show.obs     = tab_opts$show.obs,
  show.aic     = tab_opts$show.aic,
  digits       = tab_opts$digits,
  p.style      = tab_opts$p.style,
  file         = file.path(glmm_out_dir, "Table_A_TNW.html")
)
cat("Saved: Table_A_TNW.html\n")


# ==============================================================================
# 14. TABLE B: Spec_index — non-linear (primary) + linear altitude
#     Non-linear model is the primary result (better AIC).
#     Linear model column adds the single Alt_s estimate for reference.
#     All shared predictor rows are identical between the two columns.
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n--- Saving Table B: Spec_index ---\n")
tab_model(
  model_spec_nonlinear,
  model_spec_linear,
  pred.labels  = pred_labels_ind,
  dv.labels    = c("Non-linear altitude (spline df=3)", "Linear altitude"),
  title        = "Table B. Drivers of Individual Specialization Index (WIC/TNW) — Beta GLMM",
  show.r2      = tab_opts$show.r2,
  show.icc     = tab_opts$show.icc,
  show.re.var  = tab_opts$show.re.var,
  show.ngroups = tab_opts$show.ngroups,
  show.obs     = tab_opts$show.obs,
  show.aic     = tab_opts$show.aic,
  digits       = tab_opts$digits,
  p.style      = tab_opts$p.style,
  file         = file.path(glmm_out_dir, "Table_B_SpecIndex.html")
)
cat("Saved: Table_B_SpecIndex.html\n")


# ==============================================================================
# 15. TABLE C: WIC — non-linear (primary) + linear altitude
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n--- Saving Table C: WIC ---\n")
tab_model(
  model_wic_nonlinear,
  model_wic_linear,
  pred.labels  = pred_labels_ind,
  dv.labels    = c("Non-linear altitude (spline df=3)", "Linear altitude"),
  title        = "Table C. Drivers of Individual Niche Breadth (WIC) — Gamma GLMM",
  show.r2      = tab_opts$show.r2,
  show.icc     = tab_opts$show.icc,
  show.re.var  = tab_opts$show.re.var,
  show.ngroups = tab_opts$show.ngroups,
  show.obs     = tab_opts$show.obs,
  show.aic     = tab_opts$show.aic,
  digits       = tab_opts$digits,
  p.style      = tab_opts$p.style,
  file         = file.path(glmm_out_dir, "Table_C_WIC.html")
)
cat("Saved: Table_C_WIC.html\n")


# ==============================================================================
# 15. COMBINE ALL TABLES INTO ONE WORD DOCUMENT
#
# Strategy: build each model table as a native Word table using flextable.
# This produces fully editable Word tables — no images, no webshot dependency.
#
# Helper build_model_flextable() extracts estimates, CIs and p-values from a
# model object (works for both lm and glmmTMB), formats them, and returns a
# flextable ready to insert into the document.
# ==============================================================================
 
library(flextable)
 
cat("\n=== Building combined Word document (native Word tables) ===\n")
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: extract fixed-effect rows from a model and return a flextable
# Works for lm (via broom::tidy) and glmmTMB (via broom.mixed::tidy)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
build_model_flextable <- function(model, pred_labels, dv_label,
                                  digits = 3, is_mixed = FALSE) {
 
  # Extract fixed effects with CIs
  if (is_mixed) {
    tbl <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed",
                             conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "wald")
  } else {
    tbl <- broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)
  }
 
  tbl <- tbl %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      # Apply human-readable predictor labels where available
      Predictor = ifelse(term %in% names(pred_labels),
                         pred_labels[term], term),
      Estimate  = round(estimate,  digits),
      CI_low    = round(conf.low,  digits),
      CI_high   = round(conf.high, digits),
      p_value   = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
        TRUE            ~ as.character(round(p.value, digits))
      ),
      Sig = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
        p.value < 0.1   ~ "\u2020",
        TRUE            ~ ""
      ),
      CI = paste0("[", CI_low, ", ", CI_high, "]")
    ) %>%
    select(Predictor, Estimate, CI, p_value, Sig)
 
  # Add model fit statistics as footer rows
  r2_val <- tryCatch({
    if (is_mixed) {
      r2s <- performance::r2(model)
      paste0("R\u00b2m = ", round(r2s$R2_marginal,  digits),
             ", R\u00b2c = ", round(r2s$R2_conditional, digits))
    } else {
      sm <- summary(model)
      paste0("R\u00b2 = ",     round(sm$r.squared,     digits),
             ", adj. R\u00b2 = ", round(sm$adj.r.squared, digits))
    }
  }, error = function(e) "R\u00b2 not available")
 
  aic_val <- tryCatch(
    paste0("AIC = ", round(AIC(model), 1)),
    error = function(e) "AIC not available"
  )
 
  n_val <- tryCatch(
    paste0("n = ", nobs(model)),
    error = function(e) ""
  )
 
  # Build the flextable
  ft <- flextable(tbl) %>%
    set_header_labels(
      Predictor = "Predictor",
      Estimate  = "Estimate",
      CI        = "95% CI",
      p_value   = "p",
      Sig       = ""
    ) %>%
    add_header_row(
      values = c(dv_label, "", "", "", ""),
      colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1)
    ) %>%
    merge_at(i = 1, j = 1:5, part = "header") %>%
    add_footer_lines(paste(r2_val, "|", aic_val, "|", n_val)) %>%
    add_footer_lines("\u2020 p < 0.1   * p < 0.05   ** p < 0.01   *** p < 0.001") %>%
    bold(part = "header") %>%
    bold(j = "Predictor") %>%
    fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") %>%
    font(fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all") %>%
    align(align = "left",   part = "all") %>%
    align(j = c("Estimate", "CI", "p_value", "Sig"),
          align = "center", part = "body") %>%
    border_outer(part = "all") %>%
    border_inner_h(part = "body") %>%
    autofit()
 
  return(ft)
}
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: combine two model columns side by side
# Builds a single wide flextable with two Estimate/CI/p columns
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
build_two_model_flextable <- function(model1, model2,
                                      pred_labels,
                                      dv_label1, dv_label2,
                                      digits = 3,
                                      is_mixed1 = FALSE,
                                      is_mixed2 = FALSE) {
 
  extract_tidy <- function(model, is_mixed) {
    if (is_mixed) {
      broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed",
                        conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "wald")
    } else {
      broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)
    }
  }
 
  fmt <- function(tbl) {
    tbl %>%
      filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
      mutate(
        Predictor = ifelse(term %in% names(pred_labels),
                           pred_labels[term], term),
        Est  = round(estimate,  digits),
        CIlo = round(conf.low,  digits),
        CIhi = round(conf.high, digits),
        p_fmt = case_when(
          p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
          TRUE            ~ as.character(round(p.value, digits))
        ),
        Sig = case_when(
          p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
          p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
          p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
          p.value < 0.1   ~ "\u2020",
          TRUE            ~ ""
        ),
        CI = paste0("[", CIlo, ", ", CIhi, "]")
      ) %>%
      select(Predictor, Est, CI, p_fmt, Sig)
  }
 
  t1 <- fmt(extract_tidy(model1, is_mixed1))
  t2 <- fmt(extract_tidy(model2, is_mixed2))
 
  # Full predictor union — keep all rows from both models
  all_preds <- union(t1$Predictor, t2$Predictor)
  t1 <- t1 %>% right_join(data.frame(Predictor = all_preds), by = "Predictor") %>%
    arrange(match(Predictor, all_preds))
  t2 <- t2 %>% right_join(data.frame(Predictor = all_preds), by = "Predictor") %>%
    arrange(match(Predictor, all_preds))
 
  combined <- data.frame(
    Predictor = all_preds,
    Est1  = replace_na(as.character(t1$Est),   "—"),
    CI1   = replace_na(t1$CI,                  "—"),
    p1    = replace_na(t1$p_fmt,               "—"),
    Sig1  = replace_na(t1$Sig,                 ""),
    Est2  = replace_na(as.character(t2$Est),   "—"),
    CI2   = replace_na(t2$CI,                  "—"),
    p2    = replace_na(t2$p_fmt,               "—"),
    Sig2  = replace_na(t2$Sig,                 ""),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
 
  # Fit statistics
  fmt_fit <- function(model, is_mixed) {
    r2_val <- tryCatch({
      if (is_mixed) {
        r2s <- performance::r2(model)
        paste0("R\u00b2m = ", round(r2s$R2_marginal,  digits),
               ", R\u00b2c = ", round(r2s$R2_conditional, digits))
      } else {
        sm <- summary(model)
        paste0("R\u00b2 = ",        round(sm$r.squared,     digits),
               ", adj. R\u00b2 = ", round(sm$adj.r.squared, digits))
      }
    }, error = function(e) "")
    aic_val <- tryCatch(paste0("AIC = ", round(AIC(model), 1)),
                        error = function(e) "")
    n_val   <- tryCatch(paste0("n = ", nobs(model)), error = function(e) "")
    paste(r2_val, "|", aic_val, "|", n_val)
  }
 
  ft <- flextable(combined) %>%
    set_header_labels(
      Predictor = "Predictor",
      Est1 = "Estimate", CI1 = "95% CI", p1 = "p", Sig1 = "",
      Est2 = "Estimate", CI2 = "95% CI", p2 = "p", Sig2 = ""
    ) %>%
    add_header_row(
      values    = c("", dv_label1, "", "", "", dv_label2, "", "", ""),
      colwidths = c(1,  1,          1,   1,   1,  1,          1,   1,   1)
    ) %>%
    merge_at(i = 1, j = 2:5, part = "header") %>%
    merge_at(i = 1, j = 6:9, part = "header") %>%
    add_footer_lines(paste("Model 1 —", fmt_fit(model1, is_mixed1))) %>%
    add_footer_lines(paste("Model 2 —", fmt_fit(model2, is_mixed2))) %>%
    add_footer_lines("\u2020 p < 0.1   * p < 0.05   ** p < 0.01   *** p < 0.001") %>%
    bold(part = "header") %>%
    bold(j = "Predictor") %>%
    fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") %>%
    font(fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all") %>%
    align(align = "left",   part = "all") %>%
    align(j = c("Est1","CI1","p1","Sig1","Est2","CI2","p2","Sig2"),
          align = "center", part = "body") %>%
    border_outer(part = "all") %>%
    border_inner_h(part = "body") %>%
    autofit()
 
  return(ft)
}
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build all three tables
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
cat("  Building Table A (TNW)...\n")
ft_tnw <- build_two_model_flextable(
  model1      = model_tnw_primary,
  model2      = model_tnw_full,
  pred_labels = pred_labels_species,
  dv_label1   = "Primary model (TNW ~ Range_Type + spatial_niche + Tribe)",
  dv_label2   = "Full model (all 6 predictors)",
  is_mixed1   = FALSE,
  is_mixed2   = FALSE
)
 
cat("  Building Table B (Spec_index)...\n")
ft_spec <- build_two_model_flextable(
  model1      = model_spec_nonlinear,
  model2      = model_spec_linear,
  pred_labels = pred_labels_spec,
  dv_label1   = "Non-linear altitude (spline df=3)",
  dv_label2   = "Linear altitude",
  is_mixed1   = FALSE,
  is_mixed2   = FALSE
)
 
cat("  Building Table C (WIC)...\n")
ft_wic <- build_two_model_flextable(
  model1      = model_wic_nonlinear,
  model2      = model_wic_linear,
  pred_labels = pred_labels_ind,
  dv_label1   = "Non-linear altitude (spline df=3)",
  dv_label2   = "Linear altitude",
  is_mixed1   = TRUE,
  is_mixed2   = TRUE
)
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assemble into one Word document
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
doc <- officer::read_docx()
 
doc <- doc %>%
  officer::body_add_par(
    "Table A. Drivers of Total Niche Width (TNW)",
    style = "heading 2"
  ) %>%
  flextable::body_add_flextable(ft_tnw) %>%
  officer::body_add_break() %>%
 
  officer::body_add_par(
    "Table B. Drivers of Specialization Index — logit(WIC/TNW), species-level lm (n = 22)",
    style = "heading 2"
  ) %>%
  flextable::body_add_flextable(ft_spec) %>%
  officer::body_add_break() %>%
 
  officer::body_add_par(
    "Table C. Drivers of Individual Niche Breadth (WIC) — Gamma GLMM",
    style = "heading 2"
  ) %>%
  flextable::body_add_flextable(ft_wic)
 
word_path <- file.path(glmm_out_dir, "All_Model_Tables.docx")
print(doc, target = word_path)
cat("Word document saved:", word_path, "\n")

#GLMM models 
#The original script is here: desktop asr_glmm script
#the notes regarding models in this email: search : "I suggest removing Alt_mean, Alt_range, and Range_Type"



```

```{r dietrelabuntab, warning=F, message=F}
rm(list = ls())
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree.R")
ps <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree


#########individual raw abundance
ott=as.data.frame(otu_table(ps))
ott=t(ott)
taxx=as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
ind_tax_abun <- merge(taxx, ott, by="row.names", all.x = TRUE)
write.csv(ind_tax_abun, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/tables/summary tables/ind_raw_abund.csv")



ps_species <- merge_samples(ps, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun=mean)
ps_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_species, function(x) x / sum(x))


#individual ASVs abundance with taxonomy

ott=t(ott)
taxx=as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_species_rel))
ind_tax_abun <- merge(taxx, ott, by="row.names", all.x = TRUE)

# First merge samples by species (summing counts)
ps_merged <- merge_samples(ps, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others")

# Then transform to relative abundance
ps_merged_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_merged, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Now aggregate by Diet

# Extract the abundance table for diet merged species

colnames(diet_table) <- tax_table(ps_diet)[,"Diet"]
rownames(diet_table) <- sample_names(ps_diet)

# Merge the abundance and taxonomy tables

library(dplyr
        )
merged_df <- ind_rel_table2 %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(taxonomic_cols))) %>%
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), sum))

write.csv(merged_df, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/tables/ind_rel_abun_merged.csv")

# Create final data frame
diet_df <- as.data.frame(diet_table)
diet_df$Species <- rownames(diet_df)
diet_df <- diet_df[, c("Species", "Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")]

# Check that values make sense (should sum to ~1 per species)
diet_df$Total <- rowSums(diet_df[, c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")])
head(diet_df)

# Remove the Total column if you don't need it
diet_df$Total <- NULL

# View the corrected result

#####third table with relative abundances
PS_lore_new_phylum

samdat=sample_data(PS_lore_new_phylum)
# Transform counts to relative abundances
PS_lore_new_phylum <- transform_sample_counts(PS_lore_new_phylum, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Convert to data frame
df_samples <- psmelt(PS_lore_new_phylum)

# Create a wide-format table with relative abundances per phylum
df_relative_abundances <- df_samples %>%
  group_by(sample_ID, Phylum) %>%
  summarize(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Phylum, 
              values_from = Abundance,
              values_fill = 0)  # Fills with 0 where a phylum is absent in a sample


Lore_individual_relative <- left_join(df_relative_abundances, samdat, by="sample_ID")

write.csv(Lore_individual_relative, file="D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/tables/summary tables/Lore_individual_relative.csv")


################################################################################
####################################  Pogonomys #################################
################################################################################
#Pogonomys spNorthEastFR and Pogonomys spNorthEastMW

rm(list = ls())
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree.R")

PS_pogo_new <- prune_samples(sample_data(PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree)$Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others == "Pogonomys", PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree)


PS_pogo_new <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PS_pogo_new) > 0, PS_pogo_new)
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 345 taxa and 13 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 13 samples by 147 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 345 taxa by 8 taxonomic ranks ]
# phy_tree()    Phylogenetic Tree: [ 345 tips and 344 internal nodes ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 345 reference sequences ]

# Extract ASV abundance table
asv_abundance <- t(as.data.frame(otu_table(PS_pogo_new)))

# Extract taxonomy table
taxonomy <- as.data.frame(tax_table(PS_pogo_new))

# Merge the abundance and taxonomy tables
asv_abundance2 <- merge(asv_abundance, taxonomy, by="row.names", all.x = TRUE)

# View the resulting table
head(asv_abundance2)

library(phyloseq)

# Function to calculate abundance-weighted % of each Phylum in a phyloseq object
phylum_percent <- function(physeq_obj) {
  # Aggregate OTUs at Phylum level
  physeq_phylum <- tax_glom(physeq_obj, taxrank = "Phylum", NArm = TRUE)
  
  # Get abundance sums per phylum
  phylum_sums <- taxa_sums(physeq_phylum)
  
  # Convert to %
  phylum_percentages <- phylum_sums / sum(phylum_sums) * 100
  
  # Attach Phylum names
  phylum_names <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_phylum))$Phylum
  names(phylum_percentages) <- phylum_names
  
  return(sort(phylum_percentages, decreasing = TRUE))
}

# Calculate for RAT
rat_phylum_percent <- phylum_percent(RAT)

# Calculate for HYDRO
hydro_phylum_percent <- phylum_percent(HYDRO)

# View results
rat_phylum_percent
hydro_phylum_percent
```
