*Competition (also ABDOMEN) *
```{r overlap, warning=F, message=F}

################################################################################
# Individual-level dietary co-occurrence analysis v8 (revised)
#
# Changes from v7:
#   - Barplot Panel A: bracket p-value annotations for PI-requested contrasts
#   - TWO barplot versions saved:
#       Panel A1: mean +/- 95% CI  (precision of mean estimate)
#       Panel A2: mean +/- SD      (spread of raw data)
#   - Revised colour scheme: locality is the primary colour axis
#       Same locality  = dark tones  (dark blue intra, dark red inter)
#       Diff. locality = light tones (light blue intra, light orange inter)
#     This makes the co-occurrence condition (dark bars) immediately visible
#     and the PI-requested bracket comparisons intuitive (dark-to-dark,
#     light-to-light)
#
# Unit of analysis: ALL individual pairs (intra + interspecific)
# Co-occurrence: same Locality AND same Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda
# Dietary distances: Bray-Curtis + Weighted UniFrac
################################################################################

library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(patchwork)

# ==============================================================================
# 1. PATHS
# ==============================================================================

output_dir <- "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/indicspecies/cooccurrence_individual_v8"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ==============================================================================
# 2. LOAD PHYLOSEQ AND PREPARE METADATA
# ==============================================================================

load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
ps <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))
class(sample_df) <- "data.frame"
sample_df$SampleID <- rownames(sample_df)

sample_df <- sample_df %>%
  mutate(Division = case_when(
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group %in%
      c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group ") ~ "Rattus invasive",
    Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group ==
      "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) "       ~ "Rattus endemic",
    TRUE ~ Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group
  ))

cat("Total samples before filtering:", nrow(sample_df), "\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 3. FILTER: EXCLUDE MW2400
# ==============================================================================

ps_filtered <- subset_samples(ps, Locality != "MW2400")
sample_df_filtered <- sample_df %>%
  filter(Locality != "MW2400",
         SampleID %in% sample_names(ps_filtered))

sample_df_filtered <- sample_df_filtered %>%
  mutate(Locality_Habitat = paste(Locality,
                                   Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda,
                                   sep = "_"))

cat("Samples after excluding MW2400:", nsamples(ps_filtered), "\n")
cat("Unique localities retained:", length(unique(sample_df_filtered$Locality)), "\n")
cat("Unique Locality+Habitat combinations:",
    length(unique(sample_df_filtered$Locality_Habitat)), "\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 4. PROPORTION NORMALISATION
# ==============================================================================

ps_prop <- transform_sample_counts(ps_filtered, function(x) x / sum(x))
cat("Proportion normalisation applied.\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 5. COMPUTE DISTANCE MATRICES
# ==============================================================================

cat("Computing Bray-Curtis distances...\n")
bc_dist <- phyloseq::distance(ps_prop, method = "bray")
bc_mat  <- as.matrix(bc_dist)
cat("Bray-Curtis matrix:", nrow(bc_mat), "x", ncol(bc_mat), "\n")

cat("Computing Weighted UniFrac distances...\n")
wu_dist <- phyloseq::distance(ps_filtered, method = "wunifrac")
wu_mat  <- as.matrix(wu_dist)
cat("Weighted UniFrac matrix:", nrow(wu_mat), "x", ncol(wu_mat), "\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 6. BUILD PAIRS — SHARED STRUCTURE
# ==============================================================================

cat("Building all pairs for 2x2 factorial design...\n")

sdf         <- sample_df_filtered %>% filter(SampleID %in% rownames(bc_mat))
sample_ids  <- sdf$SampleID
n_ind       <- length(sample_ids)

species_id  <- setNames(sdf$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, sdf$SampleID)
loc_hab_id  <- setNames(sdf$Locality_Habitat,                  sdf$SampleID)
locality_id <- setNames(sdf$Locality,                          sdf$SampleID)
division_id <- setNames(sdf$Division,                          sdf$SampleID)

idx      <- which(upper.tri(matrix(0, n_ind, n_ind)), arr.ind = TRUE)
ind1_vec <- sample_ids[idx[, 1]]
ind2_vec <- sample_ids[idx[, 2]]
sp1_vec  <- species_id[ind1_vec]
sp2_vec  <- species_id[ind2_vec]
lh1_vec  <- loc_hab_id[ind1_vec]
lh2_vec  <- loc_hab_id[ind2_vec]
loc1_vec <- locality_id[ind1_vec]
div1_vec <- division_id[ind1_vec]
div2_vec <- division_id[ind2_vec]

same_species_vec  <- sp1_vec == sp2_vec
same_locality_vec <- lh1_vec == lh2_vec

pairs_base <- data.frame(
  ind1          = ind1_vec,
  ind2          = ind2_vec,
  sp1           = sp1_vec,
  sp2           = sp2_vec,
  loc_hab1      = lh1_vec,
  loc_hab2      = lh2_vec,
  loc1          = loc1_vec,
  div1          = div1_vec,
  div2          = div2_vec,
  same_species  = same_species_vec,
  same_locality = same_locality_vec,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      same_species  & same_locality  ~ "Intraspecific\nSame locality",
      same_species  & !same_locality ~ "Intraspecific\nDiff. locality",
      !same_species & same_locality  ~ "Interspecific\nSame locality",
      !same_species & !same_locality ~ "Interspecific\nDiff. locality"
    ),
    group = factor(group, levels = c(
      "Intraspecific\nSame locality",
      "Intraspecific\nDiff. locality",
      "Interspecific\nSame locality",
      "Interspecific\nDiff. locality"
    )),
    same_species_f  = factor(same_species,
                             levels = c(TRUE, FALSE),
                             labels = c("Same species", "Diff. species")),
    same_locality_f = factor(same_locality,
                             levels = c(TRUE, FALSE),
                             labels = c("Same locality", "Diff. locality"))
  )

cat(sprintf("Total pairs:                    %d\n", nrow(pairs_base)))
cat(sprintf("  Intraspecific same locality:  %d\n",
            sum(pairs_base$same_species  & pairs_base$same_locality)))
cat(sprintf("  Intraspecific diff locality:  %d\n",
            sum(pairs_base$same_species  & !pairs_base$same_locality)))
cat(sprintf("  Interspecific same locality:  %d\n",
            sum(!pairs_base$same_species & pairs_base$same_locality)))
cat(sprintf("  Interspecific diff locality:  %d\n",
            sum(!pairs_base$same_species & !pairs_base$same_locality)))

# ==============================================================================
# 7. COLOUR SCHEME
# Locality = primary axis: same locality = dark, diff locality = light
# Species  = hue axis:     intraspecific  = blue, interspecific = red
# ==============================================================================

# Group order: 1=IntraSame, 2=IntraDiff, 3=InterSame, 4=InterDiff
group_colours <- c(
  "Intraspecific\nSame locality"  = "#1B4F8A",   # dark blue  (intra, same loc)
  "Intraspecific\nDiff. locality" = "#89B4DA",   # light blue (intra, diff loc)
  "Interspecific\nSame locality"  = "#922B21",   # dark red   (inter, same loc)
  "Interspecific\nDiff. locality" = "#F1948A"    # light red  (inter, diff loc)
)

# ==============================================================================
# 8. HELPERS
# ==============================================================================

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("p = NA")
  if (p < 0.001) return("p < 0.001")
  return(sprintf("p = %.3f", p))
}

add_bracket <- function(plot, x1, x2, y_bracket, label,
                         tip_height = 0.002, text_size = 3.2) {
  plot +
    annotate("segment",
             x = x1, xend = x1,
             y = y_bracket - tip_height, yend = y_bracket,
             colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.5) +
    annotate("segment",
             x = x2, xend = x2,
             y = y_bracket - tip_height, yend = y_bracket,
             colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.5) +
    annotate("segment",
             x = x1, xend = x2,
             y = y_bracket, yend = y_bracket,
             colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.5) +
    annotate("text",
             x     = (x1 + x2) / 2,
             y     = y_bracket,
             label = label,
             vjust = -0.4,
             size  = text_size,
             colour = "grey20")
}

# Build a barplot panel (used for both CI and SD versions)
make_barplot <- function(bar_data, dist_label, int_p_label,
                          p_same_loc, p_diff_loc,
                          error_label,    # "95% CI" or "SD"
                          group_colours) {

  y_min       <- floor(min(bar_data$err_lo) * 20) / 20
  y_err_max   <- max(bar_data$err_hi)
  bracket_gap <- (y_err_max - y_min) * 0.06
  bracket1_y  <- y_err_max + bracket_gap
  bracket2_y  <- bracket1_y + bracket_gap * 1.8
  y_max       <- bracket2_y + bracket_gap * 2.5
  tip_h       <- (y_max - y_min) * 0.012

  p <- ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = group, y = mean_dist, fill = group)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = err_lo, ymax = err_hi),
                  width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.7, colour = "grey20") +
    geom_text(aes(y = err_hi, label = n_label),
              vjust = -0.4, size = 2.6, colour = "grey40") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colours, guide = "none") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(y_min, y_max),
                       oob    = scales::squish) +
    labs(x = NULL,
         y = paste0("Mean ", dist_label, "\ndissimilarity (", error_label, ")")) +
    annotate("text",
             x = 3.8, y = y_max * 0.995,
             label    = int_p_label,
             hjust    = 1, vjust = 1,
             size     = 3, fontface = "italic", colour = "grey30") +
    theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 9,  colour = "grey20"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
      plot.margin  = margin(12, 8, 8, 8)
    )

  # Bracket 1: Intraspecific same (x=1) vs Interspecific same (x=3)
  p <- add_bracket(p, x1 = 1, x2 = 3, y_bracket = bracket1_y,
                   label = fmt_p(p_same_loc), tip_height = tip_h,
                   text_size = 3.2)

  # Bracket 2: Intraspecific diff (x=2) vs Interspecific diff (x=4)
  p <- add_bracket(p, x1 = 2, x2 = 4, y_bracket = bracket2_y,
                   label = fmt_p(p_diff_loc), tip_height = tip_h,
                   text_size = 3.2)
  p
}

# ==============================================================================
# 9. ANALYSIS FUNCTION
# ==============================================================================

run_metric_analysis <- function(dist_mat, dist_label, dist_label_short,
                                 pairs_base, output_dir,
                                 group_colours,
                                 max_pairs = 500000) {

  cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
  cat("METRIC:", dist_label, "\n")
  cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

  pairs <- pairs_base %>%
    mutate(distance = dist_mat[cbind(ind1, ind2)])

  # ---- LMM ----
  cat("Fitting LMM...\n")
  set.seed(42)
  if (nrow(pairs) > max_pairs) {
    cat(sprintf("  Subsampling to %d pairs\n", max_pairs))
    pairs_lmm <- pairs[sample(nrow(pairs), max_pairs), ]
  } else {
    pairs_lmm <- pairs
  }

  mod <- lmer(
    distance ~ same_locality_f * same_species_f +
      (1 | sp1) + (1 | sp2) + (1 | loc1),
    data    = pairs_lmm,
    REML    = TRUE,
    control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                          optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
  )

  cat("\n--- LMM Summary ---\n")
  print(summary(mod))

  anova_tab <- anova(mod)
  cat("\n--- ANOVA Table ---\n")
  print(anova_tab)

  # ---- Emmeans ----
  emm    <- emmeans(mod, ~ same_locality_f * same_species_f)
  emm_df <- as.data.frame(emm)
  names(emm_df)[names(emm_df) %in% c("asymp.LCL", "lower.CL")] <- "LCL"
  names(emm_df)[names(emm_df) %in% c("asymp.UCL", "upper.CL")] <- "UCL"
  emm_df <- emm_df %>%
    mutate(
      species_label  = factor(same_species_f,
                               levels = c("Same species", "Diff. species")),
      locality_label = factor(same_locality_f,
                               levels = c("Same locality", "Diff. locality"))
    )

  all_contrasts <- as.data.frame(pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey"))
  cat("\n--- All pairwise contrasts (Tukey) ---\n")
  print(all_contrasts)

  int_contrast <- contrast(emm, interaction = "pairwise")
  simple_loc   <- emmeans(mod, ~ same_locality_f | same_species_f)
  cat("\n--- Interaction Contrasts ---\n"); print(int_contrast)
  cat("\n--- Simple Effects of Locality ---\n"); print(pairs(simple_loc))

  # ---- Extract PI-requested pairwise p-values ----
  get_contrast_p <- function(contrasts_df, cell_a, cell_b) {
    match_rows <- (grepl(cell_a, contrasts_df$contrast, fixed = TRUE) &
                   grepl(cell_b, contrasts_df$contrast, fixed = TRUE))
    row <- contrasts_df[match_rows, ]
    if (nrow(row) == 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  WARNING: contrast '%s' vs '%s' not found\n", cell_a, cell_b))
      cat("  Available contrasts:\n")
      print(contrasts_df$contrast)
      return(NA_real_)
    }
    row$p.value[1]
  }

  p_same_loc <- get_contrast_p(all_contrasts,
                                "Same locality Same species",
                                "Same locality Diff. species")
  p_diff_loc <- get_contrast_p(all_contrasts,
                                "Diff. locality Same species",
                                "Diff. locality Diff. species")

  cat(sprintf("\nBracket p-values:\n"))
  cat(sprintf("  Intra vs Inter (same locality): %s\n", fmt_p(p_same_loc)))
  cat(sprintf("  Intra vs Inter (diff locality): %s\n", fmt_p(p_diff_loc)))

  # ---- Interaction p ----
  int_row     <- rownames(anova_tab)[grep(":", rownames(anova_tab))]
  int_p       <- anova_tab[int_row, "Pr(>F)"]
  int_p_label <- ifelse(int_p < 0.001,
                        "Interaction p < 0.001",
                        sprintf("Interaction p = %.3f", int_p))

  # ---- Save model outputs ----
  sink(file.path(output_dir, paste0("lmm_summary_", dist_label_short, ".txt")))
  cat("=== MODEL FORMULA ===\n");            print(formula(mod))
  cat("\n=== SUMMARY ===\n");                print(summary(mod))
  cat("\n=== ANOVA ===\n");                  print(anova_tab)
  cat("\n=== EMMEANS ===\n");                print(emm)
  cat("\n=== ALL PAIRWISE CONTRASTS ===\n"); print(all_contrasts)
  cat("\n=== INTERACTION CONTRASTS ===\n");  print(int_contrast)
  cat("\n=== SIMPLE EFFECTS ===\n");         print(pairs(simple_loc))
  sink()

  write.csv(emm_df,
            file.path(output_dir,
                      paste0("emmeans_2x2_", dist_label_short, ".csv")),
            row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(all_contrasts,
            file.path(output_dir,
                      paste0("pairwise_contrasts_", dist_label_short, ".csv")),
            row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(pairs %>%
              select(ind1, ind2, sp1, sp2, loc_hab1, loc_hab2,
                     div1, div2, same_species, same_locality,
                     group, distance),
            file.path(output_dir,
                      paste0("all_pairs_", dist_label_short, ".csv")),
            row.names = FALSE)

  # ---- Summary stats per group: mean, CI, SD ----
  bar_stats <- pairs %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_dist = mean(distance),
      sd_dist   = sd(distance),
      se        = sd_dist / sqrt(n()),
      n         = n(),
      ci_lo     = mean_dist - 1.96 * se,
      ci_hi     = mean_dist + 1.96 * se,
      sd_lo     = mean_dist - sd_dist,
      sd_hi     = mean_dist + sd_dist,
      .groups   = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(n_label = paste0("n=", formatC(n, format = "d", big.mark = ",")))

  # ---- Panel A1: mean +/- 95% CI ----
  bar_ci <- bar_stats %>%
    mutate(err_lo = ci_lo, err_hi = ci_hi)

  panel_A1 <- make_barplot(bar_ci, dist_label, int_p_label,
                            p_same_loc, p_diff_loc,
                            error_label   = "mean \u00b1 95% CI",
                            group_colours = group_colours)

  # ---- Panel A2: mean +/- SD ----
  bar_sd <- bar_stats %>%
    mutate(err_lo = pmax(sd_lo, 0),   # clip at 0 for distance metrics
           err_hi = pmin(sd_hi, 1))   # clip at 1 for BC/WUniFrac

  panel_A2 <- make_barplot(bar_sd, dist_label, int_p_label,
                            p_same_loc, p_diff_loc,
                            error_label   = "mean \u00b1 SD",
                            group_colours = group_colours)

  # ---- Panel B: Interaction plot (emmeans) ----
  line_colours <- c("Same species"  = "#1B4F8A",
                    "Diff. species" = "#922B21")
  point_shapes <- c("Same species"  = 16,
                    "Diff. species" = 17)

  panel_B <- ggplot(emm_df,
                    aes(x      = locality_label,
                        y      = emmean,
                        group  = species_label,
                        colour = species_label,
                        shape  = species_label)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = LCL, ymax = UCL),
                  width = 0.08, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 3.5) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = line_colours, name = NULL) +
    scale_shape_manual(values  = point_shapes,  name = NULL) +
    labs(x = NULL,
         y = paste0("Estimated marginal mean\n", dist_label, " dissimilarity")) +
    annotate("text",
             x = 1.5, y = max(emm_df$UCL),
             label    = int_p_label,
             hjust    = 0.5, vjust = -0.5,
             size     = 3.2, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey20") +
    theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      legend.position  = "top",
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey20"),
      axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 10),
      plot.margin      = margin(8, 8, 8, 8)
    )

  # ---- Save individual panels ----
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
                   paste0("panel_A1_CI_",  dist_label_short, ".png")),
         panel_A1, width = 7, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
                   paste0("panel_A2_SD_",  dist_label_short, ".png")),
         panel_A2, width = 7, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
                   paste0("panel_B_interaction_", dist_label_short, ".png")),
         panel_B, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300)

  # ---- Species-pair summary ----
  sp_pair_summary <- pairs %>%
    filter(!same_species, same_locality) %>%
    group_by(sp1, sp2, div1, div2) %>%
    summarise(
      n_pairs      = n(),
      mean_dist    = round(mean(distance), 4),
      mean_overlap = round(1 - mean(distance), 4),
      .groups      = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(mean_dist)

  write.csv(sp_pair_summary,
            file.path(output_dir,
                      paste0("species_pairs_", dist_label_short, ".csv")),
            row.names = FALSE)

  cat(sprintf("\nGroup means (%s):\n", dist_label))
  pairs %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    summarise(mean_dist = round(mean(distance), 4),
              sd_dist   = round(sd(distance), 4),
              n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    print()

  cat(sprintf("\nLMM interaction (%s): p = %.4f\n", int_row, int_p))

  return(list(
    mod         = mod,
    anova_tab   = anova_tab,
    emm_df      = emm_df,
    int_p       = int_p,
    int_p_label = int_p_label,
    p_same_loc  = p_same_loc,
    p_diff_loc  = p_diff_loc,
    panel_A1    = panel_A1,
    panel_A2    = panel_A2,
    panel_B     = panel_B,
    bar_stats   = bar_stats,
    pairs       = pairs
  ))
}

# ==============================================================================
# 10. RUN FOR BOTH METRICS
# ==============================================================================

res_bc <- run_metric_analysis(bc_mat,  "Bray-Curtis",      "BC",
                               pairs_base, output_dir, group_colours)
res_wu <- run_metric_analysis(wu_mat,  "Weighted UniFrac", "WUniFrac",
                               pairs_base, output_dir, group_colours)

# ==============================================================================
# 11. COMBINED FIGURES
# Two combined plots saved:
#   combined_CI: all panels using 95% CI error bars
#   combined_SD: all panels using SD error bars
# ==============================================================================

make_combined <- function(res_bc, res_wu, panel_type, ci_label, output_dir) {

  panel_bc <- if (panel_type == "CI") res_bc$panel_A1 else res_bc$panel_A2
  panel_wu <- if (panel_type == "CI") res_wu$panel_A1 else res_wu$panel_A2

  combined <- (panel_bc | res_bc$panel_B) /
              (panel_wu | res_wu$panel_B) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = "Individual-level dietary co-occurrence analysis",
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Top row: Bray-Curtis | Bottom row: Weighted UniFrac\n",
        "Left panels: mean +/- ", ci_label,
        " with brackets for key contrasts. ",
        "Right panels: LMM estimated marginal means +/- 95% CI.\n",
        "Colours: dark = same locality (co-occurring); ",
        "light = different locality. Blue = intraspecific; Red = interspecific."
      ),
      caption  = paste0(
        "Brackets show Tukey-adjusted p-values for: ",
        "(1) Intraspecific vs Interspecific within same locality, ",
        "(2) Intraspecific vs Interspecific within different localities. ",
        "Interaction p shown in corner of left panels and above right panels. ",
        "LMM includes random effects for sp1, sp2, and locality."
      ),
      theme = theme(
        plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9,  colour = "grey30"),
        plot.caption  = element_text(size = 8,  colour = "grey40",
                                     hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 6))
      )
    )

  fname <- file.path(output_dir,
                     paste0("cooccurrence_v8_combined_", panel_type, ".png"))
  ggsave(fname, combined, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  cat(sprintf("Saved: %s\n", fname))
}

make_combined(res_bc, res_wu, "CI", "95% CI", output_dir)
make_combined(res_bc, res_wu, "SD", "SD",     output_dir)

# ==============================================================================
# 12. FINAL CONSOLE SUMMARY
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("FINAL SUMMARY\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

for (res in list(list(r = res_bc, label = "Bray-Curtis"),
                 list(r = res_wu, label = "Weighted UniFrac"))) {
  int_row <- rownames(res$r$anova_tab)[grep(":", rownames(res$r$anova_tab))]
  cat(sprintf("%-20s | interaction p = %.4f\n", res$label, res$r$int_p))
  cat(sprintf("  Intra vs Inter (same loc): %s\n", fmt_p(res$r$p_same_loc)))
  cat(sprintf("  Intra vs Inter (diff loc): %s\n", fmt_p(res$r$p_diff_loc)))
}

cat("\nAll outputs saved to:", output_dir, "\n")
cat("Done.\n")


# ===============================================================================
# ============================= ABDOMEN ======================================== #
# ===============================================================================
library(phyloseq)
library(ape)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lattice)
library(latticeExtra)
library(mvMORPH)
library(RPANDA)
library(rstan)
rstan_options(auto_write = TRUE)
library(RColorBrewer)
options(scipen=100)
rm(list = ls())

# Source ABDOMEN functions (must be in working directory)
source("ABDOMEN.R")

# ===== LOAD DATA =====
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# Aggregate to Diet level
PS_diet <- tax_glom(PS, taxrank = "Diet")

# ===== LOAD HOST TREE (MERGED RATTINI + HYDROMYINI) =====
host_tree_raw <- ape::read.tree("D:/Rwd/papuang/ddRAD_trees/new_merged_trees_10.10.2024/Joined_scaled_RH85.nwk")

# Clean tip labels to sample IDs
host_tree_raw$tip.label <- sub("_.*$", "", host_tree_raw$tip.label)

# Get sample data
ps_sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(PS_diet))
ps_sample_data$SampleID <- rownames(ps_sample_data)

# Map tree tips to species
tree_tip_species <- sapply(host_tree_raw$tip.label, function(tip) {
  species <- ps_sample_data[ps_sample_data$SampleID == tip, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"]
  if(length(species) > 0) return(as.character(species[1]))
  return(NA)
})

# Keep only matched tips
tips_to_keep <- host_tree_raw$tip.label[!is.na(tree_tip_species)]
host_tree_matched <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_raw, tips_to_keep)
kept_species <- tree_tip_species[!is.na(tree_tip_species)]

# Keep one representative per species
unique_species <- unique(kept_species)
representative_tips <- sapply(unique_species, function(sp) {
  tips_to_keep[kept_species == sp][1]
})

# Create species-level tree
tree <- ape::keep.tip(host_tree_matched, representative_tips)
species_for_tips <- sapply(tree$tip.label, function(tip) {
  sp <- ps_sample_data[ps_sample_data$SampleID == tip, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others"]
  if(length(sp) > 0) return(as.character(sp[1]))
  return(NA)
})
tree$tip.label <- species_for_tips

# ===== ENSURE TREE IS ROOTED, BINARY, AND ULTRAMETRIC =====
if(!is.rooted(tree)) {
  tree <- root(tree, outgroup = tree$tip.label[1], resolve.root = TRUE)
}

if(!is.binary(tree)) {
  tree <- multi2di(tree)
}

if(!is.ultrametric(tree)) {
  tree <- chronos(tree)
}

# ===== CALCULATE DIET PROPORTIONS PER SPECIES =====
# Merge samples by species
PS_species <- merge_samples(PS_diet, "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", fun = mean)

# Get relative abundance (proportions)
PS_species_rel <- transform_sample_counts(PS_species, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Extract OTU table
otu_table_species <- as(otu_table(PS_species_rel), "matrix")

# Check orientation (species as rows for ABDOMEN)
if(ncol(otu_table_species) > nrow(otu_table_species)) {
  otu_table_species <- t(otu_table_species)
}

# Get taxonomy table
tax_table_species <- as(tax_table(PS_species_rel), "matrix")

# Create diet proportion matrix (species x diet types)
# Each row = species, each column = diet type (Animal, Plant, Fungi)
diet_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(otu_table_species), ncol = 3,
                     dimnames = list(rownames(otu_table_species), c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")))

for(sp in rownames(otu_table_species)) {
  for(asv in colnames(otu_table_species)) {
    if(asv %in% rownames(tax_table_species)) {
      diet_type <- tax_table_species[asv, "Diet"]
      if(!is.na(diet_type) && diet_type %in% c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")) {
        diet_matrix[sp, diet_type] <- diet_matrix[sp, diet_type] + otu_table_species[sp, asv]
      }
    }
  }
}

# Filter to species in tree
shared_species <- intersect(tree$tip.label, rownames(diet_matrix))
table <- diet_matrix[shared_species, ]
tree <- keep.tip(tree, shared_species)

# Ensure rownames match tree tip labels exactly
table <- table[tree$tip.label, ]

# ===== ABDOMEN PARAMETERS =====
name <- "rodent_diet_proportions"
code_path <- getwd()
detection_threshold <- 1e-05
seed <- 3
prior_Z0 <- "empirical"
mean_prior_logY <- 0
sd_prior_logY <- 2
nb_cores <- 10
chains <- 4
warmup <- 3000
iter <- 8000

# ===== RUN ABDOMEN =====
fit_summary <- ABDOMEN(tree, table, name, 
                       code_path = code_path,
                       prior_Z0 = prior_Z0,
                       detection_threshold = detection_threshold, 
                       seed = seed, 
                       mean_prior_logY = mean_prior_logY, 
                       sd_prior_logY = sd_prior_logY,
                       nb_cores = nb_cores, 
                       chains = chains, 
                       warmup = warmup, 
                       iter = iter)

# ===== EXTRACT RESULTS =====

# Pagel's lambda (phylosymbiosis measure)
original_lambda <- ABDOMEN_extract_lambda(tree, table, fit_summary, 
                                         detection_threshold = detection_threshold)

# Ancestral microbiota composition at root
Z0 <- ABDOMEN_extract_Z0(tree, table, fit_summary, 
                        detection_threshold = detection_threshold)

# Ancestral composition at all nodes
Z0_nodes <- ABDOMEN_extract_Z0_nodes(tree, table, fit_summary, 
                                    detection_threshold = detection_threshold)

# Variance-covariance matrix (microbial integration)
R_matrices <- ABDOMEN_extract_R(tree, table, fit_summary, 
                               detection_threshold = detection_threshold)

# ===== GENERATE PLOTS =====
list_colors <- c("Animal" = "#f8766d", "Plant" = "#00ba38", "Fungi" = "#619cff")

ABDOMEN_process_output(tree, table, name, fit_summary, 
                       code_path = code_path, 
                       detection_threshold = detection_threshold, 
                       list_colors = list_colors)

# ===== DISPLAY KEY RESULTS =====
original_lambda
Z0
head(Z0_nodes)

# ===== PERMUTATION TEST FOR SIGNIFICANCE (OPTIONAL) =====
# Run this section only if you want to test significance of phylosymbiosis
# WARNING: This takes a long time!

nb_permutations <- 100
list_lambda_permutations <- c()

for (seed_perm in 1:nb_permutations) {
  set.seed(seed_perm)
  name_random <- paste0("rodent_diet_permutation_", seed_perm)

  table_random <- table[sample(tree$tip.label), ]
  rownames(table_random) <- rownames(table)

  fit_summary_permut <- ABDOMEN(tree, table_random, name = name_random,
                                code_path = code_path,
                                prior_Z0 = prior_Z0,
                                detection_threshold = detection_threshold,
                                seed = seed_perm,
                                mean_prior_logY = mean_prior_logY,
                                sd_prior_logY = sd_prior_logY,
                                nb_cores = nb_cores,
                                chains = chains,
                                warmup = warmup,
                                iter = iter)

  list_lambda_permutations <- rbind(list_lambda_permutations,
                                   ABDOMEN_extract_lambda(tree, table_random,
                                                         fit_summary_permut,
                                                         detection_threshold = detection_threshold))
}

# p-value for significance of phylosymbiosis
p_value <- length(which(list_lambda_permutations[,1] >= original_lambda[1])) / nb_permutations

# ===== SAVE PERMUTATION RESULTS =====
# After the permutation loop completes, save everything:
# Save fit_summary immediately after ABDOMEN finishes
save(fit_summary, 
     file = "D:/Rwd/abdomen/fit_summary.RData")

cat("fit_summary saved successfully.\n")
cat("Object size:", format(object.size(fit_summary), units = "MB"), "\n")


# Save the lambda permutation matrix
write.csv(list_lambda_permutations, 
          "D:/Rwd/abdomen/abdomen_lambda_permutations.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Save the p-value
p_value_results <- data.frame(
  original_lambda = original_lambda[1],
  p_value = p_value,
  nb_permutations = nb_permutations,
  significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "YES", "NO")
)

write.csv(p_value_results,
          "D:/Rwd/abdomen/abdomen_phylosymbiosis_pvalue.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Save as RData for easy reloading
save(list_lambda_permutations, p_value, original_lambda, nb_permutations,Z0_nodes,R_matrices, Z0,
     file = "D:/Rwd/abdomen/abdomen_permutation_results.RData")

save(Z0, file="D:/Rwd/abdomen/Z0.Rdata")
save(Z0_nodes, file="D:/Rwd/abdomen/Z0_nodes.Rdata")



#Create divisions
library(dplyr)
# Create the new Division column as a direct copy
sample_data(PS)$Division <- sample_data(PS)$Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group

# Rename the specific values in the Division column
sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division %in% c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group "),
  "Rattus invasive",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division == "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) ",
  "Rattus endemic",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

sort(unique(sample_data(PS)$Division))
# [1] "Coccymys division"  "Hydromys division"  "Mallomys division"  "Pogonomys division" "Rattus endemic"
# [6] "Rattus invasive"    "Uromys division"


# -------------------------------------------------
#  POLISHED ABDOMEN DIET TREE VISUALIZATION
# -------------------------------------------------
library(ggtree)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scatterpie)

# ---------- 1. NEW COLOR PALETTE ----------
list_colors <- c(
  "Animal" = "#f8766d",   # red
  "Plant"  = "#00ba38",   # green
  "Fungi"  = "#619cff"    # blue
)
diet_cols <- names(list_colors)

# ---------- 2. PREPARE DATA ----------
# Ancestral states (internal nodes)
Z0_nodes_df <- as.data.frame(Z0_nodes)
if (!"node" %in% colnames(Z0_nodes_df))
  Z0_nodes_df$node <- as.numeric(rownames(Z0_nodes_df))

internal_data <- Z0_nodes_df %>%
  select(node, all_of(diet_cols)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(diet_cols), as.numeric))

# Current diet (tips)
tip_data <- as.data.frame(diet_matrix[tree$tip.label, ])
tip_data$node <- seq_along(tree$tip.label)
tip_data <- tip_data %>% select(node, all_of(diet_cols))

# Combine
all_node_data <- bind_rows(tip_data, internal_data)

# ---------- 3. GET SAMPLE COUNTS PER SPECIES ----------
# Count number of individuals per species
sample_counts <- sample_data(PS) %>%
  as("data.frame") %>%
  dplyr::count(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others, n_samples = n)

# ---------- 4. CREATE DIVISIONS DATA ----------
# Create the new Division column as a direct copy
sample_data(PS)$Division <- sample_data(PS)$Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group

# Rename the specific values in the Division column
sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division %in% c("incertae sedis group", "R. rattus group "),
  "Rattus invasive",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

sample_data(PS)$Division <- ifelse(
  sample_data(PS)$Division == "R. leucopus group (New Guinean group) ",
  "Rattus endemic",
  sample_data(PS)$Division
)

# Get species divisions
species_divisions <- sample_data(PS) %>%
  as("data.frame") %>%
  group_by(Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others) %>%
  summarize(Division = first(Division)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(Species = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others)

# Create division colors
division_colors <- c(
"Coccymys division" = "#E60000",   # Vibrant Red
  "Hydromys division" = "#004D99",   # Deep Blue
  "Mallomys division" = "#33CC33",   # Bright Green
  "Pogonomys division" = "#FFCC00",  # Golden Yellow
  "Rattus endemic" = "#660066",      # Deep Purple
  "Rattus invasive" = "#FF6600",     # Bright Orange
  "Uromys division" = "#00CCCC"      # Turquoise/Cyan
)

# ---------- 5. SCALE TREE TO BALANCE X/Y ----------
p_tmp   <- ggtree(tree, layout = "rectangular")
tmp_df  <- p_tmp$data
x_range <- max(tmp_df$x) - min(tmp_df$x)
y_range <- diff(range(tmp_df$y))

scale_factor <- if (x_range > 0) y_range / x_range else 1

tree_scaled <- tree
tree_scaled$edge.length <- tree_scaled$edge.length * scale_factor

# ---------- 6. GET COORDINATES ----------
p_base      <- ggtree(tree_scaled, layout = "rectangular", size = 0.8)
tree_coords <- p_base$data

piechart_data <- tree_coords %>%
  left_join(all_node_data, by = "node") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Animal))

internal_pies <- piechart_data %>% filter(!isTip)
tip_pies      <- piechart_data %>% filter(isTip)

# ---------- 7. SINGLE PIE RADIUS ----------
pie_radius <- y_range * 0.01   # same size for nodes & tips

# ---------- 8. TIP-LABEL OFFSET (after scaling) ----------
label_offset <- pie_radius * 1.8   # space between pie and label

# ---- CORRECT X-AXIS: SHOW ORIGINAL BRANCH LENGTHS ----
max_orig <- max(tree$edge.length)                    # original max depth
max_rounded <- ceiling(max_orig * 5) / 5             # round up to 0.2

# -------------------------------------------------
# ADD LAMBDA & P-VALUE TO TOP-LEFT OF PLOT
# -------------------------------------------------

# ---- Extract values from your ABDOMEN results ----
lambda_val <- original_lambda[1]  # Pagel's lambda (first element)
p_val      <- p_value             # From permutation test

# Format as text (e.g., "λ = 0.85, p = 0.01")
annotation_text <- paste0("λ = ", round(lambda_val, 2), ", p = ", ifelse(p_val < 0.001, "<0.001", round(p_val, 3)))

# ---------- 9. CREATE THE MODIFIED PLOT ----------

# First, prepare tree data with divisions
tree_data_with_divisions <- p_base$data %>%
  left_join(species_divisions, by = c("label" = "Species"))

# Create division background rectangles
division_backgrounds <- tree_data_with_divisions %>%
  filter(isTip) %>%
  group_by(Division) %>%
  summarize(
    ymin = min(y) - 0.5,
    ymax = max(y) + 0.5,
    xmin = -0.05 * max(tree_coords$x),
    xmax = max(tree_coords$x) * 1.7
  )

# Calculate center y-position for each division for text labels
division_labels <- division_backgrounds %>%
  mutate(
    y_center = (ymin + ymax) / 2,
    x_position = max(tree_coords$x) * 1.55  # Position text to the right of the tree
  )

# Build the plot step by step
p <- ggtree(tree_scaled, layout = "rectangular", size = 0.8) +
  
  # ADD COLORED BACKGROUND FOR DIVISIONS FIRST
  geom_rect(
    data = division_backgrounds,
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    fill = rep(division_colors[division_backgrounds$Division], length.out = nrow(division_backgrounds)),
    alpha = 0.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # ADD DIVISION LABELS (centered vertically for each division)
  geom_text(
    data = division_labels,
    aes(x = x_position, y = y_center, label = Division),
    size = 3.5,
    hjust = 0.3,
    fontface = "bold",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # DASHED ROOT BRANCH
  geom_rootedge(rootedge = tree_scaled$edge.length[1] * 0.3, linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +

  ## PIE CHARTS (same size)
  geom_scatterpie(
    data = internal_pies,
    aes(x = x, y = y, r = pie_radius),
    cols = diet_cols,
    color = "black", size = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_scatterpie(
    data = tip_pies,
    aes(x = x, y = y, r = pie_radius),
    cols = diet_cols,
    color = "black", size = 0.3
  ) +

  ## TIP LABELS WITH SAMPLE COUNTS
  geom_tiplab(
    aes(label = paste0(label, " (n=", 
                       sample_counts$n_samples[match(label, sample_counts$Species)], 
                       ")")),
    size = 3.0,
    offset = label_offset,
    hjust = 0,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  ## ANNOTATION: Lambda & P-value in top-left
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02 * max(tree_coords$x),  # 2% of x-range from left
    y = max(tree_coords$y) * 0.98,  # 98% up the y-axis (top)
    label = annotation_text,
    size = 4,
    hjust = 0,                      # left-aligned
    vjust = 1,                      # top-aligned
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +

  ## AESTHETICS - ONLY DIET LEGEND
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = list_colors,
    name   = "Diet Composition",
    guide  = guide_legend(
      nrow = 1,
      override.aes = list(size = 4)
    )
  ) +

  xlim(0, max(tree_coords$x) * 1.8) +  # Increased to accommodate division labels
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +

  ggtitle("Ancestral Reconstruction of Diet Composition") +

  theme_tree2() +

  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 15, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box      = "horizontal",
    legend.title    = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.margin     = margin(10, 100, 30, 10),  # Increased right margin for division labels
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),  # Ensure white background
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white")   # Ensure white panel
  ) +

  ## CORRECT X-AXIS (your fixed version)
  scale_x_continuous(
    name   = "substitutions/site",
    breaks = seq(0, max_rounded, by = 0.2) * scale_factor,
    labels = seq(0, max_rounded, by = 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.20))  # Increased right expansion for labels
  )

print(p)

# -------------------------------------------------
# SAVE (with all modifications)
# -------------------------------------------------
n_tips <- length(tree$tip.label)

ggsave(
  filename = "Rodent_Diet_Tree_Final_with_modifications.png",
  plot     = p,
  width    =9,  # Increased width to accommodate division labels
  height   = 18,
  dpi      = 300,
  bg       = "white",
  limitsize = FALSE
)

# Also save as PDF for publication
ggsave(
  filename = "Rodent_Diet_Tree_Final_with_modifications.pdf",
  plot     = p,
  width    = 18,
  height   = 9,
  device   = "pdf",
  limitsize = FALSE
)



## ======= save ancestral percentages and CIs === #
################################################################################
# Extract ancestral diet compositions with credible intervals from ABDOMEN
# v2: fixed Hydromyini ancestor to use most inclusive node (node_46)
#
# Root: CIs from fit_summary$summary (proper posterior CIs)
# Internal nodes: posterior means from Z0_nodes
#
# Run after loading fit_summary, Z0_nodes, Z0
################################################################################

library(dplyr)

abdomen_dir <- "D:/Rwd/abdomen"

diet_cols <- c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi")

# ==============================================================================
# 1. ROOT ANCESTOR — from fit_summary$summary (has proper CIs)
# ==============================================================================

z0_rows    <- grep("^Z0\\[", rownames(fit_summary$summary))
z0_summary <- fit_summary$summary[z0_rows, ]
rownames(z0_summary) <- diet_cols

root_df <- data.frame(
  ancestor   = "Root (all rodents)",
  diet       = diet_cols,
  mean_pct   = round(z0_summary[, "mean"]  * 100, 1),
  CI_low_95  = round(z0_summary[, "2.5%"]  * 100, 1),
  CI_high_95 = round(z0_summary[, "97.5%"] * 100, 1),
  CI_low_50  = round(z0_summary[, "25%"]   * 100, 1),
  CI_high_50 = round(z0_summary[, "75%"]   * 100, 1),
  has_CI     = TRUE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("=== ROOT ANCESTOR ===\n")
print(root_df[, c("diet", "mean_pct", "CI_low_95", "CI_high_95")])

# ==============================================================================
# 2. IDENTIFY INTERNAL NODES FROM MRCA COLUMN
# ==============================================================================

rattus_all_str      <- sort(c("Rattus rattus", "Rattus exulans",
                               "Rattus steini",  "Rattus niobe",
                               "Rattus verecundus"))
rattus_endemic_str  <- sort(c("Rattus steini", "Rattus niobe",
                               "Rattus verecundus"))
rattus_invasive_str <- sort(c("Rattus rattus", "Rattus exulans"))

# Find node containing all required species
# For Hydromyini: use MOST INCLUSIVE node (most species, no Rattus)
# For Rattini groups: use MOST DERIVED node (fewest species)
find_node <- function(Z0_nodes, required_species, label,
                      most_inclusive = FALSE) {

  matches <- sapply(Z0_nodes$MRCA, function(mrca_str) {
    mrca_species <- strsplit(mrca_str, "-")[[1]]
    all(required_species %in% mrca_species)
  })

  matched_rows <- which(matches)

  if (length(matched_rows) == 0) {
    cat("WARNING: No node found for", label, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  mrca_lengths <- sapply(Z0_nodes$MRCA[matched_rows], function(x) {
    length(strsplit(x, "-")[[1]])
  })

  # Most inclusive = most species (Hydromyini ancestor)
  # Most derived   = fewest species (Rattini ancestors)
  best_row <- if (most_inclusive) {
    matched_rows[which.max(mrca_lengths)]
  } else {
    matched_rows[which.min(mrca_lengths)]
  }

  cat("Found node for", label, ":", rownames(Z0_nodes)[best_row],
      "(node", Z0_nodes$node[best_row], ")\n")
  cat("  MRCA species count:", mrca_lengths[which(matched_rows == best_row)], "\n")
  return(best_row)
}

# Hydromyini ancestor — most inclusive node not containing any Rattus
# First exclude rows whose MRCA contains any Rattus species
Z0_nodes_no_rattus <- Z0_nodes[!grepl("Rattus", Z0_nodes$MRCA), ]

hydromyini_row <- find_node(
  Z0_nodes_no_rattus,
  required_species = c("Abeomelomys spMW", "Hydromys sp",
                        "Mallomys istapantap", "Pseudohydromys ellermani"),
  label            = "Hydromyini ancestor",
  most_inclusive   = TRUE
)
# Convert back to index in full Z0_nodes
hydromyini_row_full <- which(rownames(Z0_nodes) ==
                               rownames(Z0_nodes_no_rattus)[hydromyini_row])

# All Rattini ancestor
rattini_row <- find_node(
  Z0_nodes,
  required_species = rattus_all_str,
  label            = "All Rattini ancestor",
  most_inclusive   = FALSE
)

# Endemic Rattini ancestor
endemic_row <- find_node(
  Z0_nodes,
  required_species = rattus_endemic_str,
  label            = "Endemic Rattini ancestor",
  most_inclusive   = FALSE
)

# Invasive Rattus ancestor
invasive_row <- find_node(
  Z0_nodes,
  required_species = rattus_invasive_str,
  label            = "Invasive Rattus ancestor",
  most_inclusive   = FALSE
)

# ==============================================================================
# 3. EXTRACT POINT ESTIMATES FOR INTERNAL NODES
# ==============================================================================

extract_node_row <- function(Z0_nodes_df, row_idx, ancestor_label) {
  if (is.null(row_idx)) return(NULL)
  node_data <- Z0_nodes_df[row_idx, diet_cols]
  data.frame(
    ancestor   = ancestor_label,
    diet       = diet_cols,
    mean_pct   = round(as.numeric(node_data) * 100, 1),
    CI_low_95  = NA, CI_high_95 = NA,
    CI_low_50  = NA, CI_high_50 = NA,
    has_CI     = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

nodes_df <- bind_rows(
  extract_node_row(Z0_nodes, hydromyini_row_full, "Hydromyini ancestor"),
  extract_node_row(Z0_nodes, rattini_row,          "All Rattini ancestor"),
  extract_node_row(Z0_nodes, endemic_row,          "Endemic Rattini ancestor"),
  extract_node_row(Z0_nodes, invasive_row,         "Invasive Rattus ancestor")
)

# ==============================================================================
# 4. COMBINE AND PRINT
# ==============================================================================

all_results <- bind_rows(root_df, nodes_df)

cat("\n=== FULL RESULTS TABLE ===\n")
print(all_results)

# ==============================================================================
# 5. FORMATTED SUMMARY FOR MANUSCRIPT
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== FORMATTED FOR MANUSCRIPT ===\n")
for (anc in unique(all_results$ancestor)) {
  sub <- all_results %>% filter(ancestor == anc)
  cat("\n", anc, ":\n")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub))) {
    if (sub$has_CI[i]) {
      cat(sprintf("  %s: %.1f%% (95%% CI: %.1f%%–%.1f%%)\n",
                  sub$diet[i], sub$mean_pct[i],
                  sub$CI_low_95[i], sub$CI_high_95[i]))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("  %s: %.1f%%\n",
                  sub$diet[i], sub$mean_pct[i]))
    }
  }
}

# ==============================================================================
# 6. SAVE
# ==============================================================================

write.csv(all_results,
          file.path(abdomen_dir, "ancestral_diet_compositions_v2.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# Also save fit_summary if not already saved
if (!file.exists(file.path(abdomen_dir, "fit_summary.RData"))) {
  save(fit_summary, file = file.path(abdomen_dir, "fit_summary.RData"))
  cat("fit_summary saved.\n")
}

cat("\nSaved:", file.path(abdomen_dir, "ancestral_diet_compositions_v2.csv"), "\n")
cat("Done.\n")

```

*Maaslin2 *
```{r gllvm, warning=F, message=F}

#Exploratory models

# Load necessary libraries (Maaslin2 must be installed: install.packages("Maaslin2"))
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(Maaslin2)
library(gllvm) 


#______________________ CUSTOM FUNCTIONS ______________________________#
#The rotate() function extracts components from the model that can be used to create an ordination with ggplot. I show a very basic description here. You can find a more detailed description here: https://github.com/JenniNiku/gllvm/discussions/116


manage_unassigned<-function(PHYLOSEQ,UNASS_STRING=NA,ADD,AFTER=TRUE){
   TAXall<-data.frame(tax_table(PHYLOSEQ),stringsAsFactors = F)
   if(!is.na(UNASS_STRING)){TAXall[UNASS_STRING]<-NA}
   # TAXall<-as.character(TAXall[i,])
   for(i in 1:dim(TAXall)[1]){
       TAX<-as.character(TAXall[i,])
       if(AFTER==TRUE) {for(j in 2: length(TAX)){
                            if(is.na(TAX[j])){TAX[j]<-ifelse(regexpr(ADD,TAX[j-1])>0,
                                           TAX[j-1],paste(TAX[j-1],ADD,sep=""))}}}
      if(AFTER==FALSE) {for(j in 2: length(TAX)){
                            if(is.na(TAX[j])){TAX[j]<-ifelse(regexpr(ADD,TAX[j-1])>0,
                                     TAX[j-1],ADD,paste(TAX[j-1],sep=""))}}}
      TAXall[i,]<-TAX
    }
   TAXA<-colnames(TAXall)
   SPECIES<-rownames(TAXall)
   TAXall<-tax_table(TAXall)
   taxa_names(TAXall)<-SPECIES
   colnames(TAXall)<-TAXA
   tax_table(PHYLOSEQ)<-TAXall

   #output
   PHYLOSEQ
}

# --- NEW FUNCTION TO RENAME OTUs UNIQUELY BY TAXONOMY ---
# Renames ASVs/OTUs based on a specified taxonomic rank, adding a unique number to duplicates.

rename_taxa_uniquely <- function(physeq, taxrank) {
  # Requires dplyr to be loaded for group_by and mutate
  if (!requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'dplyr' is required for this function to work.")
  }

  taxa <- data.frame(tax_table(physeq), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # 1. Generate the base name from the specified rank
  taxa$BaseName <- taxa[[taxrank]]
  
  # 2. Handle NA/Empty names
  taxa$BaseName[is.na(taxa$BaseName) | taxa$BaseName == ""] <- 
    paste0("Unassigned_", 1:nrow(taxa[is.na(taxa$BaseName) | taxa$BaseName == "",]))
  
  # 3. Create a unique suffix for duplicates using simple grouping and indexing
  # This section is the robust fix:
  taxa <- taxa %>%
    dplyr::group_by(BaseName) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # Create a row number (count) within each group (BaseName)
      Count = dplyr::row_number(),
      # If there's more than one in the group (n() > 1), append the Count
      UniqueName = dplyr::if_else(
        dplyr::n() > 1, 
        paste(BaseName, Count, sep = "_"), 
        BaseName
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
  
  # 4. Overwrite taxa names in the phyloseq object
  taxa_names(physeq) <- taxa$UniqueName
  
  return(physeq)
}


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA LOADING, FILTERING, AND PREPARATION (Mt. Wilhelm - Order Level)
# ==============================================================================
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS=PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

#exclude invasive rattini
PS_filtered <- subset_samples(
  PS,
  Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group != "R. rattus group" &
  Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group != "incertae sedis group"
) %>% 
  filter_taxa(function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)


PS_wilhelm=subset_samples(PS_filtered, Metalokalita =="Mt_Wilhelm")%>% 
  filter_taxa(function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)

PS_finisterres=subset_samples(PS_filtered, Metalokalita =="Finisterres")%>% 
  filter_taxa(function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)

PS_wilhelm_order=tax_glom(PS_wilhelm, taxrank = "Order", NArm=FALSE)

PHYSEQ=PS_wilhelm_order

any(is.na(paste0(tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"]))) #FALSE but there are "NA"
#If neccesary: To replace missing “Order” with “Class” or “Phylum”, you can use the “manage_unassigned” function (https://github.com/jakubkreisinger/HM_soc_gen/blob/main/04.stat.Rmd)

PHYSEQ=manage_unassigned(PHYLOSEQ=PHYSEQ, UNASS_STRING = "NA", ADD = "", AFTER=TRUE)

which(tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"]=="") #60 98
tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"][60]="Agaricomycetes1"
tax_table(PHYSEQ)[, "Order"][98]="Gastropoda1"


# --- CRITICAL NEW STEP: RENAME OTUs UNIQUELY BY ORDER ---
PHYSEQ <- rename_taxa_uniquely(PHYSEQ, taxrank = "Order")

# 4. Filter high abundance taxa (>50 samples)
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$SSUMS <- sample_sums(PHYSEQ)
PHYSEQ.01 <- transform_sample_counts(PHYSEQ, function(x) ifelse(x == 0, 0, 1))
PHYSEQ.01.H <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.01) > 50, PHYSEQ.01)
PHYSEQ.H <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(PHYSEQ) %in% taxa_names(PHYSEQ.01.H), PHYSEQ)
PHYSEQ.H <- prune_samples(sample_sums(PHYSEQ.H) > 0, PHYSEQ.H)

# 5. Extract and prepare matrices
OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ.H)
SD <- sample_data(PHYSEQ.H)
SD.df <- as(SD, "data.frame")

# 6. Subset Metadata
KEEP <- c("sample_ID", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "Alt", "Metalokalita", "Locality", "spatial_niche", "SSUMS", "seqrun", "Y", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")
SD.sub <- SD.df[, KEEP]

# 7. Filter NAs (Matching the GLLVM covariate filtering)
NANs <- apply(SD.sub[, c("Alt", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche", "Tribe", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")], 1, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub[NANs == 0, ]
OTU_tab.sub <- OTU_tab[rownames(OTU_tab) %in% rownames(SD.sub2), ]

# 8. Transpose OTU table (Maaslin2 requirement: features in rows, samples in columns)
OTU_tab.sub <- t(as.matrix(OTU_tab.sub))

# 9. RENAME METADATA COLUMNS 
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub2 %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    species_re = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others,
    habitat = Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda,
    water_body = Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda,
    spatial_niche_col = spatial_niche
  )

# 10. Add squared Altitude term
SD.sub2$Alt_sq <- scale(SD.sub2$Alt)^2

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: RUN MAASLIN2 ANALYSIS (Mt. Wilhelm - Order Level)
# ==============================================================================

# Define fixed and random effects
fixed_effects <- c("habitat", "water_body", "Alt", "Alt_sq", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche_col")
random_effects <- c("species_re")

# NOTE: Output folder must exist before running.
maaslin2_output_MW_Order <- Maaslin2(
  input_data = OTU_tab.sub,
  input_metadata = SD.sub2,
  output = "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/MW_Order_Results", # Changed output folder name
  fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
  random_effects = random_effects,
  normalization = "TSS", 
  transform = "LOG", # Log transformation of TSS counts
  plot_heatmap = FALSE,
  plot_scatter = FALSE
)


# ======== virsualizations ============== #
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr) 

# 1. Extract significant results (qval <= 0.05)
sig_results <- maaslin2_output_MW_Order$results %>%
  filter(qval <= 0.05)

# Stop if no significant results are found
if(nrow(sig_results) == 0) {
  stop("No significant results (qval <= 0.05) found for visualization.")
}

# --- MISSING STEP: DATA PREPARATION FOR HEATMAP ---
# Create 'heatmap_long_data' by formatting the results table.
heatmap_long_data <- sig_results %>%
  # 1. Create a combined label for the X-axis (Variable_Level)
  # This handles continuous variables (like Alt) differently from categorical levels (like habitat: primary forest).
  dplyr::mutate(
    Variable_Level = case_when(
      # Case for continuous variables (Alt, Alt_sq, mean_mass)
      metadata %in% c("Alt", "Alt_sq", "mean_mass") ~ metadata,
      # Case for categorical variable levels (combine variable name and the specific level)
      TRUE ~ paste(metadata, value, sep = ": ")
    )
  ) %>%
  # 2. Select only the columns needed for the heatmap
  dplyr::select(feature, coef, Variable_Level)

# 2. Determine color limits
# Define a symmetrical limit, e.g., using the 98th percentile of absolute LFC to avoid outliers dictating the scale.
color_limit <- quantile(abs(heatmap_long_data$coef), 0.98) 

# --- Generate the Heatmap using ggplot2 with Dashed Grid and Enhanced Colors ---
ggplot(heatmap_long_data, aes(x = Variable_Level, y = feature, fill = coef)) +
  # Use geom_tile for the heatmap. Set the color (border) to be a light gray.
  geom_tile(color = "gray85", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  # Set the improved divergent color scale (Log-Fold Change)
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#0000FF",        # Deep Blue
    mid = "gray95",         # Very light gray/off-white for near-zero
    high = "#FF0000",       # Deep Red
    midpoint = 0,           # Center the color scale at zero
    limits = c(-color_limit, color_limit), # Use calculated limits to boost contrast
    oob = scales::squish,   # 'squish' extreme values to the limit colors
    name = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)" # Custom Legend Label
  ) +
  
  # Labels and Title
  labs(
    title = "Significant Effects of variables on Taxonomic Orders (qval ≤ 0.05)",
    x = "Predictor Variable",
    y = "Taxonomic Order"
  ) +
  
  # Theme and Formatting
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    
    # --- CRITICAL CHANGE: ADDING CUSTOM GRID LINES ---
    panel.grid.major = element_line(
      color = "gray60",     # Light gray color
      linetype = "dashed",  # Dashed lines
      linewidth = 0.2       # Very thin lines
    ),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), # Keep minor grid lines off
    # --- END CRITICAL CHANGE ---
    
    # Optional: Remove the X and Y axis ticks/lines usually present in theme_minimal
    axis.line = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank()
  )


# ================ Barplots for factor variables ================ #
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# --- REUSABLE BAR PLOT FUNCTION ---
plot_maaslin_bars <- function(maaslin_results, category_var) {
  
  # Filter and prepare data for the specified categorical variable
  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>%
    filter(metadata == category_var, qval <= 0.05) %>% # Use 'metadata' column
    
    # Order features by LFC for better visualization
    arrange(desc(coef)) %>%
    mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = unique(feature))) 
  
  # Check if any significant results exist for this variable
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    message(paste("No significant effects found for the variable:", category_var))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Determine the reference level (for title/caption purposes, not directly plotted)
  # NOTE: Maaslin2 omits the reference level, so we just plot the non-reference levels.
  
  plot_title <- paste("Significant Taxonomic Order Abundance Changes by", tools::toTitleCase(category_var))
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = feature, y = coef, fill = value)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    
    # Add error bars (using the stderr column from Maaslin2)
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = coef - stderr, ymax = coef + stderr), 
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
    
    # Add a line at LFC = 0
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    
    coord_flip() + # Flip coordinates for readability
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      y = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)",
      x = "Taxonomic Order (Feature)",
      fill = paste(tools::toTitleCase(category_var), "Level")
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
  
  # Print and save the plot
  print(p)
  ggsave(paste0("Maaslin2_BarPlot_", category_var, ".pdf"), plot = p, width = 8, height = 10)
  
  return(p)
}

# 1. Plot for Habitat
habitat_plot <- plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_MW_Order, "habitat")

# 2. Plot for Water Body
water_body_plot <- plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_MW_Order, "water_body")

# 3. Plot for Tribe
tribe_plot <- plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_MW_Order, "Tribe")

# ================ Volcano for contunius variables ================ #

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)

# --- REUSABLE VOLCANO PLOT FUNCTION ---
plot_maaslin_volcano <- function(maaslin_results, continuous_var) {
  
  # Filter and prepare data for the specified continuous variable
  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>%
    filter(metadata == continuous_var) %>% # Use 'metadata' column
    
    # Calculate -Log10(qval) for the y-axis
    mutate(log10_qval = -log10(qval),
           # Define the significance and direction for coloring
           significance = case_when(
             qval <= 0.05 & coef > 0 ~ "Significantly Increased (+LFC)",
             qval <= 0.05 & coef < 0 ~ "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)",
             TRUE ~ "Not Significant"
           ))
  
  # Check if any results exist for this variable
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    message(paste("No results found for the variable:", continuous_var))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Define the q-value threshold line
  qval_threshold <- -log10(0.05)
  
  plot_title <- paste("Taxonomic Order Abundance vs.", tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", continuous_var)))
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = coef, y = log10_qval, color = significance, label = feature)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 3) +
    
    # Add significance threshold line
    geom_hline(yintercept = qval_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      x = "Log-Fold Change (LFC) per Unit Increase in Predictor",
      y = "-Log10(q-value)",
      color = "Association"
    ) +
    
    # Use custom colors
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Significantly Increased (+LFC)" = "red", 
                                  "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)" = "blue", 
                                  "Not Significant" = "gray")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")) +
    
    # Add labels only for the significant features
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = plot_data %>% filter(qval <= 0.05), 
                             size = 3, max.overlaps = 20, force = 2)
  
  # Print and save the plot
  print(p)
  ggsave(paste0("Maaslin2_VolcanoPlot_", continuous_var, ".pdf"), plot = p, width = 10, height = 7)
  
  return(p)
}


# ================================================================= #
# =====================     WILHELM DIET    ======================= #
# ================================================================= #

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA LOADING, FILTERING, AND PREPARATION (Mt. Wilhelm - Order Level)
# ==============================================================================
PHYSEQ=PS_wilhelm_order
PHYSEQ=tax_glom(PHYSEQ, taxrank = "Diet", NArm=FALSE)
# --- CRITICAL NEW STEP: RENAME OTUs UNIQUELY BY ORDER ---
PHYSEQ <- rename_taxa_uniquely(PHYSEQ, taxrank = "Diet")

# 5. Extract and prepare matrices
OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ)
SD <- sample_data(PHYSEQ)
SD.df <- as(SD, "data.frame")
SD.df$SSUMS=sample_sums(PHYSEQ)
# 6. Subset Metadata
KEEP <- c("sample_ID", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "Alt", "Metalokalita", "Locality", "spatial_niche", "SSUMS", "seqrun", "Y", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")
SD.sub <- SD.df[, KEEP]

# 7. Filter NAs (Matching the GLLVM covariate filtering)
NANs <- apply(SD.sub[, c("Alt", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche", "Tribe", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")], 1, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub[NANs == 0, ]
OTU_tab.sub <- OTU_tab[rownames(OTU_tab) %in% rownames(SD.sub2), ]

# 8. Transpose OTU table (Maaslin2 requirement: features in rows, samples in columns)
OTU_tab.sub <- t(as.matrix(OTU_tab.sub))

# 9. RENAME METADATA COLUMNS 
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub2 %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    species_re = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others,
    habitat = Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda,
    water_body = Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda,
    spatial_niche_col = spatial_niche
  )

# 10. Add squared Altitude term
SD.sub2$Alt_sq <- scale(SD.sub2$Alt)^2

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: RUN MAASLIN2 ANALYSIS (Mt. Wilhelm - Diet Level)
# ==============================================================================
SAVE_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/6-Maaslin2/Mt_wilhelm_diet"
MAASLIN_OUT_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/MW_diet_results"

fixed_effects <- c("habitat", "water_body", "Alt", "Alt_sq", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche_col")
random_effects <- c("species_re")

maaslin2_output_MW_Diet <- Maaslin2(
  input_data = OTU_tab.sub,
  input_metadata = SD.sub2,
  output = MAASLIN_OUT_DIR, 
  fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
  random_effects = random_effects,
  normalization = "TSS",
  transform = "LOG",
  plot_heatmap = FALSE,
  plot_scatter = FALSE
)


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4: VISUALIZATION (Mt. Wilhelm - Diet Level)
# ==============================================================================

# --- Heatmap Generation ---
sig_results <- maaslin2_output_MW_Diet$results %>% filter(qval <= 0.05)
if(nrow(sig_results) == 0) {message("No significant results (qval <= 0.05) found for visualization.")} else {
    heatmap_long_data_NA <- sig_results %>%
      select(feature, metadata, value, coef) %>%
      mutate(Variable_Level = ifelse(value == "", as.character(metadata), paste0(metadata, "_", value))) %>%
      select(feature, Variable_Level, coef) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Variable_Level, values_from = coef)
    heatmap_long_data_final <- heatmap_long_data_NA %>%
      tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -feature, names_to = "Variable_Level", values_to = "coef")
    valid_coefs <- na.omit(heatmap_long_data_final$coef)
    color_limit <- if(length(valid_coefs) > 0) quantile(abs(valid_coefs), 0.98) else 1
    heatmap_p <- ggplot(heatmap_long_data_final, aes(x = Variable_Level, y = feature, fill = coef)) +
      geom_tile(color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.2, na.rm = FALSE) + 
      scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = "#0000FF", mid = "gray95", high = "#FF0000", midpoint = 0,
        limits = c(-color_limit, color_limit), oob = scales::squish, name = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)"
      ) +
      labs(
        title = "Significant Effects of variables on Diet Taxa (qval ≤ 0.05)",
        x = "Predictor Variable", y = "Diet Taxonomic Level"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray60", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.2),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()
      )
    ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, "Maaslin2_Heatmap_MW_Diet.pdf"), plot = heatmap_p, width = 12, height = 10)
}


# ================ Barplots for factor variables ================ #
plot_maaslin_bars <- function(maaslin_results, category_var) {
  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>% filter(metadata == category_var, qval <= 0.05) %>%
    arrange(desc(coef)) %>% mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = unique(feature)))
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {message(paste("No significant effects found for the variable:", category_var)); return(NULL)}
  plot_title <- paste("Significant Diet Taxa Abundance Changes by", tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", category_var)))
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = feature, y = coef, fill = value)) + geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = coef - stderr, ymax = coef + stderr), position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + coord_flip() + 
    labs(title = plot_title, y = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)", x = "Diet Taxonomic Level (Feature)",
      fill = paste(tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", category_var)), "Level")) + theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
  ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, paste0("Maaslin2_BarPlot_", category_var, ".pdf")), plot = p, width = 8, height = 10)
  print(p); return(p)
}

# --- Execute Bar Plots for Categorical Variables ---
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_MW_Diet, "habitat")
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_MW_Diet, "water_body")
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_MW_Diet, "Tribe")
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_MW_Diet, "spatial_niche_col")


# ================ Volcano for contunius variables ================ #
plot_maaslin_volcano <- function(maaslin_results, continuous_var) {
  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>% filter(metadata == continuous_var) %>%
    mutate(log10_qval = -log10(qval),
           significance = case_when(qval <= 0.05 & coef > 0 ~ "Significantly Increased (+LFC)",
             qval <= 0.05 & coef < 0 ~ "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)", TRUE ~ "Not Significant"))
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {message(paste("No results found for the variable:", continuous_var)); return(NULL)}
  qval_threshold <- -log10(0.05)
  plot_title <- paste("Diet Taxa Abundance vs.", tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", continuous_var)))
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = coef, y = log10_qval, color = significance, label = feature)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 3) + geom_hline(yintercept = qval_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(title = plot_title, x = "Log-Fold Change (LFC) per Unit Increase in Predictor", y = "-Log10(q-value)", color = "Association") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Significantly Increased (+LFC)" = "red", "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)" = "blue", "Not Significant" = "gray")) +
    theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = plot_data %>% filter(qval <= 0.05), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20, force = 2)
  ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, paste0("Maaslin2_VolcanoPlot_", continuous_var, ".pdf")), plot = p, width = 10, height = 7)
  print(p); return(p)
}

# --- Execute Volcano Plots for Continuous Variables ---
plot_maaslin_volcano(maaslin2_output_MW_Diet, "Alt")
plot_maaslin_volcano(maaslin2_output_MW_Diet, "Alt_sq")
plot_maaslin_volcano(maaslin2_output_MW_Diet, "mean_mass")
```
