```{r gllvm, warning=F, message=F}
# ===========       FINISTERRES ORDER    ========================= #
# ================================================================ #
SAVE_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/6-Maaslin2/fr_order"
MAASLIN_OUT_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/FR_order_results"


# --- LOCATION CHANGE: Finisterres ---
PS_finisterres=subset_samples(PS_filtered, Metalokalita =="Finisterres") %>%
  filter_taxa(function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)

# --- GLOMERATION: Diet Level ---
PS_finisterres_diet=tax_glom(PS_finisterres, taxrank = "Diet", NArm=FALSE)
PHYSEQ=PS_finisterres_diet

# --- RENAME OTUs UNIQUELY BY Diet ---
PHYSEQ <- rename_taxa_uniquely(PHYSEQ, taxrank = "Diet")

# No abundance filtering. Ensure samples still have reads.
PHYSEQ.H <- prune_samples(sample_sums(PHYSEQ) > 0, PHYSEQ) 
OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ.H)

# 5. Extract and prepare matrices (Metadata preparation)
SD <- sample_data(PHYSEQ.H)
SD.df <- as(SD, "data.frame")
SD.df$SSUMS=sample_sums(PHYSEQ) # Re-add SSUMS
OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ.H)

# 6. Subset Metadata (Using the previously fixed KEEP vector)
KEEP <- c("sample_ID", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "Alt", "Metalokalita", "Locality", "spatial_niche", "SSUMS", "seqrun", "Y", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")
SD.sub <- SD.df[, KEEP]

# 7. Filter NAs (Matching the GLLVM covariate filtering)
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub[complete.cases(SD.sub[, c("Alt", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche", "Tribe", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")]), ]

# Prune OTU table to match filtered samples
OTU_tab.sub <- OTU_tab[rownames(OTU_tab) %in% rownames(SD.sub2), ]
OTU_tab.sub <- t(as.matrix(OTU_tab.sub))

# 8. Prepare metadata for Maaslin2
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub2 %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    species_re = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others,
    habitat = Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda,
    water_body = Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda,
    spatial_niche_col = spatial_niche
  )
SD.sub2$Alt_sq <- scale(SD.sub2$Alt)^2

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: RUN MAASLIN2 ANALYSIS (Finisterres - Diet Level)
# ==============================================================================

fixed_effects <- c("habitat", "water_body", "Alt", "Alt_sq", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche_col")
random_effects <- c("species_re")

maaslin2_output_FR_Diet <- Maaslin2(
  input_data = OTU_tab.sub,
  input_metadata = SD.sub2,
  output = MAASLIN_OUT_DIR, 
  fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
  random_effects = random_effects,
  normalization = "TSS",
  transform = "LOG",
  plot_heatmap = FALSE,
  plot_scatter = FALSE
)


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4: VISUALIZATION (Finisterres - Order Level)
# ==============================================================================

# --- Heatmap Generation (Finisterres - Order Level) ---
sig_results <- maaslin2_output_FR_Order$results %>% filter(qval <= 0.05)
if(nrow(sig_results) == 0) {
    message("No significant results (qval <= 0.05) found for visualization.")
} else {
    # Prepare data for heatmap
    heatmap_long_data_NA <- sig_results %>%
      select(feature, metadata, value, coef) %>%
      mutate(Variable_Level = ifelse(value == "", as.character(metadata), paste0(metadata, "_", value))) %>%
      select(feature, Variable_Level, coef) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Variable_Level, values_from = coef)
    
    heatmap_long_data_final <- heatmap_long_data_NA %>%
      tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -feature, names_to = "Variable_Level", values_to = "coef")
      
    # Calculate color limits (using 98th percentile of absolute coefficients)
    valid_coefs <- na.omit(heatmap_long_data_final$coef)
    color_limit <- if(length(valid_coefs) > 0) quantile(abs(valid_coefs), 0.98) else 1
    
    # Generate the Heatmap
    heatmap_p <- ggplot(heatmap_long_data_final, aes(x = Variable_Level, y = feature, fill = coef)) +
      
      # 1. Use geom_tile with the light gray border (Aesthetics from your request)
      geom_tile(color = "gray85", linewidth = 0.5, na.rm = FALSE) + 
      
      # 2. Set the improved divergent color scale
      scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = "#0000FF",     # Deep Blue
        mid = "gray95",      # Very light gray/off-white for near-zero
        high = "#FF0000",    # Deep Red
        midpoint = 0,        # Center the color scale at zero
        limits = c(-color_limit, color_limit), # Use calculated limits
        oob = scales::squish,  # 'squish' extreme values
        name = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)", # Custom Legend Label
        na.value = "white" # CRITICAL: Ensure NA values (non-significant) are explicitly white
      ) +
      
      # Labels and Title
      labs(
        title = "Significant Effects of variables on Taxonomic Orders (qval ≤ 0.05) - Finisterres",
        x = "Predictor Variable",
        y = "Taxonomic Order"
      ) +
      
      # Theme and Formatting
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        
        # 3. Dashed grid lines (Aesthetics from your request)
        panel.grid.major = element_line(
          color = "gray60",    # Light gray color
          linetype = "dashed", # Dashed lines
          linewidth = 0.2      # Very thin lines
        ),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), # Keep minor grid lines off
        
        # 4. Remove default theme_minimal background and border (ensures white background)
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA), 
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
        
        # Optional: Remove the X and Y axis ticks/lines
        axis.line = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()
      )
    ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, "Maaslin2_Heatmap_FR_Order.pdf"), plot = heatmap_p, width = 12, height = 10)
}


# ================ Barplots for factor variables ================ #
plot_maaslin_bars <- function(maaslin_results, category_var) {
  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>% filter(metadata == category_var, qval <= 0.05) %>%
    arrange(desc(coef)) %>% mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = unique(feature)))
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {message(paste("No significant effects found for the variable:", category_var)); return(NULL)}
  plot_title <- paste("Significant Order Taxa Abundance Changes by", tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", category_var)))
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = feature, y = coef, fill = value)) + geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = coef - stderr, ymax = coef + stderr), position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + coord_flip() + 
    labs(title = plot_title, y = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)", x = "Taxonomic Order (Feature)",
      fill = paste(tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", category_var)), "Level")) + theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
  ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, paste0("Maaslin2_BarPlot_", category_var, ".pdf")), plot = p, width = 8, height = 10)
  print(p); return(p)
}

  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>% filter(metadata == continuous_var) %>%
    mutate(log10_qval = -log10(qval),
           significance = case_when(qval <= 0.05 & coef > 0 ~ "Significantly Increased (+LFC)",
             qval <= 0.05 & coef < 0 ~ "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)", TRUE ~ "Not Significant"))
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {message(paste("No results found for the variable:", continuous_var)); return(NULL)}
  qval_threshold <- -log10(0.05)
  plot_title <- paste("Order Taxa Abundance vs.", tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", continuous_var)))
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = coef, y = log10_qval, color = significance, label = feature)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 3) + geom_hline(yintercept = qval_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(title = plot_title, x = "Log-Fold Change (LFC) per Unit Increase in Predictor", y = "-Log10(q-value)", color = "Association") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Significantly Increased (+LFC)" = "red", "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)" = "blue", "Not Significant" = "gray")) +
    theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = plot_data %>% filter(qval <= 0.05), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20, force = 2)
  ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, paste0("Maaslin2_VolcanoPlot_", continuous_var, ".pdf")), plot = p, width = 10, height = 7)
  print(p); return(p)

# ================================================================ #
SAVE_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/6-Maaslin2/fr_diet"
MAASLIN_OUT_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/FR_diet_results"


# Load necessary libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(Maaslin2)
library(gllvm)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(tidyr)
library(tools)

# --- DIRECTORY DEFINITIONS (FINISTERRES - DIET) ---
SAVE_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/to_the_mail/complete_plots/6-Maaslin2/fr_diet"
MAASLIN_OUT_DIR <- "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/FR_diet_results"
if (!dir.exists(SAVE_DIR)) {dir.create(SAVE_DIR, recursive = TRUE)}
if (!dir.exists(MAASLIN_OUT_DIR)) {dir.create(MAASLIN_OUT_DIR, recursive = TRUE)}
message(paste("Maaslin2 output directory set to:", MAASLIN_OUT_DIR))


#______________________ CUSTOM FUNCTIONS ______________________________#
# Only the rename_taxa_uniquely function is necessary for Diet level
rename_taxa_uniquely <- function(physeq, taxrank) {
  if (!requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) {stop("Package 'dplyr' is required for this function to work.")}
  taxa <- data.frame(tax_table(physeq), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  taxa$BaseName <- taxa[[taxrank]]
  taxa$BaseName[is.na(taxa$BaseName) | taxa$BaseName == ""] <-
    paste0("Unassigned_", 1:nrow(taxa[is.na(taxa$BaseName) | taxa$BaseName == "",]))
  taxa <- taxa %>%
    dplyr::group_by(BaseName) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Count = dplyr::row_number(),
      UniqueName = dplyr::if_else(
        dplyr::n() > 1,
        paste(BaseName, Count, sep = "_"),
        BaseName
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
  taxa_names(physeq) <- taxa$UniqueName
  return(physeq)
}

# NOTE: The PS object and the exclusion of invasive rattini are assumed to be 
# loaded and executed just before this script block.

# Exclude invasive rattini
PS_filtered <- subset_samples(
  PS,
  Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group != "R. rattus group" &
  Hydromyini.Division.Rattini.Group != "incertae sedis group"
) %>%
  filter_taxa(function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA LOADING, FILTERING, AND PREPARATION (Finisterres - Diet Level)
# ==============================================================================

# --- LOCATION CHANGE: Finisterres ---
PS_finisterres=subset_samples(PS_filtered, Metalokalita =="Finisterres") %>%
  filter_taxa(function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)

# --- GLOMERATION: Diet Level ---
PS_finisterres_diet=tax_glom(PS_finisterres, taxrank = "Diet", NArm=FALSE)
PHYSEQ=PS_finisterres_diet

# --- RENAME OTUs UNIQUELY BY Diet ---
PHYSEQ <- rename_taxa_uniquely(PHYSEQ, taxrank = "Diet")

# No abundance filtering. Ensure samples still have reads.
PHYSEQ.H <- prune_samples(sample_sums(PHYSEQ) > 0, PHYSEQ) 
OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ.H)

# 5. Extract and prepare matrices (Metadata preparation)
SD <- sample_data(PHYSEQ.H)
SD.df <- as(SD, "data.frame")
SD.df$SSUMS=sample_sums(PHYSEQ) # Re-add SSUMS
OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ.H)

# 6. Subset Metadata (Using the previously fixed KEEP vector)
KEEP <- c("sample_ID", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "Alt", "Metalokalita", "Locality", "spatial_niche", "SSUMS", "seqrun", "Y", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")
SD.sub <- SD.df[, KEEP]

# 7. Filter NAs (Matching the GLLVM covariate filtering)
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub[complete.cases(SD.sub[, c("Alt", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche", "Tribe", "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda", "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")]), ]

# Prune OTU table to match filtered samples
OTU_tab.sub <- OTU_tab[rownames(OTU_tab) %in% rownames(SD.sub2), ]
OTU_tab.sub <- t(as.matrix(OTU_tab.sub))

# 8. Prepare metadata for Maaslin2
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub2 %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    species_re = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others,
    habitat = Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda,
    water_body = Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda,
    spatial_niche_col = spatial_niche
  )
SD.sub2$Alt_sq <- scale(SD.sub2$Alt)^2

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: RUN MAASLIN2 ANALYSIS (Finisterres - Diet Level)
# ==============================================================================

fixed_effects <- c("habitat", "water_body", "Alt", "Alt_sq", "Tribe", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche_col")
random_effects <- c("species_re")

maaslin2_output_FR_Diet <- Maaslin2(
  input_data = OTU_tab.sub,
  input_metadata = SD.sub2,
  output = MAASLIN_OUT_DIR, 
  fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
  random_effects = random_effects,
  normalization = "TSS",
  transform = "LOG",
  plot_heatmap = FALSE,
  plot_scatter = FALSE
)


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4: VISUALIZATION (Finisterres - Diet Level)
# ==============================================================================

# --- Heatmap Generation ---
sig_results <- maaslin2_output_FR_Diet$results %>% filter(qval <= 0.05)
if(nrow(sig_results) == 0) {message("No significant results (qval <= 0.05) found for visualization.")} else {
    # Prepare data for heatmap
    heatmap_long_data_NA <- sig_results %>%
      select(feature, metadata, value, coef) %>%
      mutate(Variable_Level = ifelse(value == "", as.character(metadata), paste0(metadata, "_", value))) %>%
      select(feature, Variable_Level, coef) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Variable_Level, values_from = coef)
    
    heatmap_long_data_final <- heatmap_long_data_NA %>%
      tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -feature, names_to = "Variable_Level", values_to = "coef")
      
    # Calculate color limits (using 98th percentile of absolute coefficients)
    valid_coefs <- na.omit(heatmap_long_data_final$coef)
    color_limit <- if(length(valid_coefs) > 0) quantile(abs(valid_coefs), 0.98) else 1
    
    # Generate the Heatmap with desired aesthetics
    heatmap_p <- ggplot(heatmap_long_data_final, aes(x = Variable_Level, y = feature, fill = coef)) +
      
      # Use geom_tile with the light gray border (Aesthetics from your request)
      geom_tile(color = "gray85", linewidth = 0.5, na.rm = FALSE) + 
      
      # Set the improved divergent color scale
      scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = "#0000FF",     # Deep Blue
        mid = "gray95",      # Very light gray/off-white for near-zero
        high = "#FF0000",    # Deep Red
        midpoint = 0,        # Center the color scale at zero
        limits = c(-color_limit, color_limit), # Use calculated limits
        oob = scales::squish,  # 'squish' extreme values
        name = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)", # Custom Legend Label
        na.value = "white" # CRITICAL: Ensure NA values (non-significant) are explicitly white
      ) +
      
      # Labels and Title
      labs(
        title = "Significant Effects of variables on Diet Taxa (qval ≤ 0.05) - Finisterres",
        x = "Predictor Variable",
        y = "Diet Taxonomic Level"
      ) +
      
      # Theme and Formatting
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        
        # Dashed grid lines (Aesthetics from your request)
        panel.grid.major = element_line(
          color = "gray60",    # Light gray color
          linetype = "dashed", # Dashed lines
          linewidth = 0.2      # Very thin lines
        ),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), # Keep minor grid lines off
        
        # Remove default theme_minimal background and border (ensures white background)
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA), 
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
        
        # Optional: Remove the X and Y axis ticks/lines
        axis.line = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()
      )
    ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, "Maaslin2_Heatmap_FR_Diet.pdf"), plot = heatmap_p, width = 12, height = 10)
}


# ================ Barplots for factor variables ================ #
plot_maaslin_bars <- function(maaslin_results, category_var) {
  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>% filter(metadata == category_var, qval <= 0.05) %>%
    arrange(desc(coef)) %>% mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = unique(feature)))
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {message(paste("No significant effects found for the variable:", category_var)); return(NULL)}
  plot_title <- paste("Significant Diet Taxa Abundance Changes by", tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", category_var)))
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = feature, y = coef, fill = value)) + geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = coef - stderr, ymax = coef + stderr), position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + coord_flip() + 
    labs(title = plot_title, y = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)", x = "Diet Taxonomic Level (Feature)",
      fill = paste(tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", category_var)), "Level")) + theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
  ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, paste0("Maaslin2_BarPlot_", category_var, ".pdf")), plot = p, width = 8, height = 10)
  print(p); return(p)
}

# --- Execute Bar Plots for Categorical Variables ---
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_FR_Diet, "habitat")
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_FR_Diet, "water_body")
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_FR_Diet, "Tribe")
plot_maaslin_bars(maaslin2_output_FR_Diet, "spatial_niche_col")


# ================ Volcano for contunius variables ================ #
plot_maaslin_volcano <- function(maaslin_results, continuous_var) {
  plot_data <- maaslin_results$results %>% filter(metadata == continuous_var) %>%
    mutate(log10_qval = -log10(qval),
           significance = case_when(qval <= 0.05 & coef > 0 ~ "Significantly Increased (+LFC)",
             qval <= 0.05 & coef < 0 ~ "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)", TRUE ~ "Not Significant"))
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {message(paste("No results found for the variable:", continuous_var)); return(NULL)}
  qval_threshold <- -log10(0.05)
  plot_title <- paste("Diet Taxa Abundance vs.", tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", continuous_var)))
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = coef, y = log10_qval, color = significance, label = feature)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 3) + geom_hline(yintercept = qval_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(title = plot_title, x = "Log-Fold Change (LFC) per Unit Increase in Predictor", y = "-Log10(q-value)", color = "Association") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Significantly Increased (+LFC)" = "red", "Significantly Decreased (-LFC)" = "blue", "Not Significant" = "gray")) +
    theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = plot_data %>% filter(qval <= 0.05), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20, force = 2)
  ggsave(file.path(SAVE_DIR, paste0("Maaslin2_VolcanoPlot_", continuous_var, ".pdf")), plot = p, width = 10, height = 7)
  print(p); return(p)
}

# --- Execute Volcano Plots for Continuous Variables ---
plot_maaslin_volcano(maaslin2_output_FR_Diet, "Alt")
plot_maaslin_volcano(maaslin2_output_FR_Diet, "Alt_sq")
plot_maaslin_volcano(maaslin2_output_FR_Diet, "mean_mass")



# ================================================================= #
# ====================== INVASIVE VS LOCAL ======================= #
# ================================================================= #
# Load necessary libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(Maaslin2)
library(gllvm) 
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2) 
library(scales) 

#______________________ CUSTOM FUNCTIONS ______________________________#

manage_unassigned<-function(PHYLOSEQ,UNASS_STRING=NA,ADD,AFTER=TRUE){
   TAXall<-data.frame(tax_table(PHYLOSEQ),stringsAsFactors = F)
   if(!is.na(UNASS_STRING)){TAXall[UNASS_STRING]<-NA}
   
   for(i in 1:dim(TAXall)[1]){
       TAX<-as.character(TAXall[i,])
       if(AFTER==TRUE) {for(j in 2: length(TAX)){
                            if(is.na(TAX[j])){TAX[j]<-ifelse(regexpr(ADD,TAX[j-1])>0,
                                           TAX[j-1],paste(TAX[j-1],ADD,sep=""))}}}
      if(AFTER==FALSE) {for(j in 2: length(TAX)){
                            if(is.na(TAX[j])){TAX[j]<-ifelse(regexpr(ADD,TAX[j-1])>0,
                                     TAX[j-1],ADD,paste(TAX[j-1],sep=""))}}}
      TAXall[i,]<-TAX
    }
   TAXA<-colnames(TAXall)
   SPECIES<-rownames(TAXall)
   TAXall<-tax_table(TAXall)
   taxa_names(TAXall)<-SPECIES
   colnames(TAXall)<-TAXA
   tax_table(PHYLOSEQ)<-TAXall

   #output
   PHYLOSEQ
}

rename_taxa_uniquely <- function(physeq, taxrank) {
  # Requires dplyr to be loaded for group_by and mutate
  if (!requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'dplyr' is required for this function to work.")
  }

  taxa <- data.frame(tax_table(physeq), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # CRITICAL: Store original rownames and Diet info before any operations
  original_names <- rownames(taxa)
  original_diet <- taxa$Diet
  
  # 1. Generate the base name from the specified rank
  taxa$BaseName <- taxa[[taxrank]]
  
  # 2. Handle NA/Empty names - Use Class level taxonomy instead
  unassigned_mask <- is.na(taxa$BaseName) | taxa$BaseName == ""
  
  if (sum(unassigned_mask) > 0) {
    # For unassigned taxa, use Class level taxonomy
    for (i in which(unassigned_mask)) {
      if (!is.na(taxa$Class[i]) && taxa$Class[i] != "") {
        taxa$BaseName[i] <- paste0("Class: ", taxa$Class[i])
      } else {
        # Fallback if Class is also missing - use Phylum level
        if (!is.na(taxa$Phylum[i]) && taxa$Phylum[i] != "") {
          taxa$BaseName[i] <- paste0("Phylum: ", taxa$Phylum[i])
        } else {
          taxa$BaseName[i] <- paste0("Unassigned_", i)
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  # 3. Create a unique suffix for duplicates using simple grouping and indexing
  taxa <- taxa %>%
    dplyr::group_by(BaseName) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Count = dplyr::row_number(),
      UniqueName = dplyr::if_else(
        dplyr::n() > 1, 
        paste(BaseName, Count, sep = "_"), 
        BaseName
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
  
  # 4. Store the mapping using the preserved original info
  name_mapping <- data.frame(
    old_name = original_names,
    new_name = taxa$UniqueName,
    Diet = original_diet,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # 5. Overwrite taxa names in the phyloseq object
  taxa_names(physeq) <- taxa$UniqueName
  
  # 6. Return both the phyloseq object and the mapping
  return(list(physeq = physeq, mapping = name_mapping))
}

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA LOADING AND PREPARATION (Global Dataset - Species-Defined Invasiveness)
# ==============================================================================
load("D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/phyloseqs/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS=PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

# --- STEP 1: CREATE INVASIVENESS METADATA COLUMN (Corrected Logic) ---
invasive_species <- c("Rattus rattus", "Rattus exulans")

# Create the invasiveness column based on the Species name
sample_data(PS)$invasiveness <- factor(
  ifelse(
    sample_data(PS)$Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others %in% invasive_species,
    "Invasive",
    "Local"
  ),
  levels = c("Local", "Invasive") # Setting Local as the reference group
)

# Define the starting phyloseq object, filtering out zero-sum taxa
PS_full <- filter_taxa(PS, function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)


# --- STEP 2: AGGLOMERATE TO ORDER LEVEL ---
PS_order=tax_glom(PS_full, taxrank = "Order", NArm=FALSE)


which(tax_table(PS_order)[, "Order"]=="") #60 98
tax_table(PS_order)[, "Order"][67]="Agaricomycetes1"
tax_table(PS_order)[, "Order"][114]="Gastropoda1"
tax_table(PS_order)[, "Order"][139]="Symphyla1"

PHYSEQ=PS_order

# --- STEP 4: RENAME OTUs UNIQUELY BY ORDER ---
rename_result <- rename_taxa_uniquely(PHYSEQ, taxrank = "Order")
PHYSEQ <- rename_result$physeq
taxa_diet_mapping <- rename_result$mapping

# --- STEP 5: MINIMAL FILTERING ---
# Only remove samples with zero total reads (required for TSS normalization).
PHYSEQ <- prune_samples(sample_sums(PHYSEQ) > 0, PHYSEQ)

# 6. Extract and prepare matrices
OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ)
SD <- sample_data(PHYSEQ)
SD.df <- as(SD, "data.frame")

# 7. Subset Metadata (Only key columns)
KEEP <- c("sample_ID", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "invasiveness") 
SD.sub <- SD.df[, KEEP]

# 8. Filter NAs (Ensure no NAs in the required columns)
NANs <- apply(SD.sub[, c("invasiveness", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others")], 1, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub[NANs == 0, ]
OTU_tab.sub <- OTU_tab[rownames(OTU_tab) %in% rownames(SD.sub2), ]

# 9. Transpose OTU table (Maaslin2 requirement: features in rows, samples in columns)
OTU_tab.sub <- t(as.matrix(OTU_tab.sub))

# 10. RENAME METADATA COLUMNS 
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub2 %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    species_re = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others
  )

# *** Verification of retained samples ***
cat("Total samples retained for Maaslin2 analysis:", nrow(SD.sub2), "\n")
cat("Invasive samples retained:", length(which(SD.sub2$invasiveness == "Invasive")), "\n")


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: RUN MAASLIN2 ANALYSIS (Invasiveness Fixed Effect Only)
# ==============================================================================

# Define fixed and random effects
fixed_effects <- c("invasiveness")
random_effects <- NULL # 

# NOTE: Updated output folder name
maaslin2_output_Global_Invasiveness_Order_Fixed <- Maaslin2(
  input_data = OTU_tab.sub,
  input_metadata = SD.sub2,
  output = "F:/Rwd/maaslin2/complete_dataset_invasiveness",
  fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
  random_effects = random_effects,
  normalization = "TSS", 
  transform = "LOG", 
  plot_heatmap = FALSE,
  plot_scatter = FALSE
)


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4: VISUALIZATION (Invasiveness vs. Order) - WITH DIET SOURCE GROUPING
# ==============================================================================

# 1. Extract significant results (qval <= 0.05)
sig_results <- maaslin2_output_Global_Invasiveness_Order_Fixed$results %>%
  filter(qval <= 0.05, metadata == "invasiveness")

# Stop if no significant results are found
if(nrow(sig_results) == 0) {
    message("No significant results (qval <= 0.05) found for visualization.")
} else {
    
    # --- DATA PREPARATION ---
    # Prepare data for scatter plot: extract coef and stderr for error bars
    plot_data <- sig_results %>%
        dplyr::select(feature, coef, stderr, qval)
    
    # --- ADD DIET SOURCE INFORMATION ---
    plot_data <- plot_data %>%
        left_join(taxa_diet_mapping[, c("new_name", "Diet")], 
                  by = c("feature" = "new_name"))
    
    # Workaround: Assign NAs to Plant as per previous logic
    plot_data$Diet[is.na(plot_data$Diet) | plot_data$Diet == ""] <- "Plant"
    
    # Order the diet factor for grouping
    plot_data$Diet <- factor(plot_data$Diet, levels = c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi"))
    
    # Sort data by Diet, then by coefficient value
    plot_data <- plot_data %>%
        arrange(Diet, coef)
    
    # Create ordered factor for feature to maintain grouping in plot
    plot_data$feature <- factor(plot_data$feature, levels = unique(plot_data$feature))

    # --- GENERATE THE SCATTER PLOT (FOREST PLOT STYLE) ---
    p_scatter <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = coef, y = feature, color = coef)) +
        # Add a vertical reference line at 0 (No Change)
        geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.8) +
        
        # Add error bars (standard error)
        # We use coef +/- stderr. For 95% CI, you could use 1.96 * stderr
        geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = coef - stderr, xmax = coef + stderr), 
                       height = 0.3, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
        
        # Add the points
        geom_point(size = 3) +
        
        # Set the divergent color scale for visual consistency with heatmap
        scale_color_gradient2(
            low = "#0000FF",    # Negative (Less in Invasive)
            mid = "gray60", 
            high = "#FF0000",   # Positive (More in Invasive)
            midpoint = 0,
            name = "Log-Fold Change"
        ) +
        
        # Labels and Title
        labs(
            title = "Taxonomic Differences: Invasive vs. Local Hosts",
            subtitle = "Points represent Maaslin2 coefficients ± standard error (qval ≤ 0.05)",
            x = "Effect Size (Invasive relative to Local)",
            y = "Order"
        ) +
        
        # Facet by Diet source
        facet_grid(Diet ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
        
        # Theme and Formatting
        theme_bw() +
        theme(
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, face = "italic"),
            axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 10)),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, color = "gray30"),
            
            # Grid and Facet styling
            panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
            strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", size = 11),
            strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
            legend.position = "right"
        )
    
    print(p_scatter)
}

# --- Save the Plot ---
ggsave(
    filename = "D:/Rwd/papuang/PNG_18S_FINAL2/plots/maaslin2/Invasiveness_Order_ScatterPlot.png", # Can be .png, .pdf, .tiff, or .svg
    plot = p_scatter,
    width = 10,       # Width in inches
    height = 8,        # Height in inches
    dpi = 300,         # High resolution for publication
    bg = "white"       # Ensures a white background
)


# ================================================================= #
# ====================== RATTINI VS HYDROMYINI ===================== #
# Revised: poly() for orthogonal altitude terms + marginal plots    #
# ================================================================= #

library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(Maaslin2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# ==============================================================================
# CUSTOM FUNCTIONS
# ==============================================================================

manage_unassigned <- function(PHYLOSEQ, UNASS_STRING = NA, ADD, AFTER = TRUE) {
  TAXall <- data.frame(tax_table(PHYLOSEQ), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (!is.na(UNASS_STRING)) { TAXall[UNASS_STRING] <- NA }
  for (i in 1:dim(TAXall)[1]) {
    TAX <- as.character(TAXall[i, ])
    if (AFTER == TRUE) {
      for (j in 2:length(TAX)) {
        if (is.na(TAX[j])) {
          TAX[j] <- ifelse(regexpr(ADD, TAX[j-1]) > 0,
                           TAX[j-1], paste(TAX[j-1], ADD, sep = ""))
        }
      }
    }
    if (AFTER == FALSE) {
      for (j in 2:length(TAX)) {
        if (is.na(TAX[j])) {
          TAX[j] <- ifelse(regexpr(ADD, TAX[j-1]) > 0,
                           TAX[j-1], ADD, paste(TAX[j-1], sep = ""))
        }
      }
    }
    TAXall[i, ] <- TAX
  }
  TAXA    <- colnames(TAXall)
  SPECIES <- rownames(TAXall)
  TAXall  <- tax_table(TAXall)
  taxa_names(TAXall) <- SPECIES
  colnames(TAXall)   <- TAXA
  tax_table(PHYLOSEQ) <- TAXall
  PHYLOSEQ
}

rename_taxa_uniquely <- function(physeq, taxrank) {
  if (!requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE))
    stop("Package 'dplyr' is required.")

  taxa          <- data.frame(tax_table(physeq), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  original_names <- rownames(taxa)

  if ("Diet" %in% colnames(taxa)) {
    original_diet <- taxa$Diet
  } else {
    warning("Diet column not found.")
    original_diet <- rep(NA, nrow(taxa))
  }

  taxa$BaseName <- taxa[[taxrank]]
  unassigned_mask <- is.na(taxa$BaseName) | taxa$BaseName == ""

  if (sum(unassigned_mask) > 0) {
    for (i in which(unassigned_mask)) {
      if (!is.na(taxa$Class[i]) && taxa$Class[i] != "") {
        taxa$BaseName[i] <- paste0("Class..", taxa$Class[i])
      } else if (!is.na(taxa$Phylum[i]) && taxa$Phylum[i] != "") {
        taxa$BaseName[i] <- paste0("Phylum..", taxa$Phylum[i])
      } else {
        taxa$BaseName[i] <- paste0("Unassigned_", i)
      }
    }
  }

  taxa <- taxa %>%
    dplyr::group_by(BaseName) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Count      = dplyr::row_number(),
      UniqueName = dplyr::if_else(
        dplyr::n() > 1,
        paste(BaseName, Count, sep = "_"),
        BaseName
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

  name_mapping <- data.frame(
    old_name = original_names,
    new_name = taxa$UniqueName,
    Diet     = original_diet,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  taxa_names(physeq) <- taxa$UniqueName
  return(list(physeq = physeq, mapping = name_mapping))
}


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 1: DATA LOADING AND PREPARATION
# ==============================================================================

load("D:/Rwd/PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean.R")
PS <- PNG_18S_rodent_filtered_ensembles_tree_NEWMT_W_clean

PS_full  <- filter_taxa(PS, function(x) sum(x) > 0, prune = TRUE)
PS_order <- tax_glom(PS_full, taxrank = "Order", NArm = FALSE)

# Manual fixes for unassigned orders
tax_table(PS_order)[, "Order"][67]  <- "Agaricomycetes1"
tax_table(PS_order)[, "Order"][114] <- "Gastropoda1"
tax_table(PS_order)[, "Order"][139] <- "Symphyla1"

PHYSEQ <- PS_order

rename_result    <- rename_taxa_uniquely(PHYSEQ, taxrank = "Order")
PHYSEQ           <- rename_result$physeq
taxa_diet_mapping <- rename_result$mapping

cat("\n=== Diet distribution in mapping ===\n")
print(table(taxa_diet_mapping$Diet, useNA = "ifany"))

# Filter high-abundance taxa (present in > 50 samples)
sample_data(PHYSEQ)$SSUMS <- sample_sums(PHYSEQ)
PHYSEQ.01   <- transform_sample_counts(PHYSEQ, function(x) ifelse(x == 0, 0, 1))
PHYSEQ.01.H <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(PHYSEQ.01) > 50, PHYSEQ.01)
PHYSEQ.H    <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(PHYSEQ) %in% taxa_names(PHYSEQ.01.H), PHYSEQ)
PHYSEQ.H    <- prune_samples(sample_sums(PHYSEQ.H) > 0, PHYSEQ.H)

cat("\nRetained taxa:", ntaxa(PHYSEQ.H), "\n")
cat("Retained samples:", nsamples(PHYSEQ.H), "\n")

OTU_tab <- otu_table(PHYSEQ.H)
SD      <- sample_data(PHYSEQ.H)
SD.df   <- as(SD, "data.frame")

KEEP <- c("sample_ID", "Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others",
          "Genus_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others", "Tribe", "mean_mass",
          "Alt", "Metalokalita", "Locality", "spatial_niche",
          "SSUMS", "seqrun", "Y",
          "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda",
          "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")
SD.sub <- SD.df[, KEEP]

NANs <- apply(SD.sub[, c("Alt", "mean_mass", "spatial_niche", "Tribe",
                          "Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda",
                          "Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda")],
              1, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
SD.sub2 <- SD.sub[NANs == 0, ]

OTU_tab.sub <- OTU_tab[rownames(OTU_tab) %in% rownames(SD.sub2), ]
OTU_tab.sub <- t(as.matrix(OTU_tab.sub))

SD.sub2 <- SD.sub2 %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    species_re  = Species_consensus_ddRAD_mt_others,
    habitat     = Habitat_polished_Daniel.Fanda,
    water_body  = Habitat_water_body_prox_Daniel.Fanda,
    spatial_niche_col = spatial_niche
  )

# ==============================================================================
# REVISED: Orthogonal polynomial altitude terms using poly()
# This replaces Alt_std + Alt_sq (which are collinear) with two orthogonal
# polynomial contrasts that can be independently tested for significance.
# ==============================================================================

Alt_poly <- poly(SD.sub2$Alt, 2)
SD.sub2$Alt_poly1 <- Alt_poly[, 1]   # linear component (orthogonal)
SD.sub2$Alt_poly2 <- Alt_poly[, 2]   # quadratic component (orthogonal)

# Store polynomial attributes for later use in marginal effect plots
alt_poly_coefs <- attr(Alt_poly, "coefs")

cat("\n=== Final data for Maaslin2 ===\n")
cat("Samples:", nrow(SD.sub2), "\n")
cat("Taxa:", nrow(OTU_tab.sub), "\n")
cat("VIF check — correlation between Alt_poly1 and Alt_poly2:",
    round(cor(SD.sub2$Alt_poly1, SD.sub2$Alt_poly2), 6),
    "(should be ~0)\n")


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: RUN MAASLIN2
# ==============================================================================

fixed_effects  <- c("Alt_poly1", "Alt_poly2", "Tribe", "mean_mass",
                    "spatial_niche_col", "Metalokalita")
random_effects <- c("species_re")

maaslin2_output_MW_Order <- Maaslin2(
  input_data     = OTU_tab.sub,
  input_metadata = SD.sub2,
  output         = "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/rat_vs_hyd",
  fixed_effects  = fixed_effects,
  random_effects = random_effects,
  normalization  = "TSS",
  transform      = "LOG",
  plot_heatmap   = FALSE,
  plot_scatter   = FALSE
)


# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: HEATMAP VISUALIZATION
# ==============================================================================

fixed_effects_order <- c("Alt_poly1", "Alt_poly2", "Tribe", "mean_mass",
                          "spatial_niche_col", "Metalokalita")

sig_results <- maaslin2_output_MW_Order$results %>%
  filter(qval <= 0.05, metadata %in% fixed_effects_order)

cat("\nSignificant associations (qval <= 0.05):", nrow(sig_results), "\n")

if (nrow(sig_results) == 0) {
  message("No significant results found.")
} else {

  heatmap_long_data <- sig_results %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # Human-readable labels for altitude polynomial terms
      Variable_Level = dplyr::case_when(
        metadata == "Alt_poly1" ~ "Altitude (linear)",
        metadata == "Alt_poly2" ~ "Altitude (quadratic)",
        metadata == value       ~ paste(metadata, "Coefficient", sep = ": "),
        TRUE                    ~ paste(metadata, value, sep = ": ")
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(feature, coef, Variable_Level, metadata)

  heatmap_long_data <- heatmap_long_data %>%
    left_join(taxa_diet_mapping[, c("new_name", "Diet")],
              by = c("feature" = "new_name"))

  # Handle missing Diet
  if (any(is.na(heatmap_long_data$Diet))) {
    if (exists("PHYSEQ.H")) {
      tax_df <- data.frame(tax_table(PHYSEQ.H), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      rownames(tax_df) <- taxa_names(PHYSEQ.H)
      for (feat in unique(heatmap_long_data$feature[is.na(heatmap_long_data$Diet)])) {
        if (feat %in% rownames(tax_df)) {
          d <- tax_df[feat, "Diet"]
          if (!is.na(d) && d != "")
            heatmap_long_data$Diet[heatmap_long_data$feature == feat] <- d
        }
      }
    }
    heatmap_long_data$Diet[is.na(heatmap_long_data$Diet)] <- "Unassigned"
  }

  diet_levels <- c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi", "Unassigned")
  heatmap_long_data$Diet <- factor(heatmap_long_data$Diet, levels = diet_levels)
  if (!any(heatmap_long_data$Diet == "Unassigned", na.rm = TRUE))
    heatmap_long_data$Diet <- droplevels(heatmap_long_data$Diet)

  # Column order: altitude terms first, then other predictors
  fixed_effects_order_labels <- c(
    "Altitude (linear)", "Altitude (quadratic)",
    unique(heatmap_long_data$Variable_Level[
      !heatmap_long_data$Variable_Level %in%
        c("Altitude (linear)", "Altitude (quadratic)")
    ])
  )

  column_order <- heatmap_long_data %>%
    dplyr::distinct(metadata, Variable_Level) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      metadata_ordered = factor(metadata, levels = fixed_effects_order)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(metadata_ordered, Variable_Level) %>%
    dplyr::pull(Variable_Level)

  heatmap_long_data$Variable_Level <- factor(
    heatmap_long_data$Variable_Level, levels = column_order
  )

  heatmap_long_data <- heatmap_long_data %>%
    arrange(Diet, desc(coef))
  heatmap_long_data$feature <- factor(
    heatmap_long_data$feature, levels = unique(heatmap_long_data$feature)
  )

  color_limit <- quantile(abs(heatmap_long_data$coef), 0.98, na.rm = TRUE)

  p_heatmap <- ggplot(heatmap_long_data,
                       aes(x = Variable_Level, y = feature, fill = coef)) +
    geom_tile(color = "gray85", linewidth = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low      = "#0000FF",
      mid      = "gray95",
      high     = "#FF0000",
      midpoint = 0,
      limits   = c(-color_limit, color_limit),
      oob      = scales::squish,
      name     = "Log-Fold Change (LFC)"
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = "Dietary Orders Associated with Host Traits and Environment (qval \u2264 0.05)",
      subtitle = paste0("Maaslin2: TSS/LOG | Random effect: species | ",
                        "Altitude as orthogonal polynomials (poly, df=2)"),
      x        = "Model Term",
      y        = ""
    ) +
    facet_grid(Diet ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1,
                                       size = 10, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 8),
      plot.title       = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 9, color = "gray30"),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray60",
                                       linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.2),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      strip.text.y     = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", size = 10),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "gray50")
    )

  print(p_heatmap)
  ggsave(
    "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/rat_vs_hyd/heatmap_MW_multivariable_revised.png",
    p_heatmap, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300
  )
  cat("Heatmap saved.\n")


  # ==============================================================================
  # SECTION 4: MARGINAL EFFECT PLOTS FOR ALTITUDE
  # Shows the actual shape of the altitude-abundance relationship for each
  # taxon with a significant linear or quadratic altitude association.
  # Addresses PI concern about interpretability of polynomial coefficients.
  # ==============================================================================

  alt_sig_taxa <- maaslin2_output_MW_Order$results %>%
    filter(qval <= 0.05,
           metadata %in% c("Alt_poly1", "Alt_poly2")) %>%
    pull(feature) %>%
    unique()

  cat("\nTaxa with significant altitude associations:", length(alt_sig_taxa), "\n")
  print(alt_sig_taxa)

  if (length(alt_sig_taxa) > 0) {

    # Extract coefficients for linear and quadratic terms
    alt_coefs <- maaslin2_output_MW_Order$results %>%
      filter(feature %in% alt_sig_taxa,
             metadata %in% c("Alt_poly1", "Alt_poly2")) %>%
      select(feature, metadata, coef) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(
        names_from  = metadata,
        values_from = coef,
        values_fill = 0
      )

    # Ensure both columns exist
    if (!"Alt_poly1" %in% names(alt_coefs))
      alt_coefs$Alt_poly1 <- 0
    if (!"Alt_poly2" %in% names(alt_coefs))
      alt_coefs$Alt_poly2 <- 0

    # Prediction grid using the same polynomial basis as the model
    alt_range      <- seq(min(SD.sub2$Alt, na.rm = TRUE),
                          max(SD.sub2$Alt, na.rm = TRUE),
                          length.out = 200)
    alt_poly_pred  <- poly(alt_range, 2, coefs = alt_poly_coefs)

    pred_df <- lapply(alt_sig_taxa, function(taxon) {
      coef_row  <- alt_coefs %>% filter(feature == taxon)
      b1        <- coef_row$Alt_poly1
      b2        <- coef_row$Alt_poly2
      predicted <- b1 * alt_poly_pred[, 1] + b2 * alt_poly_pred[, 2]
      data.frame(
        feature   = taxon,
        Alt       = alt_range,
        predicted = predicted
      )
    }) %>% bind_rows()

    # Add diet info for colouring
    pred_df <- pred_df %>%
      left_join(taxa_diet_mapping[, c("new_name", "Diet")],
                by = c("feature" = "new_name"))

    pred_df$Diet <- factor(pred_df$Diet,
                            levels = c("Animal", "Plant", "Fungi"))

    n_cols <- 4
    n_rows <- ceiling(length(alt_sig_taxa) / n_cols)

    p_alt_marginal <- ggplot(pred_df,
                              aes(x = Alt, y = predicted,
                                  color = Diet, group = feature)) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
                 color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.5) +
      facet_wrap(~feature, scales = "free_y", ncol = n_cols) +
      scale_color_manual(
        values = c("Animal" = "#f8766d",
                   "Plant"  = "#00ba38",
                   "Fungi"  = "#619cff"),
        na.value = "gray60"
      ) +
      labs(
        title    = "Marginal effect of altitude on dietary order abundance",
        subtitle = paste0(
          "Taxa with significant altitude association (q \u2264 0.05) | ",
          "Orthogonal polynomial basis (poly, df=2) | ",
          "y-axis = log-fold change relative to mean"
        ),
        x     = "Altitude (m a.s.l.)",
        y     = "Predicted log-fold change",
        color = "Diet"
      ) +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(
        plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray30"),
        strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold.italic", size = 8),
        legend.position  = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90",
                                         linetype = "dashed")
      )

    print(p_alt_marginal)
    ggsave(
      "D:/Rwd/Maaslin2_Results/rat_vs_hyd/altitude_marginal_effects.png",
      p_alt_marginal,
      width  = 14,
      height = n_rows * 3 + 2,
      dpi    = 300
    )
    cat("Marginal effect plots saved.\n")
  }
}


```
